If you'd like to remove the NA tag from the first booking, you can adjust that job's length to be shorter.

Click here to learn how to use the booking length and price adjustment features.

Travel/Non-Travel Break Compliance

If you use travel/non-travel breaks to build a period between bookings, the "NA" tag will be added to any bookings that extend into this buffer.

For example, let's say a provider has a 1-hour travel time break in between bookings.

If a 2-hour job is set at 8 AM, and another job is scheduled for 10 AM, the 8 AM job will have a "NA" tag added to it. This happens because the first job does not include enough travel time to get to the second job. If the second job is rescheduled to 11 AM instead, the tag will be removed from the 8 AM job.

Click here to learn how to set up travel and non-travel breaks as a part of your scheduling settings.



Same-day scheduling
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/same-day-scheduling

Same-day scheduling
Learn how to enable or disable same-day scheduling for customers.

Last updated on 09 Aug, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to set up same-day scheduling
Ģ How to disable same-day scheduling

Same-Day Scheduling
Admin and staff accounts have a great amount of flexibility when it comes to scheduling appointments. Bookings can be scheduled up to 6 months into the future, on the day of the service, or up to 30 days in the past.

If you would also like to allow customers to book same-day services, you can update the settings under your Customer Store Options section. To begin, please go to:

Settings > General > Store Options

Click on the "Customer" tab at the top of the page.

Next, click on the "Bookings" section to expand and display the various settings.

Scroll down until you see the question, "Would you like to allow customers to book appointments for the same day?"

Select "Yes" to enable same-day scheduling for customers.

Ģ If you've enabled same-day scheduling, provide an answer to the following question, "Would you like to set a limit as to how early a customer can book a same-day appointment?"
? Select "Yes" to set a limit of up to 24 hours before the desired booking time.
Ģ For example, if your limit is set to 3 hours before the start time, a customer booking at 8 AM will not see availability until 11 AM or after.
? Select "No" to allow customers to book a service without any buffer between scheduling and the job's start time.
Ģ For example, a customer can schedule a booking at 11:59 AM for 12:00 PM (just one minute later).

If you selected "No" to allow same-day appointments, set the time and days for "When is the latest the customer is allowed to book?"

Ģ Use the boxes to set the time of day and number of days before the job.
? For example, if you set the latest a customer can book to 5 PM one day before the job, customers would need to schedule next-day services no later than 5 PM. If they check availability at 5:01 PM, they will not see any spots until the following day.

Once you are finished, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page to update your customer settings.

Time Zone

Select which time zone your business operates in.

For clients in the United States who observe Daylight Saving Time, please use the following key to select a timezone that will automatically move the clock forward and backward one hour on the designated dates.
Ģ EST5EDT- Eastern Standard Time/Eastern Daylight Time

EST5EDT- Eastern Standard Time/Eastern Daylight Time

Log in as a customer or provider
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/logging-in-as-a-customer

Log in as a customer or provider
Learn how to log in as a customer or provider without logging out of your admin account.

Last updated on 28 Apr, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Log in as a customer without exiting your admin account
Ģ Log in as a provider without leaving your admin account

Why would I log in as the customer or provider?
One of the many options located in BookingKoala, you can visit any customer or provider account and log in as that user. You may want to do this for a few reasons:
445 See what the customer or provider dashboard is set up
446 Guide a customer or provider on how to navigate their dashboard
447 To see how your customer or provider permissions have impacted their account
448 Troubleshoot app or account issues

Log In as a Customer
To log in as a customer, you must first locate the customer's dashboard in your system.

To do this, you can either search the customer's name, email address, phone number, or address in the top search bar, or you can select them from: Customers > Customers

Click on the customer's name to be redirected to their dashboard.

Then, click the green "Log In As User" button in the top-right corner of the page.

After you click this button, you'll see a green system notification letting you know you've been successfully logged in as the customer. Once the page loads, you will see the account as if you are the customer.

Once you are finished, you can switch back to your admin dashboard by clicking on the "Log In As Admin" button at the top right corner of the page.

Please note: If you have any other tabs open that are logged in as the admin, those tabs will reload as the customer's dashboard you are logged into. Once you log out as a customer, these tabs will also log you back in as an admin.

Log In as a Provider
The process for logging in as a provider is the same as if you are logging in as a customer.

Search the provider's name, email address, phone number, or address in the top search bar, or you can select them from: Providers > Providers

Click on the provider's name to be redirected to their dashboard.

Then, click the green "Log In As User" button in the top-right corner of the page.

After you click this button, you'll see a green system notification letting you know you've been successfully logged in as the provider. Once the page loads, you will see the account as if you are the provider.

Once you are finished, you can switch back to your admin dashboard by clicking on the "Log In As Admin" button at the top right corner of the page.

Please note: If you have any other tabs open that are logged in as the admin, those tabs will reload as the customer's dashboard you are logged into. Once you log out as a customer, these tabs will also log you back in as an admin.



Enable the customer and provider "My Drive" feature
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/enabling-the-customer-and-provider-my-drive-option

Enable the customer and provider "My Drive" feature
What is the "My Drive" feature?
The "My Drive" feature allows the admin to share files with customers and/or providers through their BookingKoala account.

Navigation Paths to "My Drive"
Ģ For Providers: Go to Providers > Providers > [Provider Name] > My Drive
Ģ For Customers: Go to Customers > Customers > [Customer Name] > My Drive

How to upload a file into "My Drive"
449 Click on the "My Drive" tab to open the section.
450 Click the blue "Browse" button to select the files you'd like to upload.
451 (Optional) Add a Note into the box below for reference. This will show up as a "tooltip" next to the file name.
452 Click the blue "Save" button to upload the file.

Features and How They Work
Ģ Viewing Notes: Once a file is uploaded, it can be found in the section below. Hover over the note icon next to the file name to view your note.
Ģ Hiding Files: If you'd like to hide the file from the customer or provider, click the orange "Make Private" button.
Ģ Showing Files: To make the file "public" again, click the green "Private" button.
Ģ File Management: You can also download the file or delete it altogether.
Ģ Customer/Provider Access: On the customer/provider end, users can select the "My Drive" option from the main menu to download the files you've uploaded.
Ģ Customer/Provider Uploads: If enabled, customers and providers can also upload files to share with you and your staff.

Enabling "My Drive" for Customers
Navigation Path
Go to Settings > General > Store Options > Customer

Step-by-step Instructions
453 Scroll down to the "General" section and click the "+" icon to expand it.
454 Scroll down until you find the question, "Would you like to enable the "My Drive" option for your customers?" and select "Yes". This will allow you and your staff to upload files directly into their account.
455 You can also decide if you would like to allow customers to upload files into the My Drive section under the question, "Would you like to allow your customers to upload files inside their accounts?"
? Select "Yes" to allow customers to upload files as well.
? Select "No" to only allow staff and admin to upload files into their My Drive.
456 Click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.
457 You will now see a "My Drive" tab when you visit your customer's dashboard.

Enabling "My Drive" for Providers
Navigation Path
Go to Settings > General > Store Options > Provider

Step-by-step Instructions
458 Scroll down to the "Booking" section and click the "+" icon to expand it.
459 Scroll down until you find the question, "Would you like each provider to have a "My Drive" option to share their documents?" and select "Yes". This will allow you and your staff to upload files directly into their account.
460 You can also decide if you would like to allow providers to upload files into the My Drive section under the question, "Would you like to allow your providers to upload files?"
? Select "Yes" to allow providers to upload files as well.
? Select "No" to only allow staff and admin to upload files into their My Drive.
461 Click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.
462 You will now see a "My Drive" tab when you visit your provider's dashboard.



How to enable multiple appointments per booking spot
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-multispot-settings-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

How to enable multiple appointments per booking spot
Learn how to enable multispot scheduling so providers can be scheduled for multiple appointments at the same date and start time.

Last updated on 05 Jul, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Enable the multi-spot setting to allow providers to receive more than one booking at the same time

Multispot Settings
The Multi-spot Settings section under the General Store Options tab allows you to enable the "multiple appointments" feature. Multiple appointments, or "multi-spot" booking, is a feature that allows customers to book a certain provider more than one time for the same date and time spot.

For example, in the dog walking industry, a dog walker may take out multiple clients' dogs at the same time if they are in the same neighborhood. By enabling multi-spot appointments, the business can set how many appointments the provider received for any given date and time.

To start, go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the "Multi-spot Settings" section.

Would you like to activate the multiple appointments per booking spot feature?
To enable multiple appointments per booking spot, select "Yes" to this question.

Next, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.

Click here to view the multiple booking spots overview and set up guide for providers and booking forms.



How to enable the multiple time zone feature
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/multiple-time-zones

How to enable the multiple time zone feature
Learn how multiple timezones work and how to enable this feature.

Last updated on 08 Aug, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Enable the multiple time zones feature
Ģ Understand how the multiple time zones feature works

What is the multiple time zones feature?
If your business operates in multiple time zones, you may want to enable the multiple time zones feature.

This will allow you to see the real-time at your customer's location as well as the time in your own timezone (the admin timezone can be selected and changed at any time under the General Settings in the Store Info section).

Enabling Multiple Time Zones
To enable multiple time zones, please go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General > Time Zone Settings

Scroll down the Time Zone Settings section and click the plus sign icon to expand.

Select "Yes" for "Would you like to activate the multiple time zones feature?" and then click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.

Once enabled, any bookings that are outside of the store's time zone will display two start times.
Ģ The start time in the black text is the customer's local time.
Ģ The start time in the orange text shows the admin's local time.

Remember to Save!
If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue "Save" button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.

Updating your Locations
Once you have enabled the multiple time zones feature, you will need to update your locations and add time zones to them for the feature to function as anticipated. To do so, please go to: Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > 1. Locations

Once you are on the Locations page, select "Options" then "Edit" next to a location to open its settings.

Then, select a timezone from the timezone dropdown menu.

Please note: If you live in the United States and observe Daylight Saving Time (DST) please select one of the following options so the clock automatically changes twice per year in your BookingKoala calendar.
Ģ EST5EDT - US Eastern Time
Ģ CST6CDT - US Central Time
Ģ MST7MDT - US Mountain Time
Ģ PST8PDT - US Pacific Time

Once you have selected your timezone for the location, click the blue "Next" buttons until you reach "Update". Click the blue "Update" button to update your location.



How to set up payments and automatic charges
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-payment-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

How to set up payments and automatic charges
Learn how to set up your customer payments, including enabling automatic charges, pre-charges, and refunded payment rules.

Last updated on 03 Oct, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Set rules for charging unassigned bookings
Ģ Set up automatic charging for some or all booking types
Ģ Make selections for how refunds and declined charges are handled in the system

Payment Settings
This section contains all of the settings for your customer's payment options, including options for setting up automatic charging and pre-charge rules.

To manage your Payment settings, please go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the "Payment" section.

Do you want to allow a booking charged even if it's unassigned?
Ģ Choose "Yes" to allow bookings in the unassigned folder to be charged. This means that a provider does not need to be assigned to a booking for it to be moved to the Pending Charges section once the booking time has passed.
Ģ Choose "No" to block unassigned bookings from being charged. This means past bookings will remain in the Unassigned Folder until they are canceled or rescheduled.

Do you want bookings to be automatically charged after they are done? Another feature you can set up in this section is automatic charging. This may save time if you manage many different bookings each day.
Automatic charging can be enabled for some or all of your industries and services, so you can customize the types of jobs that are charged in this manner. You can also customize when the charge will occur and which types of payment methods this applies to.
Ģ Select "Yes" to enable automatic charging.
Ģ Select "No" to process all charges manually.

When should the bookings be charged? If you selected "Yes" for the previous question, pick an option for when you would like the automatic charge to occur.
Ģ Immediately - The booking will be charged as soon as it is "completed". The system considers a booking to be complete once the job length has passed.
? For example, if a 3-hour booking starts at 8:00 AM, the system will consider it completed after 11:00 AM.
? If automatic charging is enabled, the charge for this booking would be processed at 11:01 AM.
Ģ [#] hours after booking completion - The booking will be charged a specific number of hours after completion.
? Using the same booking as an example, if you enter "2" into the "hours" box, the booking will be charged at 1:01 PM.
? The booking is considered complete at 11:01 AM, so the automatic charge will be processed 2 hours after this time, at 1:01 PM.
Ģ At this time [##:##] [AM/PM] the day after job completion - The booking will be charged the following day at a set time.
? For example, if you enter the time "10:00 AM", all pending charges for the previous day will be processed at this time.

Which services does this apply to? You can customize which service categories will be charged automatically by checking the boxnext to its name.
In the example below, all of the home cleaning industry is enabled to automatically charge EXCEPT the two re-clean options, and NONE of the pet groomer service categories are enabled to automatically charge at all.
You may have a service category or industry requiring an admin to adjust before charging. You can uncheck the boxes for those services to ensure that no jobs in this category/industry are automatically charged.PLEASE NOTE: Whenever a new service category is created, it is not automatically added to the auto charge. You must enable automatic charging after creating the category.

Which payment methods does this apply to?Select which payment methods you would like to be charged automatically.
Check theboxes next to "Credit/Debit card" and/or "Cash/Check" to include those types of payment methods when automatically processing charges.

When a booking is pre-charged with a card, then updated to cash/check, should we refund the charge?
If you pre-charge customers before their appointment has happened, decide how the system should handle updating the payment method.
Ģ Select "Yes" to refund the pre-charged amount to the customer's credit/debit card.
Ģ Select "No" to keep the pre-charge when switching the booking to cash/check.

Should the system refund the additional amount charged if the booking was pre-charged and later the amount was decreased, provided there are no existing refund logs? (Refunds will be processed upon booking completion)
This setting allows you to decide whether you would like to refund any difference in price if the pre-charge amount ends up being more than the final booking total.
Ģ Select "Yes" for the system to automatically refund the difference once the booking has been completed.
? For example, the total for a 3-hour booking at 8 AM is precharged $200. During the service, the client excludes a service they had originally scheduled. The booking is updated and the new total is $180. Once the booking is finished at 11 AM, the system will process a refund of $20. $200 (pre-charge) - $180 (final total) = -$20 (automatically processed as a refund)

If there have been refunds on the booking and the total amount changes, how would you like the system to handle any additional charges or refunds?
Ģ Consider Refund - If you choose this option, the system will consider both the amount charged initially and any refunds issued, ensuring accurate adjustments to the booking total.
Ģ Do Not Consider Refund - By selecting this option, the system will base adjustments solely on the initial charged amount, without considering any refunds issued previously.

Should the system refund any extra charges if the booking was pre-charged and the total amount was subsequently reduced, given that there are existing refund records? (Refunds will be processed upon booking completion.)
Ģ Yes, refund the additional amount. - If you choose this option, the difference between the precharged amount and the final total will be automatically refunded back to the customer.
Ģ No, do not refund the additional amount. - If you choose this option, you retain the full precharged payment of the booking.

What should happen when a charge is declined while pre-payment of booking?
Decide how you'd like the system to respond when a pre-payment charge is declined.
Ģ Select "Add booking & notify admin & leave the booking as it is" for the system to notify you whenever this happens. The booking will be saved and remain confirmed regardless of whether the pre-charge is collected or not.
Ģ Select "Add booking, notify admin & cancel before 24 hours if not yet updated" for the system to notify you and save the booking into the system. If the pre-charge is not collected 24 hours before the start time, the booking will be canceled by the system.
? To ensure that you are notified about booking cancelations due to card hold failure, make sure you have enabled the "Booking canceled due to card hold failure" notifications.
Ģ These can be enabled for email and/or SMS by going to Settings > Notifications > Email/SMS > Admin > Canceled Booking.
? To ensure that your customers are notified about booking cancelations due to card hold failure, make sure you have enabled the "Booking canceled due to card hold failure" notifications.
Ģ These can be enabled for email and/or SMS by going to Settings > Notifications > Email/SMS > Customer > Canceled Booking.

Do you want to send individual charge notifications to the customer with the bulk charge or invoice charge?
Suppose Booking 1, Booking 2, and Booking 3 are bookings or invoices that are $100 each and when a full charge is performed, then a single charge notification is sent to the customer in the total amount of $300.
Ģ Select "Yes" if you want individual charge notifications for each $100 booking will be sent to the customer along with the single $300 charge notification
Ģ Select "No" if you only want the customer to receive a single $300 charge notification.

Remember to Save!
If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue "Save" button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.



Add your location name to the customer's address

Address Settings

Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the "Address Settings" section.

Would you like to show the location name at the end of the address?
Ģ Select "Yes" if you would like to include the location name you set up at the end of your customer's address in the system.
? For example, let's say one of your service areas is called "Uptown". This area contains all zip codes for the northern section of a city.
? A customer schedules a booking for 4545 N. Sheridan Road, Chicago, IL 60640 in this service area.
? When this setting is enabled, their address will be displayed in the system as 4545 N. Sheridan Road, Chicago, IL 60640 - Uptown.
Ģ Select "No" to keep the address format as entered by the customer.

Remember to Save!
If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue "Save" button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.



How to set up reschedule fees
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-rescheduling-fees-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

How to set up reschedule fees
Learn how to set up and charge fees whenever a customer rescheduled and/or postpones their service.

Last updated on 04 Jun, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Learn how reschedule fees work
Ģ Set up rescheduling fees

Rescheduling Fees
Rescheduling fees are fees that are charged when a customer rescheduled their booking within a certain period before the start of the appointment.

When a rescheduling fee is in place, the customer will see a pop-up informing them that they will be charged a cancelation fee if they decide to reschedule their appointment.

Rescheduling fees can compensate the business and provider for lost work.

To manage your Rescheduling Fee settings, please go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the "Rescheduling Fee" section.

Would you like to charge a rescheduling fee?
If you do not want to charge customers a fee when they reschedule, select "No" to this question.

If you would like to charge a customer a fee when they reschedule services, select "Yes" and enter the amount in the box below.

The fee can be set as a fixed rate by selecting the "$" from the drop-down menu or selecting "%" for a percentage-based discount.

Override the Service Category Settings
Rescheduling fees can also be customized for each service category in your booking form. If you would like your General Settings to override any rescheduling fees you have already set up, check the box next to: [Override the Service Category Settings]

What do you consider a rescheduled booking?
This option allows you to choose what the system considers as "rescheduling" a booking. This directly impacts how your system alerts and notifications work when bookings are rescheduled.

Ģ If you check the box next to "Date", the system will consider a booking as "rescheduled" specifically if the date changes.
Ģ If you check the box next to "Time (Spots)", the system will consider a booking as "rescheduled" specifically if the booking's start time changes.
Ģ If you select "Any changes made to the booking", the system will consider any updates to any of the booking details as having "rescheduled" the booking. For example, if a customer adds a new service but does not change the date or time.

You can select up to all three options depending on your preference.

When would you like the system to charge the customer a rescheduling fee?
Use this to set when the rescheduling fee will be charged. You can choose between two options:

Ģ If the customer reschedules after a specific time the day before the job.
? For example, if you set the boxes to be "05:00" and "PM", customers will be charged the fee if they reschedule any time after 5:01 PM the day before the booking.
Ģ If the customer rescheduled less than a specific amount of hours before the job starts.
? For example, you can select the number of hours up to 120, meaning the customer will be charged a fee if they reschedule less than 5 days before the booking.
? If you would like to charge a fee any time a customer reschedules at all, you can select "00".

Below, select any additional rescheduling fee settings that fit your business's needs.

Exclude Same-Day Bookings
Check this box if you would not like to charge a rescheduling fee if a booking is added to the schedule today and then rescheduled.

Multiple Rescheduling Fees
Check this box if you would like to charge multiple rescheduling fees each time a booking is rescheduled within a 24 hours period.

For example: Let's say a customer reschedules their booking at 8:00 AM today. At 8:05 AM, they realized they selected the wrong date and rescheduled again.

When this setting is enabled, the customer will be charged the rescheduling fee at 8:00 AM and again at 8:05 AM.

Booking Postpone Fees
Check this box if you would to charge the rescheduling fee when a customer postpones their service.

Remember to Save!
If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue "Save" button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.



Set up cancellation fees & reasons
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-cancelation-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

Set up cancellation fees & reasons
Learn how to set up your cancellation settings, including when a customer can cancel and if fees will be charged.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Set up cancelation fees
Ģ Create and enable cancelation reasons

Cancelation Settings
This section contains all the settings that determine what happens in the system when a customer cancels their booking.

To manage your general Cancelation settings, please go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the " Cancelation " section.

Do you charge a cancellation fee?
If you select " No", your customers will not be charged a cancelation fee under any circumstance.
If you would like to charge a fee when a customer cancels their booking, select "Yes ".

Do you want to set a single cancellation fee or multiple cancellation fees?
Select " Single" if you would like a single cancellation fee.
The fee can be set as afixed rate by selecting the " $ " from the drop-down menu or selecting " % " for a percentage-based discount.

Select " Multiple " if you would like to set up multiple cancellation fees. Click the " Add Fee " button to set the time frame, provider payment, and fee in the resulting pop-up.

If you would like to pay some or all of the cancelation fee to the provider, click the red Disabled button to switch it to Enabled , then set the payment rate per cancelation.

Override the Service Category Settings
Cancelation fees can also be customized for each service category in your booking form. If you would like your General Settings to override any cancelation fees you have already set up, check the box next to: (Override the Service Category Settings)

Should the system stop automatically adding new bookings to the end of the schedule if a recurring service is canceled midway?
If you select " Yes" if the system should NOT continue to add bookings to the end of a recurring service once a service is canceled midway through. This assumes the customer wants a limited number of recurring bookings with the service set to end at a specified date.
If you select "No " if the system should continue to add bookings to the end of a recurring service even if a service is canceled midway through. This assumes the customer only wanted to cancel one or several appointments in the middle of the service and that the system should continue to book recurring bookings after the canceled dates/.

When will your customers be charged a cancellation fee?
If you are going to charge cancelation fees, you can use this to set when the cancelation fee will be charged. You can choose between two options:
Ģ If the customer cancels after a specific time the day before the job.
? For example, if you set the boxes to be "05:00" and "PM", customers will be charged the fee if they cancel any time after 5:01 PM the day before the booking.
Ģ If the customer cancels less than a specific amount of hours before the job starts.
? For example, you can select the number of hours up to 120 , meaning the customer will be charged a fee if they cancel less than 5 days before the booking.
? If you would like to charge a fee any time a customer cancels at all, you can select " 00 ".

Exclude same day bookings
Check this box if you would not like to charge a cancelation fee if a booking is added to the schedule today and then rescheduled.

Would you like to set up reasons for why the booking is being canceled?
When you, your staff, or your customer cancels an appointment, you can ask them to select the reason for the cancelation.
To enable this feature, select "Yes" under "Would you like to set up reasons for why the booking is being canceled?"
Below, click the blue "Manage Reasons " link to view or modify the list of cancelation reasons.

The system will come with a list of default reasons.
Click the blue "Add New " button to add a new reason to the list. You can customize which reasons will display using the options on the Add/Edit page .

Reason Title Type the reason into the box below where it says " Reason title ". Please use alphanumeric digits only. Symbols and special characters cannot be used.

Frequency
Ģ Check the box next to One Time if you would like the reason to display when a one-time booking is canceled.
Ģ Check the box next to Recurring if you would like the reason to display when a recurring service is canceled.

Applicable On
Ģ Check " Cancel all recurring appointments " if you would like the reason to display when a customer cancels an entire recurring service.
Ģ Check " Cancel single appointment " if you want the reason to show when a single appointment is canceled.
Ģ Check " Exclude cancelation fee " if you do not want to charge the customer a cancelation fee when they select this reason.
Ģ Check " Exclude cancelation after 1st appointment fee " to exclude charging additional fees if a customer cancels a recurring service after having only on booking.
? This option only applies if you give recurring customers a discount based on their frequency.
? If the customer cancels after having just one appointment, the system will charge them any difference between the one-time and discounted rate.

To edit an existing reason, click " Options ", " Edit".
To reorder the list, click and drag the reason to a new position and then click the orange "Update Priority" button.
If you uncheck theboxnext to the reason's title, it will be hidden from the list.
To delete a reason, click "Options ", " Delete".
Once you are done modifying your list of cancelation reasons, click the green "Save " button at the bottom of the pop-up window.

Would you like it to be optional to give a reason with the cancellation?
Ģ Select " Yes " to make selecting the cancelation reason optional.
Ģ Select " No " to require at least one cancelation reason to be selected in order to cancel the service.

Would you like to have a comment box in the cancellation section?
Ģ Select " Yes " to include a comment box to collect feedback when a booking is canceled.
Ģ Select " No" to hide the comment box.
If you have selected "Yes ", can you decide if the comment box will be included when a One Time and/or Recurring service is canceled

Would you like it to be optional to leave a comment with the cancellation?
Ģ Select " Yes " to make leaving a comment optional.
Ģ Select " No" to require a comment before they can cancel.
If you have selected "Yes ", can you decide if the comment box will be included when a One Time and/or Recurring service is canceled

Please select which services you would like to display the cancellation reasons in the pop-up
This section will list every industry and service category in your account. Check the box next to the service's name to display cancelation reasons when a booking in this category is canceled.
To select all service categories in your industry, check the box that says "All ".

Will the reasons in the pop-up be displayed for the admin only or customers as well?
Select whether you would like the cancelation reasons pop up to be displayed for only admin and staff , or for both customers and admin/staff.

Remember to Save!
If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue " Save " button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.



How to set up automatic credit card holds/preauthorizations
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-credit-card-hold-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

How to set up automatic credit card holds/preauthorizations
Learn how to set up card preauthorizations, or card "holds", to secure payments made with credit cards.

Last updated on 16 Oct, 2023

The purpose of this article is to help you:

Ģ Set up credit card holds for your bookings
Ģ Enable your card hold notifications
Ģ Understand your options for rescheduling a booking when the card hold has failed

Put Credit Card on Hold
The " Put Credit Card on Hold " section under your Store Options contains the settings for placing a pre-authorization or card " hold " on payments made with credit cards. If you allow your customers to book with credit cards and you are worried about payments failing, you can enable the card hold settings to make ensure the payment is secured before the booking takes place.

To manage your credit card hold settings, please go to:

Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the " Put Credit Card on Hold " section.

Would you like to place a hold on the customer's card payment to ensure funds?

Ģ Select " Yes " to enable pre-authorization of payments for your bookings.
Ģ Select " No " to disable the card hold feature.

Which of your payment charging methods would you like this to be enabled for?

If you answered " Yes " to the previous question, select which charging methods you'd like the hold to be enabled for:

Ģ Manual Charging - Card holds will only be applied to bookings that are charged manually.
Ģ Automatic Charging - Card holds will only be applied to bookings that are charged automatically.
Ģ Both - Card holds will be used for bookings that will be manually and automatically charged.

Which of the booking types would you like this enabled for?

If you selected " Manual Charging " or " Both " for the previous question, decide which booking types you would like to have a charge pre-authorization.

Ģ New Booking - The hold is only applied when a new booking is added to the system.
Ģ Reschedule booking - The hold is only applied upon rescheduling an existing booking in the system.
Ģ Both - Card holds will be placed whenever a new booking comes in and when bookings are rescheduled.

Do you want to allow the booking addition when a card hold fails?

This option allows you to choose whether to allow booking addition when a card hold fails.

Ģ Select " Yes " to add new bookings even if card holds fail.
Ģ Select " No " to add new bookings only if the card holds succeed.

NOTE : This option only works in case of a new booking.

When would you like to place the booking amount on hold?

Decide when you would like the hold to take place.

Ģ The day of the booking at [##:## AM/PM] - The pre-authorization will occur the day of the booking at a set time.

? For example, let's say the hold time is set to "6:00 AM" the day of the booking.  There are services booked for 8 AM, 9 AM, and 1 PM. When the clock hits 6:00 AM, the card holds will be placed on all the bookings regardless of their start time.
Ģ [#] day(s) before the booking date - The pre-authorization will occur a set number of days before the booking.

? For example, let's say the hold time is set to 2 days before the booking date.  If a job is scheduled for 10 AM on March 5, the pre-authorization will occur at 10 AM on March 3.

What would you like to happen if the hold for the booking amount fails?

If you selected " Automatic Charging " or " Both " for the previous question, decide what you would like to happen if the card hold fails.

Ģ Cancel the booking immediately and notify the customer, provider, and myself -

? To ensure that you are notified about booking cancelations due to card hold failure, make sure you have enabled the " Booking canceled due to card hold failure " notifications.

Ģ These can be enabled for email and/or SMS by going to Settings > Notifications > Email/SMS > Admin > Canceled Booking.
? To ensure that your customers are notified about booking cancelations due to card hold failure, make sure you have enabled the " Booking canceled due to card hold failure " notifications.

Ģ These can be enabled for email and/or SMS by going to Settings > Notifications > Email/SMS > Customer > Canceled Booking.
? To ensure that your providers are notified about booking cancelations, make sure you have enabled the " Booking canceled " notifications.

Ģ These can be enabled for email and/or SMS by going to Settings > Notifications > Email/SMS > Customer > Canceled Booking
Ģ Notify the customer and myself and cancel the booking 24 hours before it is scheduled to be started - If you have set up pre-authorizations to take place a fixed number of days before the booking, you can notify the customer and yourself at the time of the failed transaction but wait to cancel the booking until 24 hours before.

? The booking will not be canceled as long as the hold is successful before you reach 24 hours before the start time.
Ģ Leave the booking and notify me - You will receive a notification that the card hold failed and no further action will be taken by the system. This means the customer will NOT be notified of the card hold failure.

Please note : We are working on a fourth option to notify the admin AND the customer but leave the booking. We will send out an announcement once this update has been released.

Remember to Save!

If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue " Save " button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.

Card Hold Notifications
There are several email templates to alert the admin and customers if/when a card hold fails.  For the system to send them out automatically, make sure they have been enabled in your Notifications section.

To enable them, go to:

Settings > Notifications > Emails/SMS > Admin/Customer > Card Declined

If the button next to the template reads " Disabled ", click it to switch it to "Enabled". This will turn on the notification.

You can also enable the notification for " Booking modified but card hold failed " if you or your customers would like to be notified if the card hold fails due to a change in the booking price.

Rescheduling a Booking with a Card Hold Failure
If you are going to reschedule a booking and your payment processor fails to hold the card, a pop-up will appear in BookingKoala with several options.

Ģ Switch to cash/check and update the booking : In this case, the booking will be updated and switched to the " Cash/Check " payment method. Please note, you will need to select a card on file before you can charge the booking via credit or debit card.
Ģ Cancel booking : The booking will be canceled in the system. The admin or the customer can resume this booking by adding a valid credit card in the Payment Method section at the bottom of the booking form or in the customer's billing section under their Profile .
Ģ Close and try another card : This will close the popup and nothing will be updated in the booking. You can then make changes to the card details, try a different card, or select " Cash/Check " manually.

Edit the message customers and providers see when they are blocked or deactivated
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-access-settings-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

Edit the message customers and providers see when they are blocked or deactivated
Learn how to modify the messages displayed when a deactivated/blocked customer or provider sees when they try to log in.

Last updated on 14 Mar, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Change the access messages that are displayed when a provider or customer has been blocked from accessing their account

Access Settings
The Access Settings section under your General Store Options controls what your customers and providers see when they are blocked or deactivated.

Providers can be blocked from accessing their account temporarily or deactivated by clicking on the provider's name and going to their dashboard. Click the grey "Deactivate" button or the yellow "Block Access" button at the top right corner of their profile.

Customers can be blocked from accessing their account temporarily or deactivated by clicking on the customer's name and going to their dashboard. Click the grey "Deactivate" button or the yellow "Block Access" button at the top right corner of their profile.

When the customer or provider goes to log in, a red notification will appear at the top center of the page, displaying the message in your settings.

To change these messages, go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the "Access Settings" section.

Customer Message
The default message for blocked or deactivated customers is, "We apologize for the inconvenience. Please contact our office if you have any questions." If you would like to change this, type your new message into the box below.

Provider Message
The default message for blocked or deactivated providers is, "We apologize for the inconvenience. Please contact our office if you have any questions." If you would like to change this, type your new message into the box below.

Remember to Save!

How to enable/disable "Calendar View" and customize how the schedule looks
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-the-calendar-section-under-the-store-options-general-tab

How to enable/disable "Calendar View" and customize how the schedule looks
Learn how to enable or disable the calendar view and customize your calendar view preferences.

Last updated on 14 Mar, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Enable or disable calendar view
Ģ Set your default booking view
Ģ Set the default time frame in the calendar view
Ģ Set the default for where provider names display in the calendar view
Ģ Select how customer names display in the month view
Ģ Set how bookings for the same day and time will display
Ģ Choose how to display unavailable times for providers

Calendar Settings
To manage your general Calendar Settings, please go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General

Click the plus sign icon to expand the "Calendar Settings" section.

These settings pertain to using the calendar view when looking at bookings in the system.

As mentioned above, the calendar view button can be found in any section where bookings are listed, including:
Ģ Today's Bookings
Ģ Upcoming Bookings
Ģ Job History
Ģ Unassigned Bookings
Ģ Providers' dashboards
Ģ Customers' dashboards

Would you like to disable the calendar view?
If you do not want to use the calendar view at all, you can remove the option from the system by selecting "Yes" to this question.

If you answer "No", proceed by making selections for the following questions:

Would you like your bookings to appear in the calendar view or listing view by default?
Ģ Select "Calendar view" to set your default view for all sections to this.
Ģ Select "Listing view" instead if you prefer to see bookings in a list.

You will still be able to switch between viewing modes at any time.

How would you like to set up the calendar view by default?
Decide which time frame you would like the calendar view to display by default.

Ģ "Month" view will show every day of the current month.
Ģ "Week" view will show every day of the current week.
Ģ "Day" view will show the current date.

You will still be able to switch between month-, week-, and day-view in the booking sections.

Where should the providers show on your calendar by default?
Decide how you would like your providers to be displayed in calendar view.

Ģ Select "Top" to place the provider's names at the top of the calendar view. This corresponds with the "Vertical" view of the job calendar.
Ģ Select "Timelines/Horizontal" to place the provider names on the left side of the calendar. This corresponds with the "Horizontal" view of the calendar.
Ģ Select "Side" to display the provider names in a bar on the right side of the calendar. This corresponds with the "Sidebar" view of the calendar.
Ģ You can hide the provider sidebar at any time by clicking the "people" icon at the top of the page, or the "x" in the top right corner of the sidebar.

Would you like to show customer names in the month view or the dots for bookings?
Ģ Choose "Names" to see the customer's full name in a small box on the monthly calendar view.
Ģ Choose "Dots" to see dots that represent each booking on the monthly calendar view.
? You can view the booking's details by hovering over a dot or clicking it with your curser.

How do you want to show multiple bookings on a single date?
Ģ "Side by side" prioritizes the customer details by displaying two bookings next to each other when they start at the same time.
Ģ "Overlapped" prioritizes how much space is being taken up in a column by overlapping shorter appointments on top of longer appointments starting at the same time.

Would you like to hide the provider's non-working hours (grey area)?
Ģ Select "Yes" to hide the provider's non-work hours completely from the calendar view.
Ģ Select "No" to show the provider's non-working hours as "greyed out".

Remember to Save!
If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue "Save" button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.



Query parameters & preselected fields on the booking form
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/pre-select-fields-on-the-booking-form

Query parameters & preselected fields on the booking form
Learn how to preselect value, like frequencies, service categories, extras, and pricing parameters on the forms passed through URLs as "query parameters".

Last updated on 13 Mar, 2023

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ What are query parameters
Ģ How to create a custom URL with preselected fields
Ģ Which query parameters are available in BookingKoala
Ģ How to locate field IDs to build your URL

What are query parameters?
Query parameters are any values passed in an URL in "key/value pair format". This means that the link contains a label, an "equal sign" (=), and the value of the parameter you want to be preselected.

For example: https://www.bookingkoala.com/?service_id=1&frequency_id=1&email=testemail@example.com

Here, "service_id", "frequency_id" and "email" are query parameters with their associated values, "=1", "=1", and "=testemail@exmaple.com".

The associated values correspond which each option you have selected in the booking form in the case of the service and frequency IDs, while the email is placed in the email box in the booking form.

You can use this feature to build custom links with pre-selected fields to send to your customers so they can quickly book a service with those parameters already selected. Query parameters can also be used to direct a client to your booking form from a third-party form or widget.

BookingKoala Query Parameters
The following is the complete list of fields that can be passed to form using URL/query parameters and examples of values.

Ģ Industry: industry_id=1
Ģ Form number: form_id=1
Ģ Location: location=2
Ģ First Name: f_name=John
Ģ Last Name: l_name=Doe
Ģ Email Address: email=johndoe@example.com
Ģ Phone Number: phone=1122334455
Ģ Service Category: service_id=1
Ģ Frequency: frequency_id=1
Ģ Zip/Postal Code: zipcode=10001
Ģ Minutes: minutes=180
Ģ Coupon Code: coupon=OFF20
Ģ Pricing Parameters (for Form 1): pricing_parameter[10]=15
Ģ Pricing Parameters (for Form 4): area_parameter[10]=2000
Ģ Extras: extras[12]=2
Ģ Service Date: date=2018-12-13

Locating the Field IDs
Some of these parameters can be populated by yourself.

Ģ Industry: Enter the industry's number (the first industry you added will be 1, the second will be 2, etc.)
? For example, your third industry would be industry_id=3
Ģ Form number: Enter the form's number.
? For example, "Form 1" would be: form_id=1
Ģ Location: Enter the location's number (the first location in your location list will be 1, the second will be 2, etc.)
? For example, the fourth location would be: location=4
Ģ First Name: Enter the customer's first name.
? For example, if a customer's first name is "Rebecca", you would enter f_name=Rebecca
Ģ Last Name: Enter the customer's last name.
? For example, if a customer's last name is "Dawson", you would enter l_name=Dawson
Ģ Email Address: Enter the customer's email address.
? For example, if a client's email is "johndoe@example.com", you would enter email=johndoe@example.com
Ģ Phone Number: Enter the customer's phone number.
? For example, the phone number "(203) 555-1234" would be phone=2035551234
Ģ Zip/Postal Code: Enter the customer's zip/postal code.
? For example, the zipcode "60626" would be zipcode=60626
Ģ Minutes: Enter the number of minutes the appointment will take (if hourly).
? For example, 1 hour and 30 minutes would be minutes=90
Ģ Coupon Code: Enter the coupon code (not the coupon name).
? For example, if the actual coupon code is "FB15", you would enter coupon=FB15
Ģ Pricing Parameters (for Form 4): Enter the total number of units for your variable.
? For example, if your variable is "Square Feet", "2000 square feet would be: area_parameter[10]=2000
Ģ Service Date: Enter the service date in YEAR-MONTH-DATE format.
? For example, if the date is March 1, 2022, you would enter date=2022-03-01

Other parameters can be found inside your BookingKoala Account by going to: Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] >

Ģ Service Category: Click on the "Service Category" tab and then click on the ID in the "ID" column to copy it.
? To preselect the "Flat Rate Service" category, you would type: service_id=66
? "66" is the ID of this frequency
Ģ Frequency: Click on the "Frequency" tab and then click on the ID in the "ID" column to copy it.
? To preselect the "As Needed" frequency, you would type: frequency_id=1
? "1" is the ID of this frequency
Ģ Pricing Parameters (for Form 1):
? To set up query parameters for pricing parameters, there are two different IDs that you will need to collect, the variable category id and the pricing parameter ID.
Ģ Click on the orange "Manage Variables" button at the top right corner of the Pricing Parameter section and copy the ID next to the parameter's category.
Ģ Next, go back to the Pricing Parameter second and then click on the ID in the parameter's "ID" column to copy it.
? Using these cases as an example, you would type pricing_parameter[1]=3 to preselect "1 bedroom" on the booking form.
Ģ "1" is the variable category's ID (bedrooms)
Ģ "3" is the specific variable (1 bedroom).
Ģ Extras: Click on the "Extras" tab and then click on the ID in the "ID" column to copy it.
? To preselect the "Junk Removal" extra, you would type: extras[88]=1
? "88" is the extra's ID, and "1" is the quantity of this extra

If you were to compile a URL with some of the preselected values in this example, it would look like this: https://demo2.bookingkoala.com/?industry_id=1&frequency_id=1&pricing_parameter[1]=3&extras[12]=1&coupon=FB15

Create A Custom URL with Preselected Values
463 Type in the full domain beginning with "https://".
464 Type "?"
465 Enter your first query parameter, for example, "date="
466 Enter the parameter's value or ID, for example, "2022-10-23"
467 Type "&" before entering another query parameter.
468 Repeat steps 4 and 5 until your URL is complete, then finish on step 4 with your final parameter.

https://yourbusiness.com/?date=2022-10-23&minutes=60



Setting up sales tax
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-sales-tax

Setting up sales tax
Learn how to set up sales tax for your services using flat tax.

Last updated on 09 Aug, 2024

Ģ Enable sales tax
Ģ Set up flat-rate taxes for your services
Ģ Change your sales tax label
Ģ Charge tax on the service fee

Enable Sales Tax
If your local government requires sales tax for your services, you can set up your BookingKoala account to charge tax on top of their service total.

There are two different options when it comes to setting up your sales tax.

469 If you know your local tax rate and it will not change based on the services your customer purchases, you can set up flat-rate tax for each of your locations.
470 If your local taxes are complicated or change based on services and locale, you can set up and connect a third-party app called Taxify to calculate your sales tax. Click here to learn how to link Taxify to your BookingKoala account.

To begin, go to:

Settings > General > Taxes

Click on the red " Disabled " button to change it to " Enabled ".

Flat-Rate Sales Tax
If you'd like to enter a flat-rate tax for your locations, select " Flat " under " Do you want to use Taxify or flat tax? "

Next, decide whether you want to set up different taxes per location .

Ģ Select " No " to have one tax rate for all of your locations.  

Ģ Enter the tax percentage in the box below, and then click the blue " Save " button.
Ģ Select " Yes " to enable different sales tax per each location you service.

Click the blue " Save " button to proceed. A " Location Taxes " section will be added below.

Select a location from the first drop-down menu, and then enter the tax percentage into the second box.  Lastly, click the blue " Save " button to add sales tax for that location.

Once you click " Save ", the location and the corresponding tax amount will be added below.

If you'd like to edit or delete the tax for any added locations, click " Options " and " Edit " or " Delete ".

Change the Tax Label
To change what the "sales tax" label says in your account, go to:

Settings > General > Taxes

Scroll down to the "Customize Tax Label" section and enter the new term into the box below.

Then press the blue "Save" button.

Charge Sales Tax on the Service Fee
If you also charge service fees based on your locations, you can decide whether or not you would like those fees to be tax-exempt or not. This option is controlled under your Form Settings .

To charge sales tax on your service fee, please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Next, click " Options ", and then " Edit " next to the form you'd like to update.

Scroll down until you see the question, " Would you like to make the service fee taxable? " and select " Yes ".

Please note : If you have existing bookings in the system already, this change will only impact new bookings after the setting has been saved.

Then, click the blue " Save " button at the bottom of the page to update your form.  Repeat these steps for every form you'd like to charge sales tax on the service fee.

Tax-Exempt Locations
If you have tax-exempt locations, you can update your location settings to reflect them as such.  To open your locations, please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > 1. Locations

Next, select Options then Edit to open the location in which you would like to exempt taxes.

Then, scroll down to the bottom of the Details tab and select " Yes " for the question, " Do you wish to exempt sales tax for this location? "

Then, click the blue " Next " buttons until you reach " Update ". Click " Update " to save your changes.



Flushing the test data
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/flushing-the-test-data

Flushing the test data
Learn the purpose of test data in your BookingKoala account and how to remove it.

Last updated on 01 Sept, 2022

In this article, you will learn more about:
Ģ What is test data
Ģ How to flush test data from your account

What is test data?
When you first go inside your BookingKoala dashboard, you will see that it is pre-loaded with fake test data. This is to show you what the system looks like when it's been set up with store options, customers, providers, bookings, and more.

When you are ready to create real bookings, schedule customers, and add providers, you'll need to remove the test data from the system. Specifically, this means removing the fake customer, the fake provider and fake team, the test location, all the fake job history, all the fake upcoming bookings, and all the fake charges.

It is especially important to flush the test data before incorporating your payment processor, as any existing bookings with payment options may affect the accuracy of your revenue and earnings reports.

Flushing the test data will NOT affect any of the store settings that you may have changed, new industries you've added, and form components like frequencies, service categories, pricing under items/add-ons/packages/pricing parameters/variables, and extras.

Flushing Your Test Data
Once you have gotten a feel for the dashboard, you can flush the test data out of the system. To begin, please go to:

Settings > General > Store Options > Admin Tab

At the very bottom of the page, click the red Flush Test Data button . A pop-up box will appear, confirming you would like to delete the data. Select Yes to continue.

If the " Flush Test Data " button or section is missing, the data has already been deleted and you are ready to start adding real customers, providers, and bookings.



Setting up your invoice settings in "Store Options"
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-your-invoice-settings-in-store-options

Setting up your invoice settings in "Store Options"
Learn how to set up your invoice settings in the General Store Options tab.

Last updated on 03 Jun, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ How to set up your invoice store option settings

Invoice Settings
There is just one setting in this section: " What should happen if you have a card hold with invoiced bookings? Do you prefer to release the card hold and perform a single charge of the invoice or capture the card hold and perform other charges separately? "

Ģ Select " Release " to release the card hold for the system to perform a single charge of the invoice.
Ģ Select " Capture " to capture the card hold in one transaction and perform any other additional charges in a separate transaction.

Remember to Save!

If you have made any changes, remember to click the blue " Save " button at the bottom left corner of the page to update your settings.



Switching payment processors
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/switching-payment-processors

Switching payment processors
Learn about the recommended steps on how to change from one payment processor to another in your BookingKoala account.

Last updated on 05 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to switch payment processors

Switching Your Payment Processor
Step 1: Migrate Your Customer Card Details

First, it is important to understand that your existing customer's cards need to be added again to your new payment processor account, as BookingKoala does not store cards on our platform.

You will first need to move your customer cards from your current payment processor account to your new payment processor account before you can proceed with switching.

Ģ We recommend contacting customer support for both your current and new payment processors to get assistance with migrating the customer's card details from the current account to the new account.
Ģ Click the links below to locate the support centers for each of our integrated payment processors.
? Stripe Support
? Square Support
? Braintree by PayPal Support
? Authorize.net Support

Step 2:

Go to: Settings > General > Store Options > General tab > Payment section

and ensure that you have selected the " No " option for the question " When a booking is pre-charged with a card, then updated to cash/check, should we refund the charge? ".

This is to make sure that any precharged card transactions are not automatically refunded when you switch your bookings from an existing credit card to cash/check.

Step 3: Update Active Bookings to Cash/Check

The next step is to change all of your active bookings with existing cards to "cash/check".

As they are already associated with the old payment processor's keys, the system will not allow the addition of new payment processor keys until there are no bookings left with the existing keys.

For this, please check for the active bookings with existing card(s) in the " List View " at the following places:

471 Bookings > Today's Booking
472 Bookings > Upcoming Bookings
473 Bookings > Unassigned Bookings
474 Bookings > Booking Charges > Pending Charges
475 Bookings > Booking Charges > Declined
476 Bookings > Booking Charges > Card Hold(s) / Decline(s) > Card Holds
477 Bookings > Booking Charges > Card Hold(s) / Decline(s) > Card Declined

Click the pencil icon to edit the booking and scroll down to the bottom of the page to the Payment Information section. Select " Cash/Check " (or whatever you have named this option) as the payment method for the booking(s), then click the blue " Update Booking " button from the bottom or side menu.

You will also want to ensure that there are no pending provider payments connected to your original payment processor. To check for these, please go to:

Providers > Payments

Select the last 6 months or longer for the pay period to check for outstanding payments.

If there are outstanding provider payments, select " Options " then " Pay " (or " Cash / Check " if you are paying the provider outside of Bookingkoala).

Step 4: Connect Your New Payment Processor

Once you have switched all of your bookings to "cash/check", you are ready to integrate your new payment processor. Please follow the instructions linked below to set up and connect the new payment processor.
Ģ Stripe
Ģ Square
Ģ PayPal
Ģ Authorize.net

Step 5: Prepare and Share Your Client CSV File

Once the customer's card data is migrated from your original account into your new payment processor account, the customer will have a new ID. Export this information from your new payment processor and add it to a CSV file along with the client's other data like their first name , last name , address , email address , phone number , etc.

Next, share this file with us at support@bookingkoala.com and request that we map the data via the backend of your BookingKoala account. Please be sure you have fully completed steps 1-3 before emailing with your request.

Step 6: Update Your Bookings

Update the bookings with your customer's card details. You can do this by going to:

478 Bookings > Today's Booking
479 Bookings > Upcoming Bookings
480 Bookings > Unassigned Bookings
481 Bookings > Booking Charges > Pending Charges
482 Bookings > Booking Charges > Declined
483 Bookings > Booking Charges > Card Hold(s) / Decline(s) > Card Holds
484 Bookings > Booking Charges > Card Hold(s) / Decline(s) > Card Declined

Click the pencil icon to edit the booking and scroll down to the bottom of the page to the Payment Information section. Select " Existing Credit Card ", then click the blue " Update Booking " button from the bottom or side menu.



Delete or consolidate locations
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/delete-or-consolidate-locations

Delete or consolidate locations
Learn how to delete or consolidate locations in your BookingKoala account.

Last updated on 12 Nov, 2024

In this article, you will learn:
Ģ How to consolidate locations
Ģ How to delete a location

Consolidate Locations
Whether you are restructuring your business locations/service areas or looking to downgrade your subscription to the Starter Plan, you can consolidate your locations into one primary location. This can be done a few different ways depending on the type of locations you have set up. To learn more about each setting up each kind of location, click here.

Consolidating Name-Based Locations

To consolidate name-based locations, the admin will need to come up with a name that encompasses their entire location/service area and rename one of their locations to that name.

Ģ For example, if you service the cities of Dallas, Austin, San Antonio, and Houston, you could consider renaming your one central location as "Texas".
Ģ If you service multiple locations that do not have a clear grouping, you could include the name of each location separated by commas like "Illinois, Texas, and Massachusetts" for your location name.

Next, you will need to create any current bookings as new bookings under your one central location. This step is necessary as any active bookings in your other locations will eventually need to be deleted. Once you have created all of your bookings under a single location, you are ready to proceed with deleting the other location(s).

Consolidating Zip Code-Based Locations

Consolidating zipcode-based locations will require the admin to take the additional step of moving their zip/postcodes from all locations to one central location. To do this, you need to remove the zip codes from your other locations and add them to your soon-to-be single location. Once this is complete, you are ready to proceed with deleting the other location(s).

Consolidating Merchant Locations

Currently, there is no way to list multiple addresses for a single merchant location. This means that the admin must pick one address to list as the primary merchant location per BookingKoala account.

Delete a Location
If you do not plan on using a location any longer, you can go to the "Locations" section for each form and industry to delete it. Please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Locations

Click on the "Options" button under the Actions column and select "Delete".

A pop-up will ask if you are certain you would like to delete this location. This will permanently remove all data associated with that location from your account. If you are certain you are ready to proceed, click "Yes" to proceed.

Please note that all active bookings and pending booking invitations (unassigned bookings) must be canceled or rescheduled before the system will allow you to delete the location.

A green system notification will confirm once the location has been deleted successfully.

Repeat these steps for each form and industry that includes the location(s) you would like deleted, as deleting a location from one form or industry does not automatically delete it from all forms and industries.



Hide job length from customers or providers
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/hide-job-length-from-customers-or-providers

Hide job length from customers or providers
Learn how to hide the estimated job length from your customers, providers, or even the admin/staff.

Last updated on 13 Nov, 2024

In this article, you will learn more about:
Ģ How to hide job length via the service category
Ģ How to hide job length via the Website & Theme Builder

Hide Job Length: Editing Your Service Categories
There are two ways to hide the job length from your customers or providers. The first is in your service categories. Service categories allow you to show or hide the estimated job length from the customers and providers, giving you the option to customize this per service type. To edit your service categories, go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Service Categories

Under the Action column, click Options, then Edit.

Scroll down the page until you reach where it says "Display service length on customer end". There are three possible options for this setting:
485 Customer frontend, backend, & admin - New customers, returning customers, and admin/staff will be able to see the service length
486 Customer backend & admin - Only returning customers and admin/staff will be able to see service length
487 Admin only - Only the admin and staff can see the estimated service length

Just below this setting, you will see the option "Display service length on provider end". If you select Yes, providers will be able to see the estimated service length for this service category. If you select No, the service length as well as the service length label will be hidden.

If you make any changes, be sure to click the "Next" button until you reach the final page, then click "Save" to update your service category.

Hide Job Length: Updating the Website & Theme Builder
The other way to hide the job length from your customers and providers is to update the job length visibility settings via the Website & Theme Builder. The Website & Theme Builder controls many visual aspects of your booking form, including the ability to show or hide job length from providers, customers, and admin/staff. To see these options, go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Click on the "Customize Theme" button.

Next, click on the top center Page menu and navigate to the Book Now page (or whatever you have named your booking form).

Once you are on your booking form page, scroll down and locate the Booking Summary (normally along the right side of the page or at the bottom beneath the form for the mobile version). Click on the labels within the booking summary to reveal the Edit and Rearrange buttons. Click "Edit".

Scroll down the left side menu that appears until you see the "Length" or "Job Length" label. Click on the "Control visibility" link to view the Control Visibility popup.

Under the text "Which user(s) should see this section?", uncheck the box(es) next to any user(s) you do not want to see the estimated job length. Please note the following:
Ģ This setting controls whether the job length appears in the booking form, provider booking details, and notifications sections.
Ģ This setting works in juxtaposition with the option located in the service category settings.
Ģ This setting only to non-hourly bookings. By nature, hourly bookings will always display the job length to all users.

Once you have made your selections, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom corner of the pop-up. To apply these changes right away, please click the blue "Save & Publish" button in the top right corner of the Website & Themes Builder.

Upon making this update, the selected users will be able to see the estimated job length, while the unselected users will not.
