Disable gift cards and referrals
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/disable-gift-cards-and-referrals

Disable gift cards and referrals
Learn how to disable gift cards and referrals from inside the website and theme builder.

Last updated on 24 Jul, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to disable the gift cards section
Ģ How to disable the referrals section

Disabling gift cards and referrals
If you do NOT want to use gift cards and/or the referral system that comes with your website, you can easily disable them from inside the website and theme builder. To do so, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Click on the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website and theme editor.

Click on the "Page" menu at the top-center of the screen, then select "Manage Pages".

A side menu will appear on the left.

To disable gift cards, click the three dots icons next to the "Gift Card" page and select "Hide". To disable the referral page, click the three dots icons next to the "Earn Credits" page and select "Hide".

A pop-up will appear asking "Are you sure you want to disable this page?" Select "Yes" to proceed with disabling the page.

A green system notification will appear in the top center of the page, letting you know that the page has been disabled successfully. You will now see an eye icon with a slash through it next to the page(s) you have disabled.

To push this update live to your website, click the blue "Save & Publish" button in the top right corner of the screen.

Now, customers can no longer purchase gift cards and/or send and receive referral credits from these pages. This also means that any referral credits the customer has earned previously can no longer be accessed.



Enable/disable the "How It Works" section
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/enabling-the-how-it-works-section

Enable/disable the "How It Works" section
What is the "How It Works" section?The "How It Works" section is an optional feature of your booking forms. When enabled, customers can click on the "How It Works" link on your booking page to view a video of how to schedule a service with your company or any other information you'd like them to know.
This option can be enabled per form (per industry) so you have the option to upload different videos depending on which form the customer is on.

Navigation Path to Edit the "How It Works" Section: Go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Step-by-step instructions to access the section:
573 Once the web builder loads, click on the top center Page menu and select the "Book Now" page.
574 Select an industry name and form number (if you have more than one).
575 Scroll down along the right side of the page until you see the "How It Works" box.

Disable the How It Works Feature: To disable the "How It Works" feature for a booking form, click on the box to reveal the Hide button. Once you click Hide, the box will now read "Hidden Element" and will not appear on the form.

Edit the How It Works Feature: To edit the "How It Works" section, click on the box and then select the Edit button. A Settings pop-up will appear where you can edit the text, video URL, and style of the feature.
576 Edit the text that is displayed in the "How it works" box.
577 The Video URL (see below).
578 Edit the color of the text and the background color of the "How it works" box.

Getting the Video URL:There will be a default BookingKoala video link that can be replaced with your own link.
To use your own link, you will first need to upload your video to a video-hosting site like youtube.com.
579 Once the video has been uploaded to the "Content" section on YouTube, click "View on YouTube".
580 Next, click the "Share" button and select "Embed" from the pop-up.
581 Copy just the URL in the embed code. For example, if your embed code looks like this: <iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/l3KmUzXK64E" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>, you will only copy the part that says: https://www.youtube.com/embed/l3KmUzXK64E.
582 Paste the link into the box to replace the BookingKoala video.
583 Click the "Save & Publish" button at the top right corner of the page.



How to reindex your website
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-reindex-your-website

How to reindex your website
Learn how long it takes for changes to your site to appear in search engines and how to get Google to re-crawl a page.

Last updated on 12 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How long it takes Google to display site updates
Ģ How do use Google's URL Inspection tool to re-crawl a specific page

How do I edit my site's metadata?
If you would like to learn how to view or update your website's metadata for each page, please click here (https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-your-website-s-seo-metadata).

How long does it take for site changes to appear in search results?
Whenever you make changes to your site's metadata (like the title tag, meta description, and keywords), Google and other search engines must "re-crawl" your page site to see differences between the saved version in its index and the new content. Because of this, changes will not immediately appear in search engine results. Often, it will take several weeks before the page has been crawled and the index is updated.

As well, the information you use in your page titles and descriptions are just guidelines for what Google will index. Ultimately, Google will index the content that it likes best and add it to its search results. Because of this, a website can appear differently in search results on a desktop versus a mobile device.

What is Google's URL Inspection Tool and how do I use it?
To speed up this process, you can request for Google to crawl or recrawl a specific page on your website, you can use the Google URL Inspection Tool. This will prompt Google to go through your page again and look for changes and updates.

Once you've logged into the Search Console, click here (https://developers.google.com/search/docs/advanced/crawling/ask-google-to-recrawl) to view instructions on how to request Google to recall your URLs.



Disable the header and footer menus
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/disable-the-header-and-footer-menus

Disable the header and footer menus
Learn how to hide or disable the header and footer menus from your website.

Last updated on 11 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to edit header and footer menus
Ģ How to disable a header or footer

What are the header and footer menus?
The header menu, when enabled, is the topmost section on any given page of your website. This menu displays your business's logo as well as links to important pages of your website. If you are utilizing the translation feature, you can also click the flag icon to switch between languages.

When enabled, the footer menu is displayed at the very bottom of your webpage. This menu also contains your business's logo, links to various pages, as well as additional options for linking social media buttons or adding contact information.

When you first sign up for BookingKoala, your account comes with a website that has the header and footer menus enabled for all pages. However, if you want to disable one or both, can you easily do so.

Disable the Header/Footer Menus for the Entire Site
To disable the header and/or footer in general, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Click on the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website and theme editor.

By default, you will land on your website's home page. Hover over the header section until a white "Edit" button appears in the top-left corner of the block.

The header menu will appear on the left side of the screen. Scroll down until you see the section "Menu before login" and click the "Edit Menu" link.

You will be redirected to the general menu for both your header and footer. To disable the header or footer for your entire site, click the three dots on the right side of the menu and select "Hide".

A pop-up will appear asking if you are sure you want to disable the header or footer menu. Click the blue "Yes" button to proceed.

A green system notification will appear at the top of the page confirming that the header or footer menu has been disabled. An eye icon with a slash through it indicates that this menu is now disabled.

Once you are finished in this section, click the X icon to exit the header/footer menu, or the back arrow icon to go back to the main menu.

To push your updates live, please remember to click the blue "Save & Publish" button at the top-right corner of the page. You can also click the "Save" button if you only want to save these changes in the backend.

Disable the Header/Footer Menus by Page
To disable the header and/or footer on a page-by-page basis, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Click on the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website and theme editor.

By default, you will land on your website's home page. To disable the header and/or footer for this page, click on the grey "Page Settings" button near the top right corner of the screen.

A page settings menu will appear. To disable the header or footer, select a page, click on the Layouts tab, and then select "Yes" for "Do you want to hide the header/footer for this page?"

If you have selected "No" for the first question, you'll see a second question just below that says "Do you want to hide the complete menu on this page or just some specific tab(s)?"

Ģ If you select "Complete", only your business logo will be displayed in the header/footer.
Ģ If you select "Specific", you can decide which menu items you do not want to be displayed by checking the box next to the page name.
Once saved, the header/footer will only display the pages that have not been checked.
Ģ If you select "None", all of your linked pages will be displayed in the header/footer.

Once you have finished making your selections, click the blue "Save" button to update your header and/or footer preferences for the page.

To push your updates live, please remember to click the blue "Save & Publish" button at the top-right corner of the page.



Enable/disable website password protection
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/enable-disable-website-password-protection

Enable/disable website password protection
Learn how to enable or disable the password protection feature which requires visitors to enter a password before they can view or use your website.

Last updated on 13 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Understand how the password protection feature works
Ģ Learn how to enable or disable password protection for your website

How does the password protection feature work?
The password protection feature allows you to require visitors to enter a password before they can view/use your BookingKoala website. Password protection is a helpful feature if you are still setting up your website or are in the process of migrating from another platform to BookingKoala.

When enabled, this feature ensures that pages linked to your domain cannot be viewed without first providing the site's password. The admin can share the password with developers, staff, and any other users who need to view/utilize the site while it is protected from the general public. Once the site is ready to be published, password protection can be easily disabled.

Enable and Disable Password Protection
To enable password protection for your BookingKoala site, start by going to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

On this page, click the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website and theme builder.

Once you have logged into the website builder, click on the "Settings" button near the top left side of the page.

A menu will appear on the left side of the page. Scroll down the "General" tab until you see "Enable password protect?"

Ģ Toggle the slider to "Yes" (green) if you would like to activate password protection.
? If you are enabling password protection, enter the password that will be required to access your website.
? There is also a box where you can add a "message for visitors". This can include instructions and/or an explanation as to why the site is currently password-protected.
Ģ If you currently have password protection enabled, you can disable it by switching the toggle button back to "No".

Once you are finished making any changes, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the side menu. Finally, be sure to click the "Save & Publish" button in the top right corner of the website builder to push your changes live.



Edit your website's SEO metadata
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-your-website-s-seo-metadata

Edit your website's SEO metadata
Learn more about how to add or change the title tag, meta description, and keywords associated with each page in your BookingKoala website.

Last updated on 12 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ What are SEO details?
Ģ How to edit your site's metadata, including the title tag, meta description, and keywords for each page

What is SEO?
"SEO" stands for search engine optimization. This process aims to lead more traffic to your website via search engines like Google, Bing, etc. Adding a title tag, meta description, and keywords for each page correlates with how your site is indexed and ranked by these search engines.

Ģ Title tag: The title tag is displayed as the site's headline in search engine results and is also displayed as the page title at the top of your browser window.
Ģ Meta description: The description is displayed below the website's headline in search engine results and serves as a preview of what the page contains/offers to visitors.
Ģ Keywords: Adding keywords to your metadata may help rank your site and ensure the page is indexed with the correct search results.

Below is an example of the title and description of a website's page on Google.
[Image]

Your site's metadata must be relevant and updated regularly. If you use the website that comes with your BookingKoala account, you can use the website and theme builder to add this data to each page.

Adding and Editing Metadata
To begin adding or updating your site's metadata, please go to:
Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Click on the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website and theme editor.
[Image]

Once inside the website and theme builder, click on the "Page" menu at the top center of the screen. Select "Manage Pages" from the drop-down menu.
[Image]

Click on the "three dots" icon to the right of each page name and select "SEO Basics".
[Image]

This will open a second section from the menu displaying the SEO Basics tab.
[Image]

584 Preview on Google shows how the page will look on a search engine results page.
585 The URL slug is the last part of a URL and refers to a specific page.
? For example, the slug in the example above is "gift-card".
586 Enter the page's title tag in the field below.
[Image]
587 Enter the page's meta description in the text box below.
588 Enter any keywords you would like associated with this page.
? Keywords are often short, common search phrases related to your business or website. Many online tools help with finding the best keywords for your page.
589 Toggle this option to "Yes" if you would like search engines to index (include) this page.

Finally, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of this section to update the metadata and SEO preferences for the page.

Once you are finished making any/all changes to the website, you should also click the "Save & Publish" button in the top right corner of the page. This will ensure that your changes will not be lost in case you navigate away from the editor.

Please note: It can several weeks for your site to be reindexed by search engines. Click here to learn more about how long it takes for Google and other search engines to update and how to reindex your site.



How to fix the "website is not secure"/"connection is not private" error
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-fix-the-not-secure-connection-is-not-private-website-error

How to fix the "website is not secure"/"connection is not private" error
Learn why your website is showing the "not secure" error or "connection not private" page and how to correct it.

Last updated on 13 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ Why the "not secure" or "connection is not private" error is displaying for your website
Ģ How to update your site's SSL certificate to secure the website and remove the error message

What is the "not secure"/"your connection is not private" error?
The " not secure "/' your connection is not private " website error varies depending on the browser, but usually contains one or more of the following elements:

Ģ A " Not Secure " tag is in the address box just before the domain name.
Ģ An exclamation point in the triangle icon by the address box or on the page.
Ģ The " https " part of the domain is crossed out or written in red
Ģ The page is blocked by a " Connection is not private " message

If your site is displaying the " not secure " error or has a " connection is not secure " page instead, the most common cause is an expired, inaccurate, or missing SSL certificate.  A Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) certificate is part of a security protocol to authenticate your site, create a secure connection to protect your user's information, and prevent someone from creating a fake version of your site.

An invalid SSL certificate and the resulting issues normally occur after connecting a custom domain to your BookingKoala account. Click here to learn more about how to connect custom domains.

When a custom domain is connected and hosted by BookingKoala, the new server details may not match the original server that issues the site's SSL certificate. For the site to be secured, the details on the existing certification must be updated to match the current host.

How do I update my site's SSL certificate?
To fix this issue, please contact BookingKoala Support at support@bookingkoala.com so our developers can install/update the SSL certificate for your site. You do not need to request or purchase a SSL certificate from your domain host as BookingKoala provides this service for free.

When you email our team, please include the following details so we can help you more quickly:

Ģ Your BookingKoala account domain

? Ex: yourstorename.bookingkoala.com
Ģ Any custom domain(s) connected to your account

? Ex: yourstorename.com, bookings.yourstorename.com, www.yourstorename.com

As soon as we receive your request, we will forward it to our developers for processing. Please allow up to 48 hours for the SSL certificate to be updated.  Our team will follow up with you to confirm as soon as the installation is complete.



Buy a BookingKoala domain
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/buy-a-bookingkoala-domain

Buy a BookingKoala domain
Learn where to buy a BookingKoala domain and how to connect it to your account.

Last updated on 14 Nov, 2024

In this article, you will learn more about how to:

Ģ Buy a BookingKoala domain
Ģ Connect the domain to your BookingKoala account

Why would I purchase a custom domain?
There are many reasons why you may want to purchase a new domain for your BookingKoala account and website. After all, using the right domain name has a major impact on how customers find your website and access the services you offer.

If you are using the BookingKoala website, you may not want customers to see BookingKoala branding including the ".bookingkoala" part of your default account domain.

If you have a pre-existing website with embedded BookingKoala forms, you may want to create and connect a domain to hide/"mask" your default BookingKoala subdomain so users won't see "yourstorename.bookingkoala.com" when they log into their dashboard (hosted by BookingKoala) from your site (hosted elsewhere).

Or, if your business is rebranding , you may have purchased a new domain to reflect the new services or industries you are incorporating.

Whatever the case may be, you can connect and manage one or more custom domains for your BookingKoala account using the " Change My Domain " option from the Design Forms & Website section.

Purchasing a BookingKoala Domain
If you do not own a custom domain yet and would like to purchase one through BookingKoala, you can easily buy and connect the domain right from inside your account.

If you already own a domain that is hosted by another platform, click here to view instructions on how to connect it to your BookingKoala account.

Buy a Custom BookingKoala Domain

To buy a custom BookingKoala domain, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Change Your Domain

Your default primary domain will be listed under the " Domain Name " column. This is the BookingKoala subdomain that was generated when you first created your account.

If you would like to buy a new domain through BookingKoala, click the green " Buy New Domain " button in the top right corner of the page.

Type your desired domain name WITHOUT the "www." into the box at the top of the page. The bottom half of the page will display results and availability for that name, including alternative extensions .

To view additional extensions, click the blue " Load More " button.

Once you've decided which domain to select, click the green " Buy " button to the right of the name. This will take you to the Add Payment Information screen.

You can use your " Existing credit card " or select " New credit card " to add a new card for payment. Then, click the blue " Buy " button to complete your purchase.

Verify the Domain

Next, you'll need to verify your domain. Go back to the main Domain dashboard by clicking the grey " <- Domains " button at the top left side of the page.

You will see your recently purchased domain has been added to the list. Click the orange " Verify " button to verify the new domain.

This will bring you to a " Verify Domain " page. Click the blue " Verify " button.

You may see this red message in the top right corner of the page when you try to verify your domain.

There is no reason for alarm if so; it can take up to 12 hours for your domain verification process to be completed. Please wait and then check back again to try again. Once verified, the status will read as " Connected ".

If you are still seeing this error message after 12 hours, please contact our support team at support@bookingkoala.com so we can assist you.

Setting the Primary Domain

Once the domain has been verified, you can set it as your primary domain. This is important and ensures your system email and SMS notifications contain the domain you want customers to see.

To the right of the new domain, click " Options ", the " Make Primary ".

A pop-up window will appear, asking if you are sure you want to make this your primary domain. Click " Yes " to continue.

Your new domain will now have the blue " Primary " label next to it.

Your new domain has now been successfully connected to your account! To view what customers will see when they type in the domain, click the eye icon /" View " button.

Click the blue " Copy Link " button and then paste it into a private/incognito browser to see what your customers see when they navigate to your website.

Next Steps
Once you have connected a new primary domain for your BookingKoala account and website, you may need to take a few additional steps to ensure your account will work as expected.

Update Your Form(s) Embed Codes

If you have an existing website that has BookingKoala forms embedded, you will need to update your forms' embed codes to the new domain instead of the original BookingKoala domain.

590 Check that your newly connected custom domain has been set as the primary domain for your BookingKoala account. If it is not, please follow the instructions in the previous section to complete this step.
591 Log out of your admin dashboard.
592 Type the new domain into the address bar in your web browser then add "/login" to the end.
? For example, if your custom domain is " demo2llc.store ", the login URL would be " demo2llc.store/login "
593 Enter your admin or staff credentials into the form and click "Log In".
594 Follow the rest of the steps from our guide on how to embed forms on an existing website. As long as you have logged in from your new domain, the embed codes will also contain the new domain.

Secure & Authenticate Your Website

Once you connect a new domain, your website's SSL (Secure Sockets Layer) certificate may need to be updated to reflect the change. A valid SSL certificate helps show customers that your website is authentic, secure, and owned by your business.

If your domain is no longer secure due to an expired or inaccurate SSL, you may see the following indicators:

Ģ A " Not Secure " tag is shown in the address box just before the domain name.
Ģ An exclamation point in the triangle icon by the address box or on the page.
Ģ The " https " part of the domain is crossed out or written in red
Ģ The page is blocked by a " Connection is not private " message

To fix this, please contact BookingKoala Support at support@bookingkoala.com so our developers can install/update the SSL certificate for your site.

When you email our team, please include the following details so we can help you more quickly:

Ģ Your BookingKoala account domain
? Ex: yourstorename.bookingkoala.com
Ģ Any custom domain(s) connected to your account
? Ex: yourstorename.com, bookings.yourstorename.com, www.yourstorename.com

As soon as we receive your request, we will forward it to our developers for processing. Please allow up to 48 hours for the SSL certificate to be updated. Our team will follow up with you to confirm as soon as the installation is complete.

Secure & Authenticate Your Emails
When you connect a new domain to your site, it may require you to update your site's DNS records to prevent your emails from going into spam as well as prevent email spoofing (when people use what appears to be your email address for illegitimate reasons). These cases can be addressed by adding or updating your DNS's SPF and CNAME files.

Add/Update an SPF Record

A Sender Policy Framework , or " SPF " for short, is an email validation standard designed to prevent email spoofing. To set this up, you will publish a TXT record to the DNS configuration of your domain. This process varies depending on which web host you use.

If your domain doesn't have an existing SPF record, you can publish a new TXT record in your DNS Records.

595 For the record type , select TXT ( SPF is a type of TXT record).
596 For the name, enter " @ " or leave it blank (this depends on your domain host).
597 For the value , enter " v=spf1 include:amazonses.com ~all "
597.1 If you would like to authenticate emails from multiple domains, you can enter each domain separated by a space
597.2 For example: " v=spf1 include:demo2llc.com include:amazonses.com ~all "

Once the record has been updated/added and saved, it can take up to 48 hours to update globally.

Configure Your DKIM Settings

If you are using a domain email address, you can configure your DNS settings to help with verifying your sender email address. This makes it less likely for the email to be marked as "spam". If you would like to set up your DKIM settings, please follow the steps below.

598 Email us at support@bookingkoala.com to let us know if you would like to generate a CNAME file so you can configure your DKIM.
599 One of our developers will generate the records and send you a .CSV file containing CNAME records.
600 Download and open the .CSV file.
601 Login to your domain's hosting dashboard and locate the section where you can edit your DNS.
602 Copy the DNS records from the .CSV file you downloaded and add them to your domain's DNS settings.
603 Click "Save" or "Update" (this will vary depending on your domain registrar).
604 Once the settings are populated, any further emails being sent out will not go into spam unless an email from your site is manually "spammed" by the client.

Please note: It normally takes anywhere from 4 to 6 hours up to 72 hours for the DKIM settings to become verified.



Terms & conditions checkbox
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/terms-conditions-checkbox

Terms & conditions checkbox
Learn how to enable and require customers and/or admin to agree to the booking terms & conditions prior to scheduling or requesting a service.

Last updated on 13 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ What is the "terms & conditions" feature?
Ģ How to enable the terms & conditions feature
Ģ How to require customers and/or admin/staff to accept the terms & conditions before booking
Ģ How to check if the "Terms & Conditions" checkbox was checked or not checked

What is the booking form "Terms & Conditions" feature?
When enabled, the "Terms & Conditions" feature places a checkbox just above the "Save Booking" button on the booking forms.

This feature can be displayed to customers and/or the admin/staff. It is also possible to require customers and/or admin/staff to check the box and agree to the terms and conditions of your services before scheduling or requesting an appointment.

Ģ If the feature is required, users will see a red error message at the top of the page reminding them to accept the terms and conditions.
Ģ A red error message will also be displayed below the "Terms & Conditions" checkbox and text.

Once the user checks the "Terms & Conditions" box, they will be able to use the "Save Booking" button to submit the booking.

Once the booking has been saved, customers will see a green "Thank you for booking with us!" alert at the top of the page.

Enable Terms & Conditions
To enable, disable, or customize the "Terms & Conditions" feature for your booking form(s), you will first need to open the Website & Theme Builder.

Please go to:
Settings > Design Forms and Website > Website Builder and Themes

Click on the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website builder.

Once the advanced theme builder has launched, go to the booking form on the ōBook Nowö page using the top center "Page" menu. Select an industry and form number if you have more than one, as different Terms & Conditions can be set up per form.

Scroll down to the ōTerms and Conditionsö section and click on the ōTerms and Conditionsö text to reveal an ōEditö button. Click ōEditö to open the ōTerms and Conditionsö menu from the left side of the page.

Ģ There is an option to ōShow / Hideö the ōTerms and Conditionsö checkbox.
Ģ There is also an option to ōEditö the text that appears by the checkbox if it is set to ōShowö.
Ģ Next, select which user(s) should see the ōTerms & Conditionsö feature ("Customers" and/or "Admin/Staff") Below, select which user(s) are required to accept the ōTerms & Conditionsö checkbox feature ("Customers" and/or "Admin/Staff").
Ģ There is also an option to ōEditö the ōError Messageö text which will display if the user forgets to accept the ōTerms and Conditionsö before submitting the booking.
Ģ The message will only be displayed to the user(s) who are required to accept the ōTerms & Conditionsö via this feature.
Ģ Finally, select which form(s) to apply these ōTerms and Conditionsö settings.
? Select ōJust this formö to only apply these terms to the current form you are editing.
? Select ōAll forms in the systemö to apply these terms to all forms and industries in the system.

Once you are finished making your selections from the "Terms & Conditions" side menu, click the blue "Save" Button at the bottom of the page.

Remember to click the blue "Save & Publish" button to push the changes live to your website or forms.

Terms & Conditions Logs
If customers are not required to accept the booking "Terms & Conditions", you can still check if they accepted the terms before submitting their booking.

First, please go to:
Logs > System Logs

Search for the job "New Booking" log by using the filters along the top of the section, or searching the customer's name, email address, phone number, address, or booking ID in the search bar.

Once the log has been located, click the eye icon/"View" button to view the log's details.

At the bottom of the log details section, there will be a line called "Terms & Conditions (checkbox)".

Ģ If the data to the right of this says "Checked", the user accepted the terms and conditions using the "Terms & Conditions" checkbox before submitting the booking.
Ģ If the data to the right of this says "Unchecked", the user declined to check the "Terms & Conditions" checkbox before booking.



Change the layout of the "Customer Details" and address section

What is the "Customer Details" section?
The "Customer Details" section is located on the booking form and is used to collect the customer's name, contact information, and address as part of the booking process.

Edit the Customer Details Section
To change the appearance of the "Customer Details" section, please go to: Settings > Design Forms and Website > Website Builder and Themes

Click on the orange "Customize Theme" button to open the website builder.

Click on the "Page" menu at the top center of the screen and select the "Book Now" page.

Next, scroll down to the Customer Details section.

Change the Customer Details Layout & Address Format To change the layout and address format, click the Customer Details section to reveal a ōDesignö button near the top left corner of the section.

Click on the Design button to reveal a menu on the left side of the page.

There are four layouts available to choose from:
Ģ Customer details and the short address format as a single section
Ģ Customer details and the long address format as a single section
Ģ Customer details and the short address format as separate sections
Ģ Customer details and the long address format as separate sections

Customer details and the short address format as a single sectionThe customer contact information and short-format address fields are displayed as a single section.
The short-format address consists of asingle line for the address with a second field for an optional unit/apartment/floor number.

Customer details and the long address format as a single sectionThe customer contact information and long-format address fields are displayed as a single section.
The long-format address consists ofmultiple fields for each aspect of the address:
Ģ Address
Ģ Apt. No.
Ģ City
Ģ State
Ģ Zipcode/Postal code

Customer details and the short address format as separate sections The customer contact information and short-format address fields are displayed as separate sections: "Customer Details" and "Address Details".
This allows the admin to place the sections separately from each other when using the "Rearrange" button.
The short-format address section consists of a single line for the address with a second field for an optional unit/apartment/floor number.

Customer details and the long address format as separate sections The customer contact information and long-format address fields are displayed as separate sections: "Customer Details" and "Address Details".
This option also the admin to place the sections separately from each other when using the "Rearrange" button.
The long-format address consists ofmultiple fields for each aspect of the address:
Ģ Address
Ģ Apt. No.
Ģ City
Ģ State
Ģ Zipcode/Postal code

Final Steps After you have clicked "Select This Layout", the text will change to "Selected". You can now close out the side menu and proceed with any other changes you'd like to make.

Once you are finished editing the booking form, please remember to click the blue "Save & Publish" button to push the update live to your site and booking form.

Edit the customer invoice payment page
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-customer-invoice-payment-page

Edit the customer invoice payment page
Learn how to edit and set up your customer invoice payment page in the Website & Theme Builder.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to edit the Invoice Payment page

Getting Started
The Invoice Payment page is displayed when the customer clicks the Pay Invoice button at the bottom of their invoice.

To modify the appearance of this page, you must access the BookingKoala Website & Theme Builder. To do so, please go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Edit the Invoice Payment Page
To edit this page, select the top center Page menu, select Payment Details , and click on Invoice Payment.

The right side of the page will display the payment summary section for the customer. To edit this section, click on each label to reveal the Edit button.

On the main section of the page, the page title and description can be edited, shown, or hidden by clicking on the text.

Below, there are several different sections depending on the payment being made.

The topmost section is only displayed if Stripe is your payment processor and you have enabled Stripe for multiple locations . In this case, the customer will select which location they are adding a card for prior to entering their card information.

The next section is displayed if the customer has a card on file already and selects the Existing Credit Card option from the invoice form.

In case you use the authorized.net payment gateway or if you have enabled the option " Do you want to add billing address with the card? " under the store option settings of the admin payment processor section (Settings > General > Store Options > Admin) , an address section will appear with the payment fields.

The following section is displayed if the customer does not already have a card on file or selects the New Credit Card option from the invoice form.

At the bottom of the page, there is a Back button to direct the client back to their invoice, and a Pay Now button to submit their payment. The Pay Now button is linked to the Invoice Thank You page discussed in the following section.

Save & Publish
When you are finished making edits to your page, please be sure to click the " Save " button to save your changes to the backend, or " Save & Publish " to push your changes live to your site.



Edit the customer invoice thank you page
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-customer-invoice-thank-you-page

Edit the customer invoice thank you page
Learn how to edit and set up your customer invoice thank you page in the Website & Theme Builder.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to edit the Invoice Thank You page

Getting Started
The customer Invoice Thank You page is displayed after the customer submits payment by clicking the Pay Now button on the Invoice Payment page.

To modify the appearance of this page, you must access the BookingKoala Website & Theme Builder. To do so, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme.

Edit the Invoice Thank You Page
To edit this page, select the top center Page menu, select Payment Details, and click on Invoice Thank You.

The Thank You page contains several optional elements that can be shown, hidden, or edited depending on your preference. To make changes, click each element to reveal the Edit buttons.

There is an optional button at the bottom of the page that when shown is linked to the home page of your website. This can be modified and switched to any other page such as the Book Now page.

Save & Publish
When you are finished making edits to your page, please be sure to click the "Save" button to save your changes to the backend, or "Save & Publish" to push your changes live to your site.



Set up the default invoice template
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-customer-invoice-page

Set up the default invoice template
Learn how to edit and set up your customer invoice page/template in the Website & Theme Builder.

Last updated on 20 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ How to edit the Invoice template via the customer invoice page

Getting Started
The customer Invoice page displays the customer's invoice and an optional contract pop-up to collect and verify the customer's signature upon reviewing and paying the invoice.  This page is displayed when a customer is sent an invoice link as detailed in this article.

To modify the appearance and features used on this page, you must access the BookingKoala Website & Theme Builder.  To do so, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Edit the Invoice Template
To edit the Invoice page, select the top center Page menu, select Payment Details , and click on Invoice.

To add a logo to the invoice, click on the Your Logo text in the top left corner of the invoice to reveal the Edit button.  Optionally, the logo can be clickable and redirect users to a specific page on your website.

The topmost section of the invoice contains dynamic shortcodes that display your business's store name and support email .  Like other elements, these can be edited by clicking on the text to reveal the edit button.

Other sections, like the company address , invoice number, issued date, due date, and billed-to sections, can be shown or hidden depending on what you would like customers to see.

Below, two different tables represent the type of details displayed for each invoice.  The topmost table is used in the case of creating a custom invoice with customer table headers.

To modify this section, click on the table to reveal the Edit , Add Row , Add Column , and Rearrange buttons.  Use the trash can icon to delete any rows or columns that you do not want to use.

It is also possible to add multiple tables for the custom invoice by clicking on the topmost table to reveal the Add Table button.  Once more than one table has been added, a Rearrange button will appear allowing you to modify the order of the tables as they are displayed on custom invoices.

The table below the custom table(s) is used for invoices that are generated from bookings . The content of this table varies from merchant to merchant and depends upon the parameters selected when creating the booking invoice as detailed in this article.  Only the Tip text can be edited from the website builder.

Below the custom and from booking tables are options for the subtotal , discount , and total tax . These can be edited or removed based on your preference.  The total amount text can also be edited.

Below this section is the note section.  This section appears as a text box to the admin and allows them to add an optional note for the customer at the bottom of the invoice. Click on the note box to reveal the edit option to alter the title of this section.

Another optional feature is the customer contract pop-up .  This section allows you to create a pop-up that displays

This option can be enabled on the invoice from the contract popup settings when you answer " Yes " to " Do you want to attach a signature to the invoice PDF? " by clicking on the section and selecting Edit.

If you want the contract to pop up before the customer pays the invoice, select Yes to the topmost question.  From here, you can decide if you'd like to collect a signature from the pop-up, if you would like the customer to authenticate their signature by entering a code, and if the signature will be attached to the invoice PDF.

Use the blue Preview button to view what the contract pop-up will look like based on your answers to each setting.

Below the Pay Invoice button, you will see a preview of the log(s) the customer will see on the invoice once they have completed the payment process.

To edit the log sections, click to reveal the Edit button, then click Edit to modify the section titles.

Along the upper right side of the invoice, you can view the payment summary section.  This section displays the payment options that you have enabled during the invoice creation process as detailed in this article.

If you have enabled the partial/advance payment option, customers can select whether they are paying the full invoice or the advance portion.  You can edit these labels by clicking on the section to reveal the Edit button.

If you have enabled the customer tip payment option, the customer will see an Add Tip section just above the payment summary.  The text in this section can also be edited using the Edit option.

Finally, the customer's invoice will display a summary of the charge.  Each of these labels can be edited by clicking on the label and selecting the Edit button.

Save & Publish
When you are finished making edits to your page, please click the " Save " button to save your changes to the backend, or " Save & Publish " to push your changes live to your default invoice template.



Edit the reauth card page
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-reauth-card-page

Edit the reauth card page
Learn how to edit and set up your customer reauth page in the Website & Theme Builder.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to edit the Reauth Card page

Getting Started
The customer Reauth Card page is displayed when the 3DS option is enabled on your account in store option settings and a card payment link is sent to the customer to reauthorize an existing card or add a new card as detailed in this article.

To modify the appearance of this page, you must access the BookingKoala Website & Theme Builder. To do so, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme.

Edit the Reauth Card Page
As mentioned above, the Reauth Card page is displayed when the 3DS option is enabled on your account in store option settings and a card payment link is sent to the customer to reauthorize an existing card or add a new card.

To edit this page, select the top center Page menu, select Payment Details, and click on Reauth Card.

There are two sections on this page: the reauthorization form on the top and the credit card authentication form for new credit cards below.

Edit the Reauthorization Form

To edit the credit card reauthorization form, click on the title, the description, or the "Authorize Card" button to reveal the edit button.

There are two dynamic values used in the title and description: one for the CVV/CVC and one for the add new card link. In this case, wherever you place {{.cvvText}}, the text will be dynamically generated based on your payment processor. For example, "CVC" will be used for Stripe, and "CVV" will be used for the rest. Use {{.newCardText}} to display the "add new card" link.

You can also decide which page the "Authorize Card" button redirects users to after successful completion of the card authorization.

Edit the New Card Authentication Form

The second half of the page displays the authentication form for new credit cards.

There is also a dynamic value used in the description to display the CVC/CVV wherever you place {{.cvvText}}.

In case you use the authorized.net payment gateway or if you have enabled the option "Do you want to add billing address with the card?" under the store option settings of the admin payment processor section (Settings > General > Store Options > Admin), an address section will appear with the payment fields.

Like above, you can also decide which page the "Authorize Card" button redirects users to after successful completion of the card authentication.

Save & Publish
When you are finished making edits to your page, please be sure to click the "Save" button to save your changes to the backend, or "Save & Publish" to push your changes live to your site.



Edit the customer dashboard page
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-customer-dashboard-page

Edit the customer dashboard page
Learn how to edit and set up your customers' dashboard page in the Website & Theme Builder.

Last updated on 06 Jun, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to locate the customer dashboard pages in the website builder
Ģ How to edit the customer Dashboard page

Getting Started
Whenever you book or create a customer profile in BookingKoala, that customer gains access to a customer dashboard on your website.

To modify the appearance and enable/disable some of the features used on these pages, you must access the BookingKoala Website & Theme Builder. To do so, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Edit the Customer Dashboard Page
To edit the customer dashboard page, click on the top center Pages menu, select Customer Dashboard Pages, and click on the Dashboard page.

Background Color & Padding

To change the background color of the customer dashboard, click on the background and select the white Edit button that appears in the top left corner of the section.

Click the box to the right of where it says Background color and use the color popup to select a color.

To adjust the padding on each side of the customer dashboard, enter a number for the number of pixels you would like between the edge of the page and the page content.

Customer Details Box Colors, Font & Buttons

To edit the background color of the customer details box, click the background or element number on the upper right corner of the box and then select the Edit button.

Click the box to the right of where it says Background color and use the color popup to select a color.

The text color and font size within this section can also be changed by clicking on the customer name, email address, and/or phone number and selecting another color from the color popup and font size menu.

The button color, style, and text content can be altered by clicking on a button and selecting the Edit button that appears.

Ģ Click on the Text Settings section to alter the text displayed on the button.
Ģ Click on the Style Settings section to alter the colors of the button's background, border, text color, and font size.
Ģ Click on the Design button to change the button's shape/style.

Text and buttons in other sections of the customer dashboard can be edited in the same manner.

If the customer has upcoming appointments, previous appointments, canceled appointments, and/or declined payments, they will see the following section on their dashboard.

To edit the text, colors, and buttons for these sections, click anywhere within the section to reveal the Edit button in the top left corner of the section.

Each tab has its own settings and style tabs which control the text color, font size, button color, button style, and tab colors.

Save & Publish
When you are finished making edits to your page, please be sure to click the "Save" button to save your changes to the backend, or "Save & Publish" to push your changes live to your site.

Edit the customer add payment page
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-customer-add-payment-page

Edit the customer add payment page
Learn how to edit and set up your customer add payment page in the Website & Theme Builder.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to edit the Add Payment page

Getting Started
The Add Payment page is displayed when a customer clicks the "Add New Card" button on the "Your Info & Billing" page in the customer dashboard.

To modify the appearance of this page, you must access the BookingKoala Website & Theme Builder. To do so, please go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Edit the Invoice Payment Page
To edit this page, select the top center Page menu, select Payment Details, and click on Add Payment.

The topmost section displays the page title which can be edited, shown, or hidden by clicking on the text.

The next section is only displayed if Stripe is your payment processor and you have enabled Stripe for multiple locations. In this case, the customer will select which location they are adding a card for prior to entering their card information.

Below is the Add a New Card field which can have the text edited and color changed.

The following options are displayed if you have selected "Give Options" for the setting, "When a customer adds a card, do you want to give them options or add the card to all "cash/check" bookings?" This setting is located under: Settings > General > Store Options > Customer > General

To change the questions and choices, click on the text and select the Edit button that appears on the top left corner of the section.

At the bottom of the page, there is a Cancel button to direct the client back to their billing section, and an Add Card button to save their new payment method. The Add Card button also redirects back to the customer dashboard's Your Info & Billing page.

Save & Publish
When you are finished making edits to your page, please be sure to click the "Save" button to save your changes to the backend, or "Save & Publish" to push your changes live to your site.



Edit the booking form booking summary
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-booking-form-booking-summary

Edit the booking form booking summary
Learn how to manage the appearance of the booking summary in the booking form.

Last updated on 29 Aug, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to go to edit the booking summary
Ģ How to show or hide booking summary aspects
Ģ How to display extras with and without pricing
Ģ How to display exclude parameters with and without pricing

The Booking Summary
The booking form's booking summary is what you and your customers see when filling out the form. It summarizes the basic details of the booking as well as the pricing for the service.

The booking summary's appearance can be edited in the website and theme builder. To begin, please go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Once you are inside the website builder, click on the top center Page menu and select the Book Now page.

Scroll down the page until you see the Booking Summary on the right side of the page.

Make the Booking Summary Scroll
To make the booking summary scroll on the customer end, please click here (link to another article).

Remove "Powered by BookingKoala" Branding
If you are a Growing or Premium subscriber, you can remove the BookingKoala branding from the booking summary. Please click here for our guide.

Show or Hide Booking Summary Labels
To show or hide the booking summary labels, click on the booking summary above the labels and select the Edit button. Then, scroll down the left side menu and click the Show/Hide buttons as needed.

Show or Hide the Price of Extras in the Booking Summary
Once you click to Edit the Booking Summary, the left-side menu will appear with options to adjust the Settings. Scroll down until you locate the "Choose layout" sections just below the Extras label.

Click on whichever option you prefer: extras without pricing, or extras with pricing.

Show or Hide the Price of Exclusions in the Booking Summary
Similarly, if you scroll down until you locate the "Choose layout" sections just below the Excludes label, you can decide whether you would like to display your exclusion parameters with or without pricing.

Show or Hide the Professionals Label
Continue to scroll down the left side menu and you will find the option to Show or Hide the "Professionals" label. This label shows how many service providers are scheduled to do the job.

In addition to the show/hide option, there is a Control Visibility button. The Control Visibility button opens a pop-up that allows you to decide which parties can see this information: customers, admin/staff, or both.

Show or Hide the Length Label
Similarly, the Length label can be shown or hidden and also has a Control Visibility button to manage which parties can see the booking time length.

Change the Booking Summary Font/Colors
To change the colors, font weight, and font size of the Booking Summary, click on the Style tab at the top of the left-side menu.

Rearrange the Order of the Booking Summary
To rearrange the order of the labels in the Booking Summary, click the Rearrange tab at the top of the left-side menu, or click the Rearrange button to open the Rearrange tab.

Then, drag and drop the labels into the desired order and click the blue "Save" button.

Edit the "Total" Summary
Just like the body of the main booking summary, you also can modify the "Total" section of the Booking Summary.

Just like the main Booking Summary, there are three tabs that control the Total Summary.



Edit the "Popular Questions"/FAQs section
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-faqs-section-on-the-booking-page

Edit the "Popular Questions"/FAQs section
Learn how to edit the FAQs/popular questions section on the Book Now page.

Last updated on 25 Nov, 2024

In this article, you will learn how to:
Ģ Edit the FAQs/popular questions section on the booking/"Get a quote" page
Ģ Edit the dynamic FAQs section

Editing the Popular Questions/FAQs Section on the Booking and Lead Form
One of the optional components on the booking form or "Book Now" page is the FAQs/popular questions section. This section can help customers better understand your business and your services. Do you get a lot of common questions about your services? If so, this is the perfect place to address those questions while the customer looks at your pricing and availability.

To edit the FAQs section, you must first open the Website and Theme Builder. To do so, please go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Next, click the orange "Customize Theme" button at the center of the page.

Once the website and theme builder have launched, click on the top-center Page menu and select the "Book Now" page. This is the default name of the booking form page.

Next, scroll down the Book Now page until you locate the FAQs section along the right side of the page. Click on the element number on the top right corner of the section to reveal the Edit button. Click Edit to launch the section editor.

From here, you can edit or delete existing questions, add new questions, and change the priority order of the questions in this section.

Ģ To edit a question, click on the down arrow to reveal the editable fields for the question and answer.
Ģ To delete a question, click on the trash can icon and select "Yes" in the following pop-up.
Ģ To add a new question, click on the blue "Add New" button at the bottom of the side menu and then enter a question and answer into the fields.
Ģ To change the priority order of the questions, drag and drop a question by holding the sandwich icon and then click the green "Update Priority" button at the bottom of the side menu.

Once you are finished making your changes, you can click the red X icon to close the side menu. Don't forget to click the Save & Publish button in the top right corner of the page so your changes are pushed live to your booking form.

Editing the Dynamic FAQs Section
If you have added a "FAQs" section from the section menu, there is a separate section dedicated to creating a more complex question-and-answer page. To add this section to your BookingKoala website, please go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Select whichever page you would like to add the FAQ section to from the top-center Page menu.
Ģ If you'd like to create a designated FAQs page, click here to learn how to add a new page to your site.
Ģ Click here to learn how to add a new page to your header or footer.

Add the FAQ Section To add the FAQ section to your page, hover your cursor between two sections (such as the header and footer) and click the "Add Section" button that appears.

Scroll down the left side menu that appears and click on where it says "FAQ". Then click "Add this section".

Once the FAQ section is added, you will see four sections each with several questions and answers listed for each section.

Edit the FAQ Section To edit the dynamic FAQs content, please visit: Settings > Design Forms & Website > FAQ

To edit an existing FAQ, select "Options" under the Actions column, and select "Edit".

From here, you can do the following:
605 Edit the question field.
606 Edit the answer field.
607 Select an existing category.
608 Add a new category.
609 Save your changes.
610 Return to the previous screen.
611 Delete this FAQ entirely.

To add a new FAQ, go to: FAQs > Add FAQ

Be sure to click the "Save" button at the bottom of the page once you have finished creating your FAQ.

You can manage FAQ categories by going to: FAQs > Categories

Here you can Edit or Delete a category by selecting "Options" under the Actions column.

To change the order in which your FAQs appear in the dynamic FAQs section, go to: FAQs > Organize FAQs

Drag and drop the questions into whatever order works best, then click the "Update Order" button at the bottom of the page.



Edit the booking form text and sections
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-the-booking-form-text-and-sections

Edit the booking form text and sections
Learn how to modify the text and format the sections of your booking form.

Last updated on 30 Aug, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to go to edit the text and section layout of the booking form
Ģ How to change the design of the Customer Details section
Ģ How to edit to appearance of buttons
Ģ How to edit the appearance of the booking calendar
Ģ How to enable/disable certain sections of the booking form
Ģ How to add a new section above or below the booking form
Ģ How to add a custom section to the booking form

Editing the Booking Form Text and Sections
The booking form's text and sections can be edited in the Website and Theme Builder. To begin, please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Once you are insight the website builder, click on the top center Page menu and select the Book Now page.

Next, select whether you would like to edit the desktop version of your booking form, or the mobile version. Please note that these two version of the site are edited separately.

Edit the Booking Form Text
To edit any of the headers or text on the booking form, scroll down to that section and click on the text. Two buttons will appear. One button will be to Edit and the other is to Hide/Show.

Once you click Edit, a pop-up will appear that allows you to change the text, add formatting, and adjust the style. Text in the text box can be styled by highlighting it with your cursor and selecting a font, color, format, etc.

Please note that some text, such as the labels in the Customer Details section, cannot be formatted at this time. However, most text fields include a Style Settings section in the pop-up that controls the font color and size.

Change the Design of the Customer Details Section
The Customer Details section is one of the sections on the booking form that has a Design button. This button is used to choose the layout of the customer detail fields. There are 4 options to pick from:

Ģ Layout 1 - Customer and address details are displayed as a single section along with a single address field.
Ģ Layout 2 - Customer and address details are displayed as a single section along with multiple address fields (city, state & zip code).
Ģ Layout 3 - Customer and address details are displayed in two different sections. The address section has a single address field.
Ģ Layout 4 - Customer and address details are displayed in two different sections. The address section has multiple address fields (city, state & zip code).

Please Note: the fields in which the customer's first name, last name, email address(es), and phone number(s) cannot be deleted; however, the secondary email address and phone number fields can be disabled from the front end by going to:

Settings > General > Store Options > Customer tab > Bookings section

Select the "Yes" option for the question "Would you like to disable the secondary email address and phone number on the booking form?".

Formatting Buttons on the Booking Form
Most buttons on the booking form can be modified in some way, whether it is the text, color, or style. To edit a button, click anywhere on the button to reveal the Edit button. Once you click edit, there will be a Text Settings and a Style Settings section that allows you to edit the text and style.

Some edit buttons control the formatting of multiple buttons on the page. For example, the plus/minus buttons for the exclusion parameters and extras will display the same colors and formatting.

Editing the Booking Calendar
To edit the appearance of the booking calendar, click anywhere on the calendar to reveal the Edit button. Click Edit and a Settings popup will open with options to modify almost every aspect of the calendar.

Enable/Disable Optional Sections of the Booking Form
There are several optional sections towards the bottom of the booking form that can be enabled or disabled depending on your preference.

Ģ Extras section
Ģ Exclusion parameters section
Ģ Key Information & Job Notes section
Ģ Special Notes Or Instructions section
Ģ Discount/Gift Card/Referral section
Ģ Tips section
Ģ Parking section
Ģ Reminders section

These sections will have an Enabled/Disabled button near the top right corner of the section. If a section is disabled, any existing bookings that used that section will remain the same, however, all new bookings will have it hidden.

How to Add Another Section to the Booking Form
There are two ways to go about adding a new section to the booking form. The first is to add a new section through the website builder which will appear before or after the booking form. To do so, simply scroll to the top or bottom of the booking form and click on the blue "Add Section" button that appears.

This will open the Add Section side menu where you can click to add one of the many options.

If you are looking to add a new section within the booking form itself, this can be done by adding a custom section. Custom sections are extremely versatile and allow you to create priced or non-priced questions or statements on the booking form.

Custom sections are set up under:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Custom Section

Click here to learn how to set up one or more custom sections for your form.

Next, click on the orange "Customize Theme" button.

Header Logo

Rename "cash/check" or "credit/debit" on the booking form

Rename "cash/check" or "credit/debit" on the booking form
Learn how to rename the payment options on your booking form(s).

Last updated on 20 Nov, 2024

In this article, you will learn how to:
Ģ Rename your payment options, "cash/check" and "credit/debit"

Renaming Your Payment Options
"Cash/check" and "Credit/debit" are the two types of payment options available in the BookingKoala software. "Cash/check" refers to any payment that takes place outside of BookingKoala, while "Credit/debit" refers to payments that a payment processor processes.

To learn more about the payment options available in BookingKoala, click here (https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/payment-processors-overview).

However, you may want to change the names of these options, especially if you do not accept cash/checks as payment and want to put other forms of external payment options like "Zelle/CashApp/Bank Transfer" instead.

Whatever the case may be, you can change the names that appear on your booking form through the Website & Theme Builder. Please go to:

Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes > Customize Theme

Once the web builder loads, clock on the top center Page menu and select the "Book Now" page.

Scroll down the booking page until you reach the Payment Information section. Click on the payment method option you would like to edit and click the Edit button that appears.

Once you've entered the new text, be sure to click the Save & Publish button in the top right corner of the page.

Migrate custom domain to new IP

Change the A records for an existing connected domain or subdomain
If you already have a domain connected with your BookingKoala account, please follow the guidelines to change the A records and verify the domain.

612 Login to your domain's registrar account.
613 Locate and open the domain's DNS settings page to edit them.  You may see a section called "Advanced DNS" or "Manage DNS settings".
614 Remove the A record with IP address 18.188.223.178
615 Now add following two IPs in the A record for the domain or subdomain

616 162.159.141.169
172.66.1.165  

617 Repeat following steps to add both IP addresses in A records.
618 Record Type:

? Enter or select " A " or " A record "
619 Name:

? For primary domains : Enter the @ symbol or leave the section blank if it says to leave the box empty to set up records for the primary domain.
? For other subdomains : Enter the subdomain's name/extension here.

Ģ For example, you would enter the word " bookings " here to create a subdomain called " bookings.yourbusinessname.com ".
620 Data/IP Address :

? Add the IP address
621 TTL :

? If you see this option when setting up the A record,  you use the host's default selection such as " Automatic ", " 3600", or "60 minutes".
Image
622 Click whichever option is offered to "Add", "Update", or "Save" your new/edited A record.
623 (this step is applicable only if you are connecting the main domain but not the subdomain)
Once you have added the A records for the domain, make sure all other A records are removed.
624 Next, you will need to add CNAME for the www subdomain as shown in the below example, make sure that value for CNAME contains your domain only. For example, if your domain is mydomain.com then the value should be mydomain.com.

Image

You are always welcome to contact support@bookingkoala.com and we can help you locate instructions for your registrar.

Once these changes are done, your domain will be ready to verify, please note that it might take upto few hours to update the A records and we recommend you checking them with this tool What's My DNS

Industries and booking forms
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/industries-and-booking-forms

Industries and booking forms
Learn more about the difference between industries and booking forms, and how to find the right booking form for your business.

Last updated on 18 Apr, 2023

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ The difference between are industries and booking forms
Ģ How to select the best booking form for your industry

What are industries and booking forms?
In BookingKoala, an industry represents the type of business you do. When you first create your account, you will select one or more industries from the "Add Industry" page, or can create a custom industry.

On the admin dashboard, you can see which industries you have added by going to Settings > Industries.

For example, the account in the image above has four industries: Home Cleaning, Pet Groomer, Moving Service, and Car Repair.

On the customer-facing end of the website, each industry is represented as a different tab.

For more information on how to add/edit your industries, click here.

Each industry contains one or more booking forms to carry out the process of calculating a quote based on the selections they make. Each booking form represents a different type of pricing structure that a business could use to quote their customers.

If you offer more than one type of pricing structure for your industry, you can add and combine multiple booking forms. This allows a customer to pick which structure or service type works best for them.

For more information on how to add/combine booking forms, click here.

There are five types of booking forms to chose from when you are setting up your industry: Form 1, Form 2, Form 3, Form 4, and Form 5.

Form 1

Form 1 uses variables as the pricing parameters to determine the total cost and length of each booking. A variable is any piece of information associated with a pre-defined price.

For example, the home-cleaning industry often uses bedrooms and bathrooms as variables. When a customer selects how many bedrooms and bathrooms are in their home, their selections will use these variables to determine the total cost of the booking.

To learn how to set up Form 1, click here.

Form 2

Form 2 uses items, packages, and add-ons to determine the booking total. An item represents a different type of category of service. Once the customer selects the item that applies to them, they can view a list of packages to add and schedule. Add-ons are smaller services that can be added to further customize a package.

For example, in the car detailing industry, the items would be the type of car. Once the customer selects their car type, the packages available for their car will display below. The customer can select add-ons for the package, or go back and add more items and packages.

To learn how to set up Form 2, click here.

Form 3

Form 3 uses items and add-ons to determine the cost of a booking. Items represent a category or object receiving the service, and the add-ons are the different services available for purchase.

For example, a pet grooming business may make the type of pet into their items. This means their items would be: cat, small dog, medium dog, large dog, and bird. When the customer selects their pet's species, they will see a list of services and add whichever ones they'd like to book.

To learn how to set up Form 3, click here.

Form 4

Form 4 uses unit structure in the pricing parameters to determine the total cost and length of each booking. Unit structure-based pricing means that the price is set for one unit of measurement, with the total cost depending on how many units the customer has selected.

For example, let's say a cleaning business determines its rates based on total square feet. This means that their unit structure is per a single square foot. When the customer enters the total number of square feet into the box, the system will multiply that number by the unit's price and length information to provide a quote.

To learn how to set up Form 4, click here.

Form 5

Form 5 uses hourly rates to determine the total cost and length of each booking. The system multiplies the number of hours by the rate to provide a quote for the booking.

For example, a cleaning service may provide different hourly rates depending on the type of job or space: residential, commercial, and post-construction. The customer can select whichever category applies to their situation, and then selects how many hours they would like to book. The system will multiple the number of hours by the rate to provide a quote.

To learn how to set up Form 5, click here.

Additional Resources
To view a live form on a site made using our themes, click here.

To view a live form that has been embedded on an existing site, click here.

Please note: When trying to get to the second step of the booking form above, you will be required to enter a zip code that is in this business's service area. Please enter 10001 into the zip code box,

Ģ Full 57 Minute BookingKoala Overview
Ģ Are you struggling to gain new customers?
Ģ Are you struggling to convert visitors into customers?
Ģ Is managing your service getting out of control?
Ģ Struggling with hiring providers?
Ģ Need ideas of what your booking forms could look like?
