If you would like to set up manual pricing and times instead, click the radio button next to "Manual".

Once you select "manual", an extra box for each possible quantity will be added to the Price & Time section below.

Enter the price and time for a quantity of 1 into the first set of boxes, then the pricing/time for 2 of the extras in the second box, etc. until each quantity has a price and time length.

For example, let's say an extra has a maximum quantity of three. The cost for one is $20 and it will take 10 minutes to complete, two extras cost $30 and will take 20 minutes, and three will be $40 and take 30 minutes.

The setup for the prices and times would look like this:
Ģ The first box contains the price and time when the extra is selected just once: inside one extra is an additional $20 and adds 10 minutes.
Ģ The second box contains the pricing and time information for if they increase the quantity to two: $30 extra and 20 minutes added.
Ģ The third box contains the rate and time length for three cats: $40 extra and 30 minutes added.

Once you have entered your pricing and timing information into the boxes for each quantity, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.

A green system notification will appear in the top-right corner letting you know the extra has been updated successfully.



Offer different services on the customer end
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/offer-differenct-services-on-the-customer-end

Offer different services on the customer end
Learn how to set up services that display for existing customers or can only be booked by admin/staff.

Last updated on 02 May, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to offer services to existing customers only
Ģ How to create admin-only service categories

Service Category Display Settings
Whenever you create a new service category for your booking form, you can decide where that service will be displayed. This allows you to create "secret services" that are only available to different kinds of users.

For example, if you manage a cleaning business, you would likely need a way to schedule special appointments for "re-cleans" or touch-ups of missed areas. However, you probably wouldn't want customers to be able to schedule these types of services on their own.

To achieve this, you can create a "Re-Clean" service category and set it to display only on the admin end.

In another example, let's say you run an organization business. You would like all new customers to schedule a "New Client Home Consult" for their first appointment. Once they've had one appointment with your company, they can schedule an "Existing Client Follow-Up" at a discounted rate. To achieve this, you can create an "Existing Customer" service category that only displays on the customer backend and admin end.

Navigation Path
To create or change the visibility of a service category on the customer front-end, customer-backend, or admin-end, please go to: Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > [Form #] > Service Category

Step-by-Step Instructions
790 Go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > [Form #] > Service Category.
791 If you are setting up a new service category, follow the detailed instructions for setting up service categories.
792 If you are updating an existing category, click "Options" then "Edit" to view its settings.
793 Scroll down to the "Display" section.
794 Select one of the display options (see below).
795 Once you have made the change, click the blue "Save Category" button at the bottom right corner of the page.

Settings and Features Described
In the "Display" section, you can select one of the following options:
Ģ Customer frontend, backend & admin - The service category can be selected by new customers, existing customers, and the admin/staff for booking.
Ģ Customer backend & admin - The service category can only be selected by existing customers or admin/staff and will not display for new customers.
Ģ Admin only - The service category can only be used by admin or staff when making bookings.



How to remove "dependencies" from the booking form
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/removing-dependncy-from-the-booking-form

How to remove "dependencies" from the booking form
Learn how to disable dependencies from sections on the booking form so you can rearrange sections.

Last updated on 10 Sept, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ What are dependencies
Ģ How to remove dependencies from the booking form

What are dependencies?
One of the special features of the booking forms is that you can create " dependencies" .  When you enable a dependency for a component, you can customize how other categories will display when this option is selected.  Simply put, one category's options will depend on the selection(s) in another.

For example, let's say a cleaning business offers residential, commercial, and post-construction services.  Residential and commercial customers can book any frequency they would like, however, post-construction services are always scheduled as one-time appointments.

To ensure that a client cannot book a recurring post-construction service, the company can enable service categories dependencies on frequency. This way, the post-construction service will not display if they have selected a recurring frequency.

One drawback to dependencies is that they must occur in a specific order in the form. For example, if your frequencies depend on your service category, the service category section will have to go before the customer can select a frequency. This is because only certain frequencies are available for some categories.

This can result in a red error message if you are trying to rearrange the sections of your form in the website & theme builder .

If you do not want to use dependencies on your form, this will allow you to reorder the sections of your booking form without constraint.

Click here to learn how to reorder the categories in your booking form.

Remove Dependencies from the Booking Form
To remove dependencies from your form, please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Click " Options ", " Edit " to the right of the form you'd like to update,

Scroll down to the bottom of the Form Settings page until you see the following types of questions:

Ģ Should the frequencies show based on?
Ģ Which option(s) should the service category show for?
Ģ Which option(s) should the variables show for?
Ģ Which option(s) should the exclude parameters show for?
Ģ Which option(s) should the extras show for?

The number of dependencies is controlled by which and many sections you have in your booking form.  For example, form 5 has the following section:

To remove a dependency, uncheck the box next to the category name. To disable all dependencies, make sure all of the boxes are unchecked. Then, click the blue " Save " button to update your form settings.

You will now be able to freely rearrange the sections of your booking form in the website & theme builder according to your liking.



Setting up custom sections for every form
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/add-a-custom-section-for-all-forms

Setting up custom sections for every form

Industries and booking forms

Setting up custom sections for every form

Learn how to add a custom section to any type of form.

Last updated on 17 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:

What is a custom section

How to add a custom section

How to add custom fields

How to reorder your custom sections and fields

How to edit, deactivate, and delete custom sections and fields

Ā

What are custom sections?Ā

Custom sections can be added to your booking form to gather or share specific information not covered in other sections of the form.ĀĀ

In the example above, the "Location Questions" and "Pet Questions" areas on this booking form are custom sections. In these sections, there are several different questions for the customer to select as they fill out the form.

Custom sections can be inserted into almost any step of the booking form.

In the example above, a custom section was used to create "About Our Flat Rate Services".Ā This custom section has been inserted below the Extras section and before the Customer Details section.

Ā

Adding an Individual Custom Field

To create a new custom section field, go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Custom Sections

On the Custom Sections main page, click the blueĀ"Add New" button at the top-right corner of the screen.Ā

Next, selectĀAdd Individual Custom Field.

Ā

You will be redirected to an Add Custom Field page on the Details tab.

Ā

Individual Custom Field Details Tab

Enter the Name of the new field. Check the box next toĀDifferent on customer end if you would like the customer-facing form to say something different.Ā Then, enter the alternative text into the field below.

Next, decide if you would like the custom field to carry a price by checking the box next toĀWould you like to enable pricing for this field?Ā If pricing is enabled, there are several additional settings that can be enabled.ĀĀ

Next, check the box next Would you like to apply tax to this field?ĀIf this has been selected, you will also be asked Where would you like to place this field?

Before the frequency discountĀ-Ā Tax is calculated on thisĀ

After the frequency discount - Tax is calculatedĀ

After the discounted total - Applied after all discounts including the frequency discount

If you select Before the frequency discount or After the frequency discount, you have an additional option,ĀWould you like to include the custom field as a separate charge?ĀĀIf you check the box next to this option, decide if the price will be calculated solely based on time, calculatedĀsolely based on the specified amount, or Both.ĀĀ

Field Types

Next, select the type of field this will be.

Text - Inserts a smaller one-line text box for the customer to respond in.ĀĀ

Text Area - Inserts a large multline text box for the customer to respond in.

Dropdown - Inserts a drop-down menu with one or more options to select.

Checkbox - Inserts one or more options with boxes in which the customer can select one or more options.

Radio Button - Inserts one or more options with radio buttons, in which the customer can select one option only

Date picker - Inserts a calendar box in which the customer can pick one option for the date

Time picker - Inerts a clock box in which the customer can pick one option for the time

Priceable Input - Sets one price that can be edited by the admin/staff at the time of booking.ĀĀ

Quantity Based - Sets a manual or multiply rate for one or more options from a quantity bar.ĀĀ

Note - Add text for the customer to read (no options for responses).ĀĀ

Image - Add an image for the customer to view.

Video - Add a video for the customer to watch.ĀĀ

Add An Option (For Dropdown, Checkbox, and Radio Button Types)

For Dropdown, Checkbox, and Radio Button field types, add one or more options by clicking the blue Add a new option button to the right of the Field Type.

Ā

Enter theĀname of the option and then enter a price in the boxes below (S.A. for service area and M.L. for merchant location/online).Ā You can optionally enable a message to show when the customer selects this option.ĀĀ

If there are multiple fields in the system already, one can set up dependencies from one field on another.

To do so, check the boxes next to the fields you would like to associate with this field's option.

Then click the blueĀAddĀor UpdateĀbutton to save the option to the custom field.ĀĀOnce an option has been added, it can be edited and/or deleted.

Help Text

Add optionalĀHelp Text in the form of aĀtooltip or a textĀdescription.

A tooltip is a symbol that displays a sentence or short paragraph of text when hovered over with one's cursor.ĀĀ

A description is a sentence or short paragraph that adds further clarification to the field, displayed below the main field.

Which customers would you like to apply this field to?

For this option, decide if this new custom field should be applied to all customer's bookings, or only new customer's bookings moving forward.ĀĀ

If you selectĀAll customers,Ādecide if the new custom field will be applied to All bookings, or if you'd like to exclude bookings that have already been charged.

Required

Next, decide if this custom field is required for the customer to complete in order to book a service.Ā If so, check the box next to where it saysĀRequired.Ā If you would like to exclude this requirement for completed and charges bookings, check the box next to where it saysĀExclude required validation on completed and charged bookings?.ĀĀ

Display

Next, decide who you would like to see this field.

Customer frontend, backend & admin - Both new and existing customers as well as the admin and staff can view this field.ĀĀ

Customer backend & admin -Only existing customers and admin/staff will be able to see this field.ĀĀ

Admin only -Ā Only staff and admin will be able to see this field on the booking form.Ā

Provider View and Payment

If you would like to hide this field from the provider(s), check the box next toĀExclude this field from the provider's view.ĀĀ

UnderĀWhat amount would you like to allocate as payment to the provider(s)?, select one of the following options:

FullĀ- The provider will receive the entire payment for this field.Ā For example, if the field costs $40, the provider will have $40 added to their payment.ĀĀ

Partial -The provider will receive a partial payment for this field, either a fixed rate or a percentage-based rate.Ā For example, if it is set to 50% and the field costs $40, the provider will have $20 added to their payment.Ā

None - The provider will not receive any payment related to the customer's payment of this field.Ā If the field costs $40, the provider will receive $0 in payment.ĀĀ

Specify where to apply this custom field.

Decide if this custom field will be applied to all bookings in the case of a recurring series, or if it will only be applied to the first appointment in a recurring series.ĀĀ

Ā

Specify where you would like to include this custom information for your notifications.

Decide if you would like to show the information from your custom section in your notifications to your customers, providers, and admin/staff.Ā

At the bottom of the page, click the blue Next button.ĀĀ

Individual Custom Field Dependencies Tab

The second tab on theĀAdd Custom Field page displays the other sections in your booking form to set up dependencies for when this field is displayed or hidden.ĀĀ

If you have multiple booking forms and would like to add this custom field to any of those forms, selectĀYes to the questionĀWould you like to add this custom field to other booking forms? then check the boxes next to each form you would like to apply this field to.ĀĀ

Below, select which form elements (locations, frequencies, service categories, variables, items, packages, extras, etc.) will display this custom field.Ā For example, the dependencies shown below indicate the field will be shown for all frequencies and service categories but only customers in "The Loop" location.ĀĀ

For the Extras dependencies section, there is an additional feature where you can decide whether the selected parameter should match theĀExact combination (all checked parameters must match the booking exactly),ĀAny combination (at least one of the checked parameters must match the booking), or MixedĀcombination (as long as an unselected option is not selected on the booking, the custom field will be displayed)

For example, in a scenario with 5 parameters, "P1", "P2", "P3", "P4", and "P5", assuming you've selected P1, P2, and P3, here is how each option operates:

Exact:ĀThis option necessitates the selection of all 3 checked parameters (P1, P2, and P3) to display the custom field on the booking form.

Any:ĀWith this option, the custom field will be visible on the booking form if at least one of the checked parameters (P1, P2, or P3) is selected.

Mixed:ĀIn this case, the custom field remains displayed unless any parameter not initially selected (such as P4 or P5) is chosen. If P4 or P5 is selected alongside P1, P2, or P3, the custom field will not appear on the booking form.

Ā

Save

Once you are finished setting up the custom field, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.Ā

Once you do, you will see a pop-up message confirming that your custom field has been added successfully.ĀĀ

If you wish to rearrange the default order in which the custom section appears on the booking form, click the blue "Rearrange" button to open theĀWebsite & Theme BuilderĀin a separate tab.Ā

Otherwise, you can clickĀCloseĀand you will be redirected back to the main Custom Section dashboard.ĀĀ

Ā

Adding Custom Field Groups

Once more than one custom field has been added, they will be grouped together in a custom group.Ā To edit the group name, selectĀOptions below the ActionsĀcolumn and selectĀEdit.ĀĀ

You can also add a brand new group by clicking the blueĀAdd New button and selectingĀAdd Group.Ā

A pop-up will appear where you can enter the name of the the new section and decide if it should be included in any other forms.

Then, click the blue "Save" button to save the group.ĀĀ

Ā

Add Custom Fields to a Specific Group

To see and add more custom fields to a group, click Options below the ActionsĀcolumn and selectĀSee/Add Custom Fields.

It is possible to deactivate custom groups and fields by usingĀOptions thenĀDeactivate,Āor delete is permanently by usingĀOptionsĀthenĀDelete.

Ā

Changing the Custom Fields Order

If you have added multiple custom sections, you can change the order in which they are displayed on your booking form. Go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Custom Sections > Options > Add/View Custom Fields

To change the order, click and hold the field to drag and drop it into a new position in the list.

Once you are satisfied with the order, click the blue "Update Priority" button at the bottom of the page.

A green system notification will appear in the top-right corner of the page, letting you know the priority has been updated successfully.Ā

Ā

Changing the Custom Fields Group Order

If you have multiple fields within a custom section, you can also change the order in which they are displayed in that section. Go to:

Settings > Website Buidler & Themes > Customize Theme> Page menu > Book Now page

Click the greyĀRearrangeĀbutton beneath the form number and industry name you would like to rearrange.

Drag and drop the custom field groups by clicking on their name and placing it in a new order on the list.Ā Then remember to clickĀSave & Publish to update your booking form when you are ready for the changes to go live.

A green system notification will appear in the top-right corner of the page, letting you know the priority has been updated successfully.Ā



Set up the single-step or two-step booking form for admin & staff
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-set-up-the-single-step-vs-two-step-for-admin

Set up the single-step or two-step booking form for admin & staff
Learn how to enable a one-or two-step booking form when making bookings as an admin or staff member.

The purpose of this article is to help you understand how to:

Ģ Enable the single-step vs two-step booking form for staff and admin use

Single Step vs. Two-Step Forms for Admin
As the admin, you have an option to set up your backend booking form in either one or two steps.

When the form is set to be one step , all of the various booking details are located on one page.

If the form is separated into two steps , you and your staff will click " Find Availability " at the bottom of step 1 to proceed with scheduling the service and entering in the rest of the customer's details.

To do this, you will go into your booking form settings. Please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Click " Options ", " Edit " to the right of the form you'd like to update.

The first question in this section says, " Would you like a one-step or two-step booking form as the admin? "

Ģ Select " One Step " to have the booking form appear as a single page on the backend of the site.
Ģ Select " Two-Step " if you'd like to have two pages to navigate between when booking on the backend.

Once you've made your selection, click the blue " Save " button at the bottom right corner of the page.



Enable/disable customers & providers from seeing job length
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/disable-customers-and-providers-from-seeing-job-length

Enable/disable customers & providers from seeing job length
Learn how to configure if/when providers and/or customers see how long a job is estimated to take.

Last updated on 19 Apr, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to enable or disable providers from seeing the job length
Ģ How to enable or disable customers from seeing the job length

Show or Hide the Job Length
When you are setting up your booking form, you can include estimates for how long each service takes. These job length estimates are based on the timing information you have entered when setting up your service categories, pricing parameters, packages, add-ons, or extra services.

However, you may want to modify when your customers or providers are able to view the job length. Some businesses may want to disable job length for a few reasons:
796 You do not want to confuse the customer if the service is expected to take more or less time than the booking form's estimate.
797 You do not want to influence how long the provider thinks they should be working on a job.

Whatever the case may be, you can customize each booking form to display or hide the job length from customers, providers, or both. To begin, please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Next, click "Options", then "Edit" to view your form settings.

Scroll down to the question, "Do you want your customers to see how long the service will take during bookings?"
Ģ Select "Customer front end" if you want new customers to see how long the job will take when filling out the booking form on the front end of the website.
Ģ Select "Customer back end" if you only want existing customers to see how long the job will take on the booking form. This means that a customer must log in to their account and create/edit a booking to view the service length.
Ģ Select "Both" if you would like both new and existing customers to see the job length.
Ģ Select "Off" to hide the job length on the booking form.

Once you've made your selection, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom right side of the page.

You can also control whether customers and providers see the job length inside your service categories. To edit these, please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Service Categories

Click "Options", then "Edit" next to the service category you would like to change.

Scroll down until you see "Display service length on customer end". This allows you to select if/when you would like to display the service length on your booking forms and confirmation emails.

Ģ Customer frontend, backend & admin - The service length will be displayed for potential customers, existing customers, and the admin/staff.
Ģ Customer backend & admin - The service length will only be displayed for existing customers or admin/staff, but will not display for new customers on the front-end.
Ģ Admin only - The time length will only be seen by admin or staff when making bookings.

You also have the option to add a tooltip next to the service length on the customer end of the booking form. This will insert an icon next to the service length in the booking summary box. When the customer hovers over the icon with their curser, your message will be displayed.

Check the box next to "Enable tooltip for service length on customer end" and enter the message in the box below.

You can also decide if you would like to display the service length so providers can see it. To enable this, select "Yes" under "Display service length on provider end". You can also add a tooltip about the service length for the provider's reference.

Once you are finished, click the blue "Save Category" button at the bottom right corner of the page.



Set up the single-step, two-step, or multi-step booking form for customers
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-set-up-the-single-step-two-step-or-multi-step-form-for-customers

Set up the single-step, two-step, or multi-step booking form for customers
Learn how to set up a single-step, two-step, or multi-step booking form for customer use.

Last updated on 14 Apr, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ What are single-step, two-step, and multi-step forms
Ģ How to change which type of form your customers will use when booking services

Single Step vs. Two-Step vs. Multi-Step Forms
There are three types of forms when it comes to how many steps the customer has to go through to submit a booking: single-step, two-step, and multi-step.

A single-step form allows a customer to fill out all of their booking information on a single page.

A two-step form allows customers to fill out the job information and their email address on the first page, and then navigate to the second page by clicking "Find Availability" to select a time and finish filling out their information.

A multi-step form breaks each section of the booking form into its own step, with the client clicking "Next" until they reach the final page.

Please note: The multi-step booking form is only available for Premium subscribers.

Change the From Steps Type
To change the type of booking form your client uses on the front end, please go to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Click "Options", "Edit" to the right of the form you'd like to update.

The second question in this section says, "Would you like a one-step, two-step, or multi-step booking form on the customer end?"

Ģ Select "One Step" to have the booking form appear as a single page on the backend of the site.
Ģ Select "Two-Step" if you'd like to have two pages to navigate between when booking on the backend.
Ģ Select "Multi-Step" if you'd like to break each section into its own step.

Once you've made your selection, click the blue "Save" button at the bottom right corner of the page.



Setting up tips and parking
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/set-up-tips-and-parking

Setting up tips and parking
Learn how to set up tips and parking on your booking form.

Last updated on 08 Jul, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to enable tips or parking fees for your booking form
Ģ How to enable the automated tip notifications
Ģ How to add a tip from the admin end
Ģ How customers can add a tip to their service

Enable/Disable Tips and Parking
If you would like your customers to be able to leave tips or pay parking fees for your provider, you can enable a tip and/or parking section on your booking form. 100% of tips and parking goes directly to the provider or team performing the job.

Customers can select one of the preset amounts, or click " Other " to enter a custom flat-rate or percentage-based tip.

Tips and parking are controlled under the " Service Category " section of your booking form, allowing you to decide which services you want to enable or disable this feature for.

To begin, go to: Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Service Category

Click " Options ", and then " Edit " to open an existing service category.

Scroll down to the bottom of the page until you see the " Extras " section.

To enable tips or parking fees, click the red " Disabled " button to switch to " Enabled ".

Next, decide whether you want the tip saved to " All " bookings or just the " First " booking if a customer has added a tip to a recurring service.

Under " Display ", decide when you want the tip or parking option to show on the booking form.
Ģ Customer frontend, backend, & admin - New customers, existing customers, and admin/staff will all be able to see and use the tip or parking option on the form.
Ģ Customer backend & admin - Only existing customers and admin/staff can add tips and/or parking.
Ģ Admin only - Only the admin and staff can see the tip and/or parking section on the booking form.

Once you are finished making your selections, click the blue " Save Category " button at the bottom of the page to update your changes.

Enable Automatic Tip Notifications
You can also set up BookingKoala to send customers an automated email and/or SMS notification asking them to leave a tip after completing their service.

Before you begin, please make sure you have set up your email and SMS notifications.
Click here to learn how to set up your email notifications.
Click here to learn how to set up your SMS notifications.

To enable the notification, go to: Notifications > Emails Notifications > SMS

At the top of the page, click the " Customer " tab. You'll also want to check that the toggle button at the top of the section is toggled to " Enabled ".

Scroll down to the " Completed Booking " section and click anywhere to expand it.

Click the red " Disabled " button next to the " Leave Tip " template to switch it to " Enabled ". A pop-up will appear, asking if you are sure you want to enable this notification. Click " Yes " to proceed.

You can also edit the template by clicking the " Edit " button on the far right.

The default message looks like this:
Once the customer clicks the "Go to Dashboard " link, they can click on the " Previous Appointments " tab and then click the " Add Tip " button below the booking. This is discussed in greater detail in a later section.

Adding Tips/Parking from the Admin End
Once you have enabled tips or parking for a service category, you can easily add a tip on behalf of a customer while filling out the booking form.

Simply scroll down the " Tips & Parking (Optional) " section and then use the buttons to select or enter the amount the customer would like to add.

Once you are finished filling out the form, click the green " Save Booking " or blue " Update Booking " button to add the tip.

Click here to learn how to add a tip after charging a customer for their service.



Enable the "key message" option
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/enabling-key-option

Enable the "key message" option
Learn how to enable or disable the key message and how it can be used in the booking form.

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ What is the "key message" feature
Ģ How to enable the key message for your booking form

What is the "key message"?
One of the optional features located in your booking form's settings is the ability to add a key message for the customer. When enabled, customers and staff can use this option to indicate if someone will be home for the service, if they will be hiding a key, and/or if they would like the provider to keep the key after the service has been completed.

Below, a text box is included to allow the customer to add any notes or instructions that have to do with accessing the location.

Enable the Key Message Option
The key message can be enabled or disabled under your form Settings section. You can locate this by going to:

Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Next, click " Options ", then " Edit " next to the form you'd like to view settings for.

Scroll down towards the bottom of the form until you see " Key message " with a red toggle button below it. Click the button to switch it to " Enabled ".

Finally, click the blue " Save " button at the bottom of the page to update your form settings.

You and your customers will now be able to leave and track key information using this section.



How to enable manual booking reminders
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-enable-manual-booking-reminders

How to enable manual booking reminders
You will learn how to enable manual booking reminders.

Last updated on 15 Apr, 2022

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ What are manual booking reminders
Ģ How to enable manual booking reminders for your booking form

What are manual booking reminders?
Manual booking reminders are notes and notifications that you can enable for specific bookings. These can be used for many different purposes, such as reminding yourself to contact a customer about a question, or checking that billing information has been added before the service date.

There are two types of manual booking reminders:
Ģ Alert before charge
Ģ Email reminder to admin

Once enabled, they will display at the bottom/end of your booking form in their own " Reminder " section, just before the " Payment Information " section.

Alert Before Charge

An " Alert before charge " allows you to place a pop-up notification on a booking that will display before you attempt to process the charge. You can use this feature to display reminders specific to charging the customer, especially if you want to delay the charge for any reason.

To set up an alert before charge, all you need to do is create or edit a booking, scroll down to the " Reminder " section, and check the box next to " Alert before charge now ".

Enter the message you would like to display in the box below. Once you are finished, remember to click the " Save Booking " or " Update Booking " button on the right side of the screen.

Once the booking has been completed, you can view the alert by going to: Bookings > Booking Charges

You'll notice that the booking does not have a checkbox to include it in bulk charging. This is to ensure that the alert is seen by you or your staff before processing the customer's payment.

Once you click the cash/check or $ icon to process the customer's charge, a pop-up will appear displaying your message.

When you are ready to process the charge, you can click the blue " Charge Now " button.

Email Reminder to Admin

An " Email reminder to admin " is exactly that: a notification that is emailed directly to the admin email address on file for your account.

To add a manual email reminder for the admin, edit or create a new booking, scroll down to the " Reminder " section, and check the box next to " Email reminder to admin ".

Once you check the box next to " Email reminder to admin ", you can pick one of three different options for when your notification will be sent out.
Ģ Date - Select a specific date for when the booking reminder will be sent to the admin
Ģ Days Before - Select how many days before the service you would like to receive the notification.
Ģ Hours Earlier - Select how many hours before the service you would like to receive the notification.

Then, type your reminder or not into the box below. Once you are finished, be sure to click the " Save Booking " or " Update Booking " button on the right side of the screen.

Enable Manual Booking Reminders
If you would like to turn on manual booking reminders for your form(s), please go to: Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings

Next, click " Options ", then " Edit " next to the form you'd like to view settings for.

Scroll down until you see the question, " Do you want to enable the ōReminderö option under the booking form? " and select " Yes ".

Click the blue " Save " button at the bottom left of the page to update your settings.

Next, you'll need to check that you have enabled the " Email reminder to admin " notification template. Please go to: Settings > Notifications > Emails

Under the " Admin " tab, scroll down until you see the " Reminders " section. Click on the section name to expand it.

Make sure that the button to the left of the " Email reminder to admin " is toggled to " Enabled ".

Once it has been enabled, you are ready to add manual email reminders for admin!



Booking form is not working, what to do
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/booking-form-is-not-working-what-to-do

Booking form is not working, what to do
Learn how to troubleshoot your booking form when it is not working as expected.

Last updated on 17 Jul, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ Reasons why a booking form is not behaving as expected
Ģ How to correct common form issues

Why is my booking form not working?
Based on previous issues others faced with their forms, we put together a guide to help you solve the problem(s) you are experiencing.

General Loading Issues
Ģ "I made some changes and when I went to view my booking form, things were missing on the form (such as frequencies, service categories, packages, etc)."
Ģ "I went to view my form and saw a blank/white screen."
Ģ "I went to view my form and the screen didn't load."

The most common reason forms will not load is server speed, causing the page to "time out".Ā This can be fixed by refreshing the page. If you are still having difficulties, try logging out and back in, or clearing your browser's cache.

798 Refresh the window/tab. There are a few different ways to do this depending on your device and browser type.

? Press Command + R simultaneously (for Mac devices)
? Press CTRL + R simultaneously (in most web browsers)
? Press the F5 key (for Windows PCs)
? Press CTRL + F5 simultaneously (for Windows laptops)
? Press fN + 5 simultaneously (keyboards without the F5 key)
799 Log out and back in again.

799.1 Click on your name in the top right corner of your dashboard.
799.2 Select "Logout" at the bottom of the drop-down menu.
799.3 Close the window or tab.Ā Be sure to exit any additional tabs or windows you have open that are associated with your account.
799.4 Open a new tab or window in your browser.
799.5 Log back into your admin dashboard.

Ģ Click here to learn how to log into your account.
800 Clear the browser's cache.Ā Sometimes, your cache will try to show you an older version of your site and forms. Please note it can take up to a minute or two for your browser to clear the cache.ĀĀ

800.1 Chrome

800.1.1 Click the "three dots" menu icon at the top right corner of the page.
800.1.2 Select More Tools > Clear browsing data.
800.1.3 Check the boxes next to "Browsing History", "Cookies", and "Cached images and files".
800.1.4 For the time range, we recommend selecting "All time".
800.1.5 Click the "Clear Data" button at the bottom of the pop-up.
800.2 Safari

800.2.1 Go to Settings >ĀSafari > Preferences > Privacy > Manage Website Data.
800.2.2 Click "Clear Website History".
800.2.3 Select your BookingKoala domain if it is listed, then clickĀRemove.
800.2.4 If you want to remove all website data from Safari, you can clickĀRemove Allinstead.
800.3 Microsoft Edge

800.3.1 Click on the "hamburger" menu icon.
800.3.2 Go to Settings > Settings > Privacy > Privacy, Search, and Services >ĀClear Browsing Data.
800.3.3 Click "Choose What To Clear".
800.3.4 Check the boxes next to "Browsing History" and "Cookies".
800.3.5 For the time range, we recommend selecting "All time".
800.3.6 Click on the "Clear Now" button at the bottom of the page.
800.4 Firefox

800.4.1 Click on the "hamburger" menu icon.
800.4.2 Go to Options > Privacy & Security.
800.4.3 Scroll down to the "History" section and click the "Clear History" button.
800.5 Opera

800.5.1 Click on the "clock" icon in the left sidebar.
800.5.2 Click on the "Clear Browsing Data" button.
800.5.3 Check the boxes next to "Browsing History", "Cookies", and "Cached images and files".
800.5.4 We recommend selecting "All time" for the time range.
800.5.5 Click the "Clear Data" button at the bottom of the pop-up.

If your browser is not listed here, please search the internet using the phrase "[Browser Name] clear browsing data".

Missing Forms and Industries
Ģ "My booking page says 'Under Maintenance'."
Ģ "I added a second form but it is missing from the 'Book now' page."
Ģ "An entire industry is missing from the booking form."

If your form shows an "Under Maintenance" message, it is most likely in test mode. Test mode can be turned off in the form's settings section.Ā If the form is not displaying at all, you may have not finished setting up a form combination.Ā If an entire industry is missing, the industry has likely been disabled for customersĀor disabled for all users.

801 Disable test mode. Your form can go into "test" mode so it will not display on the front end of your website.Ā If this option was enabled by mistake, you could easily disable it under the form's settings.

801.1 Go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Form Settings.
801.2 Look for any forms that say "Test" under the "status" column.
801.3 To update the form to "Live" mode, select "Options" then "Edit" to view the form's settings.
801.4 Scroll down to the bottom of the page and look for the "Form Status" toggle button.
801.5 Click the button to change it from "Disabled" to "Enabled".
801.6 Click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page to update your form.
802 Add/finish setting up a form combination.Ā If you have more than one booking form added to an industry, only the default form will be shown.Ā This can be remedied by creating a form combination.Ā If you start setting up a form combination but have not selected options for each form, this will also lead to one or more forms being hidden.

802.1 Go to Settings >ĀIndustries > [Industry Name] > Settings > Add/Combine Form.
802.2 If you see a single tab on this page that says "Combine 2 Forms", you will need to create a new combination.Ā Click here to learn how to combine two or more forms.
802.3 If you already have a form combination, click the "View All Combinations" tab.
802.4 Select "Options" then "Edit" next to the combination.
802.5 Look under each option and make sure that the missing form is selected for at least one of these options.
802.6 If the form has not been selected anywhere, click the bullet icon next to the missing form to select it for an option.Ā You can also click the blue "Add Option" button if you have 3+ forms.
802.7 Once you are finished making changes, click the blue "Update" button at the bottom of this section.
803 Enable the industry's status.Ā If an entire industry is missing, it has likely been disabled for customers or all users.Ā You can enable it again by going to the "Add/Delete Industry" section.ĀĀ

803.1 Go to Settings > Industries > Add/Delete Industries.
803.2 Click on the "Added Industries" tab at the top of the page.
803.3 Look for the missing industry and check the "Customer Status" and/or "Status" columns to see if one or both say "Disabled".
803.4 To enable the industry, click the toggle button(s) to change them from "Disabled" to "Enabled".

Missing Sections or Services
Ģ "My form is missing an entire section (such as frequencies, service categories, packages, etc)."
Ģ "My form is missing one or more options under a section."
Ģ "Customers cannot see my service options even though I can see them on the admin-end."
Ģ "Whenever a customer tries to book they see the message 'Either a service category, frequency, location, package, or variable is missing from the form. Please check your industry settings to continue."

If you are missing sections or options for your form, they may not have been added,Āor the "display to" settingsĀhave been disabled for customers.Ā ĀCertain sections require at least one option for the form to function correctly.Ā There are a few places you can check to resolve these issues.

804 A section is missing an option.ĀEvery form needs at least one option saved under the frequency, service category,Āand pricing sections (variables parameters, packages, items, etc).ĀĀIf you have locationsĀenabled, you must also have at least one location added to this section.Ā

804.1 To check that you have at least one frequency set up, go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Frequencies.
804.2 If the page says "Frequencies not found", you will need to add one by clicking the blue "Add New" button at the top right corner of the page.

Ģ Click here to learn how to set up one or more frequencies for your form.
804.3 To check that you have at least one service category set up, go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > Service Categories.
804.4 If the page says "Service categories not found", you will need to add one by clicking the blue "Add New" button at the top right corner of the page.

Ģ Click here to learn how to set up one or more service categories for your form.
804.5 To check that you have at least one variable set up, go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [1 or 4] > Pricing Parameters.
804.6 If the page says "Pricing parameters not found", you will need to add one by clicking the blue "Add New" button at the top right corner of the page.

Ģ Click here to learn how to set up one or more variables for your form.
804.7 To check that you have at least one package set up, go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form 2Ā> Packages.
804.8 If the page says "Packages not found", you will need to add one by clicking the blue "Add New" button at the top right corner of the page.

Ģ Click hereĀto learn how to set up one or more packages for your form.
804.9 To check that you have at least one item set up, go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form 3Ā> Items.
804.10 If the page says "Items not found", you will need to add one by clicking the blue "Add New" button at the top right corner of the page.

Ģ Click hereĀto learn how to set up one or more items for your form.
804.11 To check that you have at least one add-onĀset up, go to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form 3Ā> Add-ons.
804.12 If the page says "Add-ons not found", you will need to add one by clicking the blue "Add New" button at the top right corner of the page.

Ģ Click hereĀto learn how to set up one or more add-ons for your form.
804.13 To check if you have locations enabled for your form, go toĀSettings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] and look for a "Locations" option at the top of the drop-down menu.
804.14 If there is no "Locations" section, you do not need to take any further action.
804.15 If there is a "Locations" section, select it from the drop-down menu and check to see if the page says "Locations not found".
804.16 If this is the case, click the blue "Add New" button at the top of the page to add a location for your form.

Ģ Click here to learn how to add a location to your form.
804.17 You can also disable locations if you do not want to use them at all.

Ģ Click here to learn how to disable locations for your industries or booking forms.
805 Your "Display" settings are set to admin only.Ā This is a very common reason why an admin or staff can view an option but the customer cannot.Ā You can fix this by updating the "Display" section of the missing option.ĀĀ

805.1 Open the form section with the missing option by going to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > [Locations/Frequencies/Service Categories/Pricing Parameters/Packages/Items/Add-Ons/Extras].
805.2 Locate the missing option in the list.
805.3 Select "Options", and "Edit" to open up the settings for that option.
805.4 Scroll down to the "Display" or "Display On" section and select "Customer frontend, backend, & admin".
805.5 Click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.
806 Dependency selections are missing.Ā If you use "dependencies" (such as the pricing depending on which location the client picks), it is important to check that these services have at least one option selected.   Ā

806.1 Open the form section with the missing option by going to Settings > Industries > [Industry Name] > Form [#] > [Frequencies/Service Categories/Pricing Parameters/Packages/Items/Add-Ons/Extras].
806.2 Locate the missing option in the list.
806.3 Select "Options", and "Edit" to open up the settings for that option.
806.4 Look for the question "Should the [Frequencies/Service Categories/Pricing Parameters/Packages/Items/Add-Ons/Extras] be based on the [Frequencies/Service Categories/Pricing Parameters/Packages/Items/Add-Ons/Extras]?"
806.5 Make sure at least one box has been checked next to the options listed below.
806.6 Click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.

Payment Method Issues
Ģ "The debit/credit or cash/check option is missing from the form."
Ģ "I went to book an appointment and when I tried booking with a credit/debit card, I got an error message and/or couldn't complete my booking."

If you or your customers are experiencing any booking form issues regarding the add card/payment section, you should first check to see if you have enabled at least one payment method for customers.ĀIf customers are receiving an error message when they try to enter a debit or credit card on the booking form, you will need to check your payment processor settings.

807 Enable your customer payment methods.Ā If one or both of the payment options are missing from the form, they have likely been disabled in your general store options.ĀĀ

807.1 To check, please go to Settings > General > Store Options > General.
807.2 Click on the "Store Info" subcategory to display the settings in this section.
807.3 Scroll down to the "Accepted Forms of Payment" section.
807.4 If the boxes next to "Credit/Debit Card" and/or "Cash/Check" are unchecked, check the box(es) for the option you'd like to enable.
807.5 Click the blue "Save" button at the bottom of the page.
808 Check your payment processor connection.Ā If you or your customers receive a red error message when trying to enter credit card information into the booking form, there is likely a problem with how the payment processor has been connected.

808.1 Use the following links to review your payment processor connection.

Ģ Click here to review the setup guide for payments via Stripe.
Ģ Click here to review the setup guide for payments via Square.
Ģ Click here to review the setup guide for payments via PayPal (via Braintree).
808.2 Check that the entire key as displayed in the payment processor dashboard has been pasted in the connect payment processor section in BookingKoala.
808.3 MostAPI keys and/or account numbersĀconsist of many characters.
808.4 Some keys, especially secret keys, may require you to click on an option to reveal the whole key.Ā Generally, the preview for a secret key will only display the first several characters with an ellipse (...) after it.

Why no dates/times show when scheduling
