How to download or print an invoice
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-download-or-print-an-invoice

How to download or print an invoice
Learn how to download or print an active or draft invoice.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to download an active or draft invoice
Ģ How to print an active or draft invoice

Getting Started
To begin, go to the Invoice Dashboard under: Bookings > Booking Charges > Invoices tab.

Download an Invoice
To download an invoice, locate an invoice in the dashboard and click the down arrow in the Actions column to reveal the Download option.

A pop-up will ask if you are sure you want to download this invoice. Click the blue Confirm button to proceed.

A copy of the invoice will be downloaded as a PDF to your device.

Print an Invoice
If you would like to print an invoice, locate the invoice in the dashboard and click the down arrow in the Actions column to reveal the Print option. This will automatically launch the print option for your device.

Set the printer next to Destination and then click Print, or select Save as PDF and then click Save to save the file to your device.



How to charge or change the status of a custom invoice
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-charge-or-change-the-status-of-a-custom-invoice

How to charge or change the status of a custom invoice
Learn how to charge or change the payment status of an active custom invoice.

Last updated on 31 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ How to charge an active invoice
Ģ How to change the payment status of an active invoice

Getting Started
To begin, go to the Invoice Dashboard under:

Bookings > Booking Charges > Invoices tab .

Charge an Invoice
Once an invoice is due, it is possible to collect payment by charging a custom invoice manually.

Go to the Active Invoice tab, scroll down to the custom invoice, and click the Charge option beneath the Actions column.

A menu will appear on the right side of the page.

If the invoice is a custom invoice, there is an option for Do you want to pay the provider? If you select Yes , you can search for the providers and enter their portion of the payment.

Below, the Payment Information section will display options for the billing address as well as any cards on file.  There is also the option to add a new card on file for the payment.

Near the bottom of the menu, there is an option to exclude the customer notification that their card has been charged.

Finally, once a card has been selected or a new card has been entered, click the blue Charge button to charge the customer's invoice.

A green system alert will appear at the top right corner of the page, confirming the charge has been collected.  The status of the invoice will be updated to Paid.

Change an Custom Invoice's Payment Status
One can also change a custom invoice's payment status using the manual Change Status option.

To access this, go to the Active or Overdue invoice tab, click the down arrow icon beneath the actions column, and select Change Status.

A popup will appear with three different options for updating the payment status.

Ģ Paid - The payment has been collected outside of BookingKoala.  The invoice status will be updated to Paid with a Cash/Check tag added beneath the amount.
Ģ Void - The invoice was created my mistake or was accidentally finalized so no payment has been collected.
Ģ Uncollectible - Payment for this invoice is not expected; however, it is still possible to collect payment should your customer attempt to make payment.  The invoice status will be updated to Uncollectible.

You can also choose to leave an internal note viewable to the admin and staff only that describes why a particular payment status was selected.

Finally, click the blue Update button to finalize the payment status.  A green system alert will appear in the top right corner confirming the status has been updated successfully.



How to send an invoice
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-send-an-invoice-or-invoice-card-link

How to send an invoice
Learn how to resend an invoice and manually send an invoice link.

Last updated on 11 Oct, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to resend an active invoice to a customer
Ģ How to send an invoice link to a customer

Getting Started
To begin, go to the Invoice Dashboard under:

Bookings > Booking Charges > Invoices tab .

Resend an Invoice
If an invoice is overdue and the customer has not made a payment yet, you can resend the invoice to the customer. To do this, go to the invoice dashboard and click on the Overdue tab.

Next, click on the down arrow icon beneath the Actions column and select Resend.

A pop-up will ask if you are sure you'd like to resend the invoice. Click the blue Confirm button to proceed.

Send an Invoice Link
If you are manually sending an invoice link to a customer, go to the Active or Overdue tab on the invoice dashboard for any manual invoices you have created.

Then, click on the down arrow icon beneath the Actions column and select Copy Link.

A green system alert will appear at the top right corner of the page, confirming that the invoice link has been successfully copied. This link can then be added to an email or SMS message and sent to the customer manually.



How to view invoice logs
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-view-invoice-logs

How to view invoice logs
Learn where to view invoice logs including payment logs for draft invoices.

Last updated on 30 May, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to view invoice logs for active and draft invoices
Ģ How to view invoice payment logs for draft invoices

Getting Started
To begin, go to the Invoice Dashboard under: Bookings > Booking Charges > Invoices tab.

Invoice Logs
Invoice logs are logs that show the types of updates an invoice has undergone. This includes when the invoice was created, if the invoice was updated, and any changes in payment status to the invoice.

To view an invoice's logs, click on the down arrow icon beneath the Actions column and select View Log.

The invoice logs can be filtered by actions taken by the merchant, staff, and customer by clicking on the Roll drop-down menu.

Each log will include the invoice number, the action taken, who modified the invoice, and the time of the modification.

Invoice Payment Logs
Invoice payment logs show details related to payment(s) made toward an invoice. For example, if an invoice is set for partial payment, there would be multiple logs showing each payment made towards the invoice.

To view an invoice's payment logs, click on the down arrow icon beneath the Actions column and select Payment Logs.

A popup will display all payment logs related to that invoice.



Charge a booking or separate fee using the add charge option
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/charge-a-booking-or-separate-fee-using-the-add-charge-option

Charge a booking or separate fee using the add charge option
Learn how to mass charge bookings or how to charge a separate fee outside of the booking charge.

Last updated on 05 Jun, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to use the Add Charge option

Using the Add Charge Option
The Add Charge option has been designed to allow the merchant and staff to charge bookings and charge separate fees outside of the total associated with a particular booking. To access this feature, please go to:

Bookings > Booking Charges > Pending or All Charges tab

Click the white Add Charge button located in the upper-right corner of the section. There are two different options located in the Add Charge feature: Charge Booking and Separate Charge.

Ģ The Charge Booking option allows you to charge or precharge a booking already in the system.
Ģ The Separate Charge option allows you to create an extra charge not associated with a booking in the system.

Charge a Booking

To use the Charge Booking option, search the customer's name, email address, or phone number in the search field to locate their profile.

Click on the customer profile to view and check the boxes next to the booking(s) you would like to charge. The total charge amount will be displayed in the field below.

Next, click the Payment Information section to enter a new credit card , select an existing credit card , or pick cash/check to charge the booking.

At the bottom of the side menu, click the Notification section if you would like to disable or exclude customer notifications from being sent upon charging the booking(s).

Finally, click the blue Charge button at the bottom right corner of the menu to process the booking charge. A green system notification will appear in the top right corner of the page, confirming the charge has been successfully processed.

Create a Separate Charge

To use the Separate Charge option, select Separate Charge at the top of the Add Charges section. Next, select whether the charge will be for a New Customer or an Existing Customer.

Ģ If the charge is for a New Customer , fill out the fields below to create a customer profile upon charging.
Ģ If the charge is for an Existing Customer , search the customer's name, email, or phone number is the search field to select their profile and an address to associate with the charge.

Next, enter the total you want to charge the customer into the Charge Amount section.

Next, click the Do you want to pay provider(s)? section and select whether any providers should receive a portion of this charge as payment.

Ģ Select Yes to pay one or more providers by searching their name in the search field and allocating their payment amount to the right of their name.
Ģ Select No if none of the payments should be allocated to providers.

If you'd like to add any notes to the charge, click on the Notes section and click the blue Add Note button. Notes can be added for the merchant/staff , the provider(s) , and/or the customer .

If you would like to include the customer note in the customer's charge notification, check the box next to Include in notification.

Next, click the Payment Information section to enter a new credit card , select an existing credit card , or pick cash/check to charge the booking.

At the bottom of the side menu, click the Notification section if you would like to disable or exclude customer notifications from being sent upon charging the booking(s).

Finally, click the blue Charge button at the bottom right corner of the menu to process the booking charge. A green system notification will appear in the top right corner of the page, confirming the charge has been successfully processed.



Invoice payment Q&As
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/invoice-payment-q-and-as

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ What happens to invoices when bookings are canceled, voided, postponed, ID changes, or their amount is marked leave as is
Ģ How the invoice changes when the booking amount changes
Ģ What happens to invoices when the booking is charged from somewhere other than the invoice
Ģ How invoices handle bookings with credit card holds

What will happen to the invoice if bookings are canceled, voided, postponed, their ID changes, or their amount is marked as "leave as it is"?
If bookings in an invoice are canceled, voided, postponed, their ID changes, or their amount is marked as "leave as it is", the impact on the invoice will depend on the payment status of the bookings and invoice.

Please note: If the invoice is fully paid, these actions will not impact the invoice.

Ģ No charge on booking s: If the bookings are not paid yet or if no charges have been performed, the bookings will be removed from the invoice. If there was only one booking in the invoice, the invoice will be marked as canceled.
Ģ Charge on bookings: If the charge on the booking has already been performed and it is canceled, voided, postponed, their ID changes, or their amount is marked as "leave as it is", that booking will be removed from the invoice, but a static line item will be placed for that booking with the charged amount. The booking(s) static line item will be shown as follows:

? Canceled booking
? Postponed booking
? Voided booking
? Booking ID changed :
? Leave "as it is" booking with a decreased amount
? Leave "as it is" booking with an increased amount

Please note : The cancellation refund is not considered on the invoice.

How will the invoice be affected if the booking amount increases or decreases?
The booking amount can be changed from booking reschedule/update or from booking time logs.

A booking "B1" for $100 is present in invoice #101. The total amount of the invoice is $100. The booking has been rescheduled, with the amount becoming $140. As a result, the invoice total amount will automatically change to $140. The same applies to the amount decrease.

Please note: If the invoice is fully paid, these actions will not impact the invoice.

What happens to an invoice if the booking is charged from somewhere other than the invoice?
Apart from the invoice. A booking in the invoice can be charged from pending charges listing, upcoming bookings listing, or through auto-charge if the setting is enabled.

In this case, the charge will be synced on the invoice as well.

Cases that may arise include the following:

Ģ There is a booking "B1" in invoice #101.

? The B1 booking amount is $230. Thus, the total invoice amount is also $230.
? The invoice is yet unpaid.
? Booking B1 is charged from pending charges listing, upcoming bookings listing, or through auto-charge.
? The invoice #101 will be marked as paid automatically (if it is sent to the customer).
? If the invoice is not sent/scheduled, it will be marked as partially paid, and when it is sent, if there is no remaining amount it will be marked as fully paid. It will be marked as paid.

How does the invoice handle card-hold bookings?
On booking invoices, the card hold is handled through the setting given in the store options section:

Store Options > General > Invoice Settings

If the setting is set to:

Ģ Release : Upon invoice successful payment, a new charge is performed based on the selected card, and the hold amount (if any) is released.
Ģ Capture : The held amount will be captured using the card placed under the hold. Any remaining amount will then be charged against the invoice card selected.

Please Note : If a partial payment is made on an invoice and the setting is to capture, then the card hold amount will overwrite that amount.

For example, there is a booking "B1" with an amount of $200 and a card hold of $120 is present in the invoice having a total amount of $200, which can be paid in three installments of 10%, 20%, and 70%. The customer has chosen to pay 10% ($20) of the invoice charge. Instead of $20, the total hold amount of $120 will be taken.

With the invoice paid notification, you can send a single booking charge or pre-charge notification.
In addition to the "invoice paid" notification, you can also send the booking charge/ pre-charge notification using the settings under:

Store Options > General > Payment

Ģ If the setting is ō Yes ö, then along with the "invoice paid" notification, an individual booking charge notification including the service receipt (if enabled) will also be sent to the customer.
Ģ If the setting is ōNoö, then only invoice notification will be sent.

Please Note: Voided or marked "leave as it is" bookings will not be added to recurring or one-time invoices.



Recurring invoice Q&As
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/recurring-invoice-q-and-as

Recurring invoice Q&As
Learn more about how recurring invoices work concerning customers, bookings, and reminders.

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ The limitations and criteria involved in creating recurring invoices
Ģ How SMS and email reminders interact with invoice updates

Recurring Invoice Limitations and Criteria
Is there a limit on how many recurring booking invoices you can create for a customer?
You can create only one recurring booking invoice schedule per customer.

What are the criteria for which bookings are selected in recurring invoice bookings?
It depends on which frequency of recurring booking invoices are created.

Daily recurring booking invoices
Let's say a customer has active bookings with a daily schedule. We create a booking-based recurring invoice with a daily frequency with a booking date range of February 8 to the end date of February 14.  Invoices will be sent as follows:

Ģ Invoice 1 -> Feb 10 (with bookings for range Feb 8 - Feb 10)
Ģ Invoice 2 -> Feb 11 (with bookings for range  Feb 8 - Feb 11)
Ģ Invoice 3 -> Feb 12 (with bookings for range  Feb 8 - Feb 12)
Ģ Invoice 4 -> Feb 13 (with bookings for range  Feb 8 - Feb 13)
Ģ Invoice 5 -> Feb 14 (with bookings for range  Feb -8  Feb 14)

No further invoices will be generated after February 14 (the end date).

Weekly recurring booking invoices
Let's suppose the customer has active bookings with a weekly schedule and we are creating a bookings recurring invoice with a weekly frequency with a booking date range of February 10 (sent every Saturday) and an end date of March 11. Invoices will be sent as follows:

Ģ Invoice 1 -> Feb 10 (with bookings from Feb 10 to Feb 16)
Ģ Invoice 2 -> Feb 17 (with bookings for range Feb 10 - Feb 23)
Ģ Invoice 3 -> Feb 24 (with bookings for range Feb 10 - March 1)
Ģ Invoice 4 -> March 2 (with bookings for range Feb 10 - March 8)
Ģ Invoice 5 -> March 9 (with bookings for range Feb 10 - March 11)

No further invoices will be generated after 11 March.

Monthly recurring booking invoices
Let's suppose the customer has active bookings with an every 4 weeks schedule and some one-time bookings and we are creating a booking recurring invoice with a monthly frequency starting on February 10 (sent on every 10th of the month) and an end date of April 11. Invoices will be sent as follows:

Ģ Invoice 1 -> Feb 10 (with bookings from Feb 10 to March 9)
Ģ Invoice 2 -> March 10 (with bookings for the range Feb 10 - April 9)
Ģ Invoice 3 -> April 10 (with bookings for the range Feb 10 - April 11)

No further invoices will be generated after April 11, as the frequency end date is reached.

Yearly recurring booking invoices
Let's suppose the customer has active bookings with a monthly schedule and some other bookings with different frequencies. We are creating recurring invoices with a yearly frequency starting on February 10 (sent on the 10th of every February (2024) and ending on February 10, 2026. Invoices will be sent as follows:

Ģ Invoice 1 -> Feb 10, 2024 (with bookings from Feb 10 2024 to Feb 9, 2025)
Ģ Invoice 2 -> Feb 10, 2025 (with bookings for the range Feb 10, 2024 - Feb 9, 2026)
Ģ Invoice 3 -> Feb 10, 2026 (with bookings for the range Feb 10, 2024 - Feb 10, 2026 )

No further invoices will be generated after February 10, 2026, as the frequency end date is reached.

What are the criteria by which the repeat type of invoice is selected?
The recurring type of invoice is selected based on the customer's active recurring bookings frequencies. Let's suppose a customer has daily and weekly frequency bookings, the frequency will be shown in the invoice as weekly, monthly, or yearly. Based on the higher booking frequency of the customer, Bookingkoala gives options for choosing recurring invoice frequency.

Please refer to the following table to help you understand this:

Booking FrequencyInvoice FrequencyDailyDaily, Weekly, Monthly, YearlyDaily no Saturday or SundayDaily, Weekly, Monthly, YearlyDaily no SundayDaily, Weekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery Monday, FridayWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery Monday, Wednesday, FridayWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery Tuesday and ThursdayWeekly, Monthly, YearlySaturday SundayWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery Tuesday, Wednesday, FridayWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery Monday, WednesdayWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery Monday, ThursdayWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery weekWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery 2 weekWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery 3 weekWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery 4 weekWeekly, Monthly, YearlyEvery 5 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 6 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 7 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 8 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 9 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 10 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 11 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 12 weekMonthly, YearlyEvery 24 weekMonthly, YearlyIs it possible for recurring booking invoices to be canceled automatically?
Generally, there is one primary reason for canceling recurring invoices. If there are no bookings available to generate an invoice, there is a possibility that the booking recurring invoice has been canceled.

Invoice Reminders and Notifications
Can we send the customer reminders about their invoices?
Yes, you can send invoice reminders to the customer via email and SMS. For this, you must include reminders in the invoice.

Ģ How many types of reminders can we send to the customer for payment?

There are three types of invoice reminders:

Ģ "Before due-date" reminders
Ģ "On due-date" reminders
Ģ "After due-date" reminders

Is it possible to set email reminders at a different time?
For email reminders, set up the following settings:

Settings > Notifications > Emails > Customer > Invoice

There are three email templates for reminders:

Ģ "Invoice Due Today" Reminder
Ģ "Invoice Overdue" Reminder
Ģ "Upcoming Payment" For Invoice Reminder

You can select any hour range to send the email reminder to the customer.

Is it possible to set SMS reminders at a different time?
For SMS reminders, set up the following settings:

Settings > Notifications > SMS > Customer > Invoice

There are three SMS templates for reminders:

Ģ "Invoice Due Today" Reminder
Ģ "Invoice Overdue" Reminder
Ģ "Upcoming Payment For Invoice" Reminder

You can select any hour range to send the SMS reminder to the customer.

What is the maximum number of invoice reminders we can send?
The system can send up to 5 invoice reminders per customer.



Separate charge feature
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/separate-charge-feature

Separate charge feature
Learn which features are included in the separate charge feature, where to set them up, and how to use them for admin, providers, customers, and staff.

Last updated on 20 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ What is a separate charge?
Ģ How do separate charges work?
Ģ Separate payments for the providers
Ģ Notifications related to a separate charge
Ģ Syncing with third-party apps

What Is a Separate Charge?
The separate charge feature is a customer charging feature that provides an option to the admin to charge the customer irrespective of the booking. It's like a standalone charge that the admin can use to collect money from the customer for something unrelated to a booking.

How Do Separate Charges Work?
You can process a separate charge by clicking the ō Add charges ö button available at either of the following locations: Bookings > Booking charges > Pending Charges Or, Bookings > Booking charges > All Charges > Separate

By clicking the ō Add charge ö button, the ō Add charges ö side window appears from the right side of the screen from where you can select the option ō Separate Charge ö to proceed further.

Once you select the ō Separate Charge ö radio button option, you can add the ō Customer details ö for the ō New Customer ö or select the ō Existing Customer ö as mentioned below:

Ģ New Customer : If you select the new customer option, the system will add a new customer with the following customer details:
? First Name
? Last Name
? Gender (Male / Female / Unspecified)
? Email address
? Phone no.
? Address
? Apt. no.
? Please note that if the system can send notifications related to new customer additions, like "New Account" and "Reset Password", the system will send those notifications to this new customer.
Ģ Existing Customer : If you select the existing customer option, a ō Select Customer ö search bar will appear from where you can search for the existing customer's name. The search bar will show the available existing name options matching the search bar name. Click on the existing customerÆs name to select the customer and the system will display the following information related to the existing customer:
? Address Details : You can select the ō Existing address ö or add a ō New address ö.
Ģ For the ō Existing address ö option, the system shows all the saved addresses for that existing customer under the ō Saved addresses ö dropdown menu option. Upon selection of the appropriate saved address from the dropdown menu, the ō Address ö and ō Apt ö fields get filled with the data.
Ģ For the ō New address ö option, the system shows the blank fields of ō Address ö and ō Apt ö to which you can add the required data.

The next section is the ōCharge amountösection.
Clicking theōCharge amountö section opens the field where you can add a separate charge amount. Here you can add a fixed amount only. If the taxes are enabled, then the taxes will be charged on the amount added in the amount field and will be added to show the ōTotalö charge amount. You also have the option to exclude the tax for this separate charge, by checking the checkbox available in front of the option saying ō Do you want to exclude sales tax? ö.

Tax calculation on separate charge: The tax is calculated on separate charges based on the tax settings, from: Settings > General > Taxes If the tax is enabled then only it will be applicable on a separate charge.
Ģ For flat tax : If the setting, " Do you want to set different taxes for different locations?" is ō No ö, then the tax will be calculated based on the percentage selected. If the setting is ō Yes ö, then the tax will not be applicable on the separate charge.
Ģ For Taxify : The tax will be calculated based on the customer's address.

The next section is ō Do you want to pay provider(s)? ö from where you can select a provider/team to pay them separately. With the ō Yes ö option, the system allows you to select the provider/team and add the amount for making the payment against the separate charge, and if the ō No ö option is selected, then the providers/teams will not be paid any amount against this separate charge.

If the ō Yes ö option is selected, then a ō Select Provider(s) ö search bar will appear from where you can search for the name of the existing provider/team, and the search bar will show the available existing providerÆs/teamÆs name options matching the search bar name. Click on the checkbox against the existing providerÆs/teamÆs name that you want to select and click the ō Select ö button to select the provider. You can choose from here a single or multiple providers/teams. Once the ō Select ö button is clicked, the system adds all the providers/teams selected to this separate charge window and gives you the option to add the fixed amount that you want to pay to each provider/team, individually, against this separate charge.

If you have selected multiple providers/teams, you will see a trash bin icon against each one, which allows you to remove a particular provider/team from the separate charge. Please enter the fixed amount in the amount field available against each provider/team for the amount you want to pay to the provider(s)/team(s). You can click the ō Clear all ö button to reset the amount values and add again.

The next section is the ō Notesö section, where you can add notes for the merchant, customer, and provider/team.
Click on the ōAdd noteö button to open the ōAdd notesö pop-up window. Here, you can add any specific notes related to this separate charge for the merchant, customer, or provider/team. You can apply various test styles to the text and click on the ōSaveö button to save the notes.

Once the notes are saved, they appear in the ōNotesö section, with options to edit or delete them. Under the customer notes and providers/team notes, you will see a checkbox saying ō Includes in notification.ö Checking this checkbox will include the note in the customerÆs notification or the provider/team notifications, respectively.

The next section is the ōPayment Informationö section from where you can charge the separate charge amount by a credit/debit card or by cash/check.
Ģ Select the ō Existing credit card ö radio button option to charge the credit card from the already saved card option(s) available under the ō Your saved cardö dropdown field. Please note that this option will only appear for existing customers.
Ģ Select the ō New credit card ö option to open up the ō Add new cardö fields which allows you to add the card number, card expiry date in MM/YY format, and CVC on which the separate charge can be performed.
Ģ Select the ō Cash / check ö option if you want to charge the customer through cash, check, or any other mode of payment like bank transfers, etc.

The next section is the ō Notification ö section from where you have the option to exclude sending notifications regarding this separate charge. Checking the checkbox against ō Exclude notification for customer ö will exclude or will not send the notification to the customer regarding this separate charge.

Lastly, click on the ō Charge ö button to perform the charge. On the successful charge, the separate charge will appear at: Bookings > Booking Charges > All Charges > under the ōSeparateötab
From where, you can sync this charge on QuickBooks (if QuickBooks is integrated) and can refund the charge by clicking the refund icon available against this charge.

How will the separate charge be taken with the 3DS2 authentication? In the case of 3DS2 authentication, separate charge notifications will be sent to the customer and also a ōseparate charge authentication badgeö will be added to the separate charge. Once the customer authenticates the charge from the reauthentication page, the charge will succeed.



Customer booking charges
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/customer-booking-charges

Customer booking charges
Learn more about the "Add Charges" option and how to process booking charges.

Last updated on 31 May, 2024

Charges for customer bookings
BookingKoala allows you to charge all the completed bookings of a customer.

You can process a charge by clicking the ōAdd chargesö button available at either of the following locations:
Ģ Bookings > Booking charges > Pending Charges
Ģ Bookings > Booking charges > All Charges

On the click of the ōAdd chargesö button, you will see two options. From which ōCharge booking(s)ö is autoselected. Search for the customer for whom you want to charge the bookings. You can search by name, email, or phone-number.

After searching the customer, you will see all the completed bookings of the searched customer. A booking is considered "complete" once the job length has passed in the system.
Ģ For example, if you schedule a 3-hour job at 8:00 AM, the system will consider the job finished at 11:01 AM.

You can select the booking(s) on which a charge should be applied from the list. In "Charge amount", you will see the total amount to be charged.

Now, you need to provide the payment information. In the payment information, you need to select the payment method. The following options are available to you:
Ģ Existing credit card (if there is one on file with the customer)
Ģ New credit card
Ģ Cash/check

Now, to perform the charge you need to click on the ōChargeö button.

Upon clicking the "charge" button, a single charge will be performed on all the selected bookings based on the selected payment information.

Location selection for Stripe payment gateway: If you are using a Stripe payment gateway, in that case, you also need to select the location. The payment will be processed based on location. The option is given because different locations can have different Stripe accounts associated with them.

Please Note: The charge will be processed based on the selected payment information, regardless of the payment information(payment method) provided on the booking.

To learn more about integrating Stripe as your payment processor, click here.

How will the card hold be handled? If there is a card hold on any/all of the selected bookings, the card hold amount will be captured and the remaining amount(if any) will be charged.

How will the charge decline be handled? If the charge is declined because of any reason, the booking(s) will be moved to the ōDeclinedö tab. From there, you can attempt a charge on the booking(s).

How does 3DS2 impact payment processing?
If the 3DS2 is enabled on your account and the 3DS2 authentication is requested by the payment processor during the charge, then an authentication email/SMS will be sent to the customer. On successful authentication by the customer. The charge will be processed and the booking(s) will be marked as charged.

Please Note: If the 3DS2 authentication request has already been sent for the booking. Then a duplicate request will not be sent.

Notifications sent: Upon successful processing of the charge, email and SMS notifications will be sent to the customer (if enabled).

To view, edit, or enable/disable CustomerÆs notification templates, go to:
Ģ Notifications > Email > Customer tab, or
Ģ Notifications > SMS > Customer tab.

Under the Booking Fee Charged & Refund section, you will see:
Ģ Bulk bookings charge
Ģ Bulk bookings charge authentication

Apart from these notification(s), if you want to send the individual charge notifications (email and SMS) to the customer, then you can send them using the setting under:
Ģ General > Store Options > Payment > Do you want to send individual charge notifications to the customer with the bulk charge or invoice charge?

If the setting is ōYesö, then the following notification(s) along with the above-mentioned bulk charge notification will be sent:
Ģ Booking charged
Ģ Service receipt

To learn more about 3DS2 authentication in BookingKoala, click here.



Partial/advance payments on invoices
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/understanding-partial-slash-advance-payments-on-invoices

Partial/advance payments on invoices
Learn how to set up partial payments on invoices. Partial and advance payments on invoices allow customers to pay an invoice in multiple installments rather than the full amount at once.

Last updated on 05 Jun, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand the following:
Ģ How partial/advance payment works in an invoice
Ģ How to show invoice payment logs to the customer
Ģ How card holds impact the partial payment

Enabling Partial/Advance Payment
When creating an invoice, you can allow partial or advance payments. This can be set up in the "Charges" section of the invoice creation process.

Look for the option "Allow partial/advance payment" and enable it by checking the box.
Ģ You can split the payment into a range of 2 to 10 installments.
Ģ Select the desired number of installments from the provided options.

Setting the Split Percentage
Ģ Define how the total amount will be split across the chosen installments.
Ģ Ensure the sum of the split percentages for the first and intermediate payments is less than 100%, as the remaining amount will be charged in the final installment.

For example, if you choose to split the payment into 3 parts:
Ģ 1st installment: 10%
Ģ 2nd installment: 30%
Ģ The final installment will automatically be the remaining 60%.

Example Scenario Let's illustrate this with a practical example:

Chosen Installments:
1st Installment: 10%
2nd Installment: 30%
Final Installment: The remaining balance (60%).

Customer Payment Process When the invoice is sent to the customer, they can pay the full amount or make a partial payment as defined by the chosen installments.

If the customer opts for partial payment, the percentage for the current installment will be applied to each booking amount.

First Installment:
Ģ 10% of booking 1: $50
Ģ 10% of booking 2: $35
Ģ 10% of booking 3: $20
Total for 1st Installment: $105

The invoice will be marked with the status "Partial payment" upon successful charge.

Second installment:
Ģ 30% of B1: $150
Ģ 30% of B2: $105
Ģ 30% of B3: $60
Total for 2nd Installment: $315

Again, the invoice will be marked with the "Partial payment" status upon the successful charge.

Final Installment:
The remaining balance to be paid from each booking.
On successful charge, the invoice will be marked as paid.

Show Invoice Payment Logs to the Customer
You can show the payment logs to the customer for partially paid invoices. By default, these logs are hidden. HereÆs how you can make them visible.

First, you will need to access the Website Builder & Themes. Please go to: Website Builder & Themes > Customize Themes > Page > Payment Details > Invoice

Customize the Invoice Page:
Ģ Find the table section under the "Pay Invoice" button.
Ģ Choose the option to show or hide the invoice logs.

The invoice payments will be shown to the customer in the invoice.

How Card Hold Impacts Partial Payment
When an invoice includes bookings with a card hold, the hold will be visible to the customer.

The way card holds are managed in invoices is determined by the settings configured in your store options. Go to: Store Options > General > Invoice Settings

Locate the setting: "What should happen if you have a card hold with invoiced bookings?"

Choose between two options:

Release: If you select the "Release" option, the card hold will be released when the invoice is processed.
Ģ The customer will then be charged the selected amount.
Ģ For example, if the booking amount in the invoice is $200, and there was a card hold for $200. The customer chose to pay 10% of the advance payment. The hold will be released, and a single charge of $20(10%) will be made.

Capture: If you select the "Capture" option, the card hold amount will be captured first.
Ģ Subsequent charges will be made for each installment as per the partial payment plan.
Ģ For example, if the booking amount in the invoice is $200, and there was a card hold for $200. The customer chose to pay 10% of the advance payment. Irrespective of the partial payment option, a complete $200 will be charged to the customer.



How to send an invoice card link
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-send-an-invoice-card-link

How to send an invoice card link
Learn more about invoice links including how to send an invoice card link to a customer.

Last updated on 04 Jun, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to send an invoice card link to a customer

Getting Started
To begin, go to the Invoice Dashboard under: Bookings > Booking Charges > Invoices tab.

Send an Invoice Card Link
If you would like to send an invoice card link to a customer, go to the Active or Overdue tab on the invoice dashboard for any of the invoices you have created.

Then, click on the down arrow icon beneath the Actions column and select Send Card Link.

A pop-up will appear where you can either manually copy the card link URL, or you can select the blue Send button to send an email, SMS, or both with optional text to the customer with the add card link.



Resend an invoice
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/resend-an-invoice

Resend an invoice
Learn the difference between automatic versus manual invoice sending and how to resend an invoice to a client.

Last updated on 24 Oct, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ The difference between automatic and manual invoice sending
Ģ How to resend an invoice

Overview
If an invoice is overdue and the customer has not made a payment yet, you can easily resend the invoice. With our enhanced functionality, invoices can now be sent via both Email and SMS Śbut only for Premium Subscribers. Below are the details of how this feature works based on your subscription level.

Automatic vs. Manual Invoice Sending

For Premium Subscribers : Invoices can be sent automatically via Email or SMS, saving you valuable time. You simply need to configure your settings to enable this feature. If youÆre not a Premium Subscriber, youÆll need to manually send invoices through an invoice link.

Upgrade to Automatic Invoicing

If you're interested in the convenience of automatic invoicing, you can easily upgrade your subscription. Simply click the blue Upgrade Now button within the "Send Automatically" section to unlock this feature.

How to Send Invoices Automatically
To enable automatic sending of invoices for either Email or SMS:

885 Select the "Send Automatically" option for either the Email or SMS tabs (or both) when setting up your invoice.
886 Set up and verify your email for automatic email delivery . You can find step-by-step instructions on how to verify your sender email address in our dedicated email setup article.
887 Integrate with Twilio for SMS . If you wish to send invoices via SMS, you'll need to follow the instructions provided in our Twilio setup guide.

Additional Features for Premium Subscribers

Ģ CC Recipients : You can copy multiple recipients on your invoice emails. Just check the box labeled " Include additional email (CC) " and enter one or more email addresses in the provided fields. You can even save these email addresses to the customerÆs profile by checking the " Add email to profile " box.
Ģ Billing Email: Want to send the invoice to a separate billing address? Simply check the box labeled "Billing Email" and input the appropriate email address.
Ģ Attach Invoice as PDF: If you'd like to attach a PDF version of the invoice to the email, toggle the switch next to "Attach invoice as PDF" to Yes.

How to Send Invoices Manually
If you're not a Premium Subscriber, invoices can still be sent manually via email or SMS. Here's how:

888 After creating the invoice, you can choose to send it via email or SMS by selecting the respective options.
889 If you prefer, you can also send the invoice link directly to your customer. This allows them to view and pay the invoice at their convenience. Click here for detailed instructions on how to send the invoice link.



Revert a cash/check charge
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/revert-a-cash-slash-check-charge

Revert a cash/check charge
Learn how to revert a cash/check charge in the "All Charges" section.

Last updated on 24 Oct, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How to revert a cash/check charge

Revert a Cash/Check Charge
WeÆve introduced the ability to revert bookings paid via cash/check back to a " Pending " status, allowing admins to reattempt charges or make booking adjustments more easily.

If you need to revert a cash/check charge for whatever reason, go to:

Bookings > Booking Charges > All Charges

Locate the charge you would like to revert in the list below. Then, click the down arrow button located under the " Actions " column on the far right and select " Revert to pending ".

A pop-up window will appear, asking if you want to revert this cash/check payment. Select "Yes " to proceed. The booking charge will be reverted back to the " Completed " state where it can be found in the Pending charges section.



Add card link feature
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/add-card-link-feature

Add card link feature
Learn how to utilize the add card link feature by sending clients an SMS and/or email to securely add their card.

Last updated on 03 Feb, 2025

In this article, you will learn how to:
Ģ Enable and edit the "Add card link" notification templates
Ģ Locate the "Add card link" feature
Ģ Send the add card link to customers

The Add Card Link Feature
The "Send add card link" feature is a Premium feature that enables customers to add a card to their profile from various sections without logging in.

If a customer is uncomfortable providing their credit card information over the phone or wants to add a new card quickly without having to sign into their BookingKoala dashboard, the link can be sent by admin or staff during the booking process.

The "Add Card Link" can be sent via email, SMS, or both.

If you have multiple locations, the customer will select their location from a drop-down field, and then they can enter their card information into the field below. In many cases, the collection of card information is expedited by things like Google and Apple Pay.

Enable and Edit the Add Card Link Templates

To enable and preview what the actual "Add Card Link" notifications will look like, please go to: Settings > Notifications > Email/SMS > Customer tab > Account section

At the bottom of the "Account" section is the "Add card" template. Please ensure that the template is toggled to "Enabled" so the system can use it for your Add Card Link emails and SMS.

To edit the template, click the pencil icon/"Edit" button to the right of the template's description.

From here, you can apply master templates, add shortcodes, edit the text and content in the email body, and more.
Ģ Click here to learn more about master templates.
Ģ Click here to learn more about using shortcodes.
Ģ Click here to learn more about how to edit email notifications.
Ģ Click here to learn more about how to edit SMS notifications.

Locating the Send Add Card Link Buttons

The Send "Add card" link button can be located throughout the BookingKoala platform.

890 Go to any booking and click the "Edit"/"Reschedule" button. The "Send "Add card" link" button will be located along the right side of the page with the other action buttons.
891 In Calendar View, click on a booking to preview its details and locate the "Send "Add card" link" button at the bottom of the booking alongside the other action buttons.
892 Go to any customer's profile from the Customers section and then click on their Profile. Scroll down to the billing section to use the "Send "Add card" link" button.

Sending the "Add Card" Link

Once you've navigated to one of these locations, click on the button to open the Send Add Card Link popup window.

893 The topmost field allows you to add up to 200 characters worth of text to send along with the link to the customer.
894 Copy the Add Card Link URL to manually send it to the client.
895 Each link can be used once and then it will expire. If you need to send a new link, click the refresh button.
896 Decide whether you would like to send the link as an email, an SMS, or both.
897 Once you are ready, click the blue "Send" button to send the link the the customer.



Using the customer import tool
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/using-the-customer-import-tool

Using the customer import tool
Learn how to import customer information for faster migration and onboarding.

Last updated on 03 Feb, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you:
Ģ Locate the import tool in the system
Ģ Create and upload information from a .CSV file
Ģ Undo an import
Ģ Send an invitation to create a password for newly imported contacts
Ģ Connect customer payment processor accounts using the import tool
Ģ Troubleshoot the common errors while uploading/importing the customer/contact data

Whether you are switching systems or are a new business with lots of leads, our import tool is a convenient way to upload information and speed up the migration or onboarding process. We also have an export tool so you can download a file containing customer, provider, and lead information that has been stored in your account.

Where to Find the Import Tool
The import tool is associated with two different sections: one can be used to upload customer data, and the other is for uploading provider data. To find it, you will need to go to your main menu and click:
Go to Customers > Customers > Import

Getting Started
To begin the process of importing your information, you will first click the blue ōImportö button at the top of the screen.

Preparing Your File
After making your selection, you will land on the import page.
WeÆd recommend that before you search for your file, please click the ōSample File For Customersö to see all of the fields BookingKoala supports. If you do not already have a file, you may also use this template to create one by adding new rows and filling them out as needed.

Please ensure that regardless of where your file originates, it will need at minimum the three following columns: first name, last name, and email address. These three components are required for all customers and providers. If they are missing from your file, you may not be able to have a successful import.

You will also need to ensure that the extension is .CSV. or If it does not have this extension, you can use your spreadsheet builder to ōSave Asö or export your list in this format. Once these aspects have been verified, you will need to click the ōBrowseö button to select the proper file, and then click the ōNextö button below.

If you would like to connect the customers' payment processor IDs as well, you will need to create a new column in the .CSV file with the heading "Payment Processor ID".

Please note: Make sure to check the ID when copying and pasting it to the corresponding customer. If you connect the wrong ID to the wrong customer account, the card on file could be used by the incorrect owner.

The Import Process
Once youÆve uploaded your file, you will need to match or ōMapö the imported fields with those in BookingKoala. You can map whichever fields of information you would like to be imported.

Some of your fields may be labeled differently than BookingKoalaÆs. For example, BookingKoala has ōzip codeö as a field whereas you may have something like ōzipö or ōpostal codeö. You can map your field with the closest corresponding field in our system. You can also leave most of the BookingKoala fields empty if you do not have those in your file, or if you are not interested in importing that specific information.

Once you have mapped your fields, you will click ōImportö. A green system notification will let you know the import process has begun.

While your file is being imported, you will be brought to the ōImport Historyö section. Here you can view new and past imports and their corresponding details:
Ģ Filename
Ģ Total records within the file
Ģ Total records inserted into your BookingKoala account
Ģ Error count of failed records
Ģ Type of import
Ģ Import creation date and time
Ģ Import Status

To the far right, you will see the importÆs status. While the system is finalizing the import, the label will read ōProcessingö. You can click the orange ōReloadö button to check for an updated status.

New Features
898 Automated synchronization of campaign contacts during import will be based on the campaign contacts limit and audience setup.
Ģ If a merchant has an existing limit for campaign contacts, the imported users will be automatically synced to the campaigns within their defined audience.
Ģ However, if the merchant has reached their limit for synced users, the system will still allow users to be imported, but automatic syncing to campaigns will not occur in this case.
Ģ Additionally, if the merchant has some campaign contact limits remaining but the queued import exceeds that limit, automated syncing will also not function.
Regardless of the campaign contacts limit, the system will always import the users into the main system.

899 Phone (+) is specifically intended for customer imports only. It allows for the inclusion of additional phone numbers based on a revised format. If the additional phone numbers in your file do not comply with BookingKoalaÆs acceptable format, you can enter the corresponding user's additional phone number into the Phone (+) field. This number will then be treated as an additional phone number for your imported record. Please ensure that you check the format of the Phone (+) before using it.
900 Notification Settings Update:
The notification settings have been revised. During the import process, you can now choose whether to enable email and SMS notifications for the users included in your uploaded import file for the BookingKoala system.
Please ensure that the default notification preferences are taken into account based on your selections. There are two options for notification settings: one for email and the other for SMS.
Ģ If you choose to enable email and/or SMS notifications, please make sure that valid email addresses and/or phone numbers are provided for those users. If such contact information is not available, the notification settings will not be activated.
Ģ If you choose not to enable email or SMS notifications for your imported users, then notifications will be disabled for those users according to your preference.

Troubleshooting
Once your import has been completed, you will see a green ōCompleteö status after refreshing. This indicates that all data (or as much as possible) has been imported. Next to this, there is a "Learn More" link.
Ģ When this link is green, it indicates that all data has been fully imported.
Ģ If the link is orange, your import is complete but some information was not able to be imported.
Ģ When the link is red, the import was unsuccessful.

When the icon is orange or red, you can click the link to view a more detailed record of what failed.

There are several possible reasons why user information could not be imported, including:
Ģ Email or phone number field is empty.
Ģ Email or phone number field is not valid.
Ģ Email or phone number already exists in the system.
Ģ Use of special characters in the first or last name.

Any users that could not be imported will be listed in a file you can download so you can look at the original file to add or modify the corresponding entries.

Undo an Upload
If you have accidentally uploaded the wrong file altogether, you can click "Options" and then ōUndo importö to undo the import. This will permanently delete the fileÆs contents from your system.

Send an Invitation to Create a Password
If you would like to invite the people in your file to create an account with your BookingKoala platform, you can click Options then "Send Email".

From here, you can mass email all new imports with the ōNew Accountö email and even include a note for their reference. You can click ōSend Emailö when you are ready, or ōPreviewö to see what the email will look like first.

Please be sure that the "New Account" email template is enabled under the Notifications section. To check, please go to:
Settings > Notifications > Emails > Provider

Click the red ōDisabledö button to re-enable the notification. You can also click the pencil icon to edit the general message for this email as well.

Connect Customer Payment Processor Accounts
In the customer import area, you have the additional option of connecting your customerÆs payment processor account if you have imported their customer ID.

Stripe
You can find a customer's Stripe ID in your by searching their name or email address under the "Customers" section. Click on their account to view their dashboard. The Stripe ID is located in the "Details" section just beneath their email address in the left-side menu. This code will always begin with "cus_".

Paste the Stripe ID into a separate column labeled "Payment Processor ID" in your file. Make sure there are no extra spaces or characters as this may result in a failed import.

Troubleshoot the common errors while uploading/importing the customer/contact data
Please use the following steps to try and identify what issue(s) are causing your file not to upload the contact data properly.

901 Download the Sample file:
It is recommended that you click on the "Sample File For Team" or "Sample File for Individuals" before uploading your file so that you can get a better sense of all the fields that BookingKoala supports. In case you do not already have a .CSV file, you may also download this template and use it to create one by adding new rows and filling them in as needed.
902 Check the format of the file you are uploading/importing:
Ģ If the format of the file is NOT ".CSV" or .XLSX, please save the file in ".CSV" or .XLSX format and then try to upload the file.
Ģ If the format of the file is ".CSV", proceed to the next step.

903 If the file format is ".CSV" or ".XLSX", and you get the below error message:
Ģ This error means that sometimes while uploading the file, the column name has some extra characters like spaces or commas which creates an issue for the system to read the file. If this error appears, it is recommended to re-save the file as a ".CSV" file and then re-try uploading the file.

904 If you still get the error:
Please email your file and the error screenshot tosupport@bookingkoala.com seeking further assistance.



Recurring booking invoice per industry
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/recurring-booking-invoice-per-industry

Recurring booking invoice per industry
Learn how industry-specific invoices work and how to create them.

Last updated on 06 Aug, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you learn:
Ģ How booking invoices can be created with specific and all industries.
Ģ How you can create booking invoices with preferred booking statuses.

Overview
You can effortlessly create booking recurring invoices tailored to specific industries or applicable to all industries. Additionally, you have the flexibility to generate booking invoices with statuses of your choice, including completed, declined, upcoming, or any combination of these options. To further improve clarity and transparency, we have also introduced customizable options for displaying invoice details. You can choose specific parameters that will allow for a detailed breakdown of the booking invoice summary, providing both you and your clients with much greater insight.

Select Industry

Select whether you want to create an invoice with all or specific industries.

Specific Industry

To create invoices with specific industries:

Select the specific option. Select the preferred industries from the options below to select industry, you can also select multiple industries to create combinations of different industries. Once selected click on the Load booking(s) button.

This will display all your recurring schedules of selected industry(s). You can view the bookings of each schedule by clicking on View all option. To create an invoice, check the boxes for the schedules you want to include. You will be able to select a maximum of 5 schedules at a time.

You can click on the Load More button to load more schedules(if any).

Select Booking Status

Now you can select booking statuses for specific industries & for all industries recurring invoices.

Choose the booking status by checking the box for the bookings you want to include in the invoice. For example, if you select the "Completed" status, only completed bookings will be added to the recurring invoice.

Form params

If you are creating a booking invoice, you can select which form parameters and price parameters the customer will view on the invoice by clicking on the plus sign icon next to the Form Parameters section. To add form and price parameters in the booking table click on ōWhat do you want to show in the booking table?ö and check the box next to each parameter to display in the booking table.

Adding parameters in booking invoice summary

To select what parameters you want to show during the booking invoice summary breakdown, click on ōWhat do you want to show in the booking invoice summary?ö and check the box next to each parameter to display the parameter in the booking invoice summary.

Drag and drop booking table and booking summary

To rearrange the parameters added in the booking table, just click and hold the column you wish to move, drag it to your preferred position, and then release the mouse button.

To reorder the booking invoice summary, just click and hold the custom field you want to move, drag it to your desired position, and release the mouse button.

Resize your custom table from the theme builder

To adjust the column sizes on your custom invoice table, simply click and hold the blue border of the column you want to resize. Then, drag it to your preferred width and release the border when you're done.

Introduced Notifications for Skipped Invoices & Ended Recurring Invoice Schedules

WeÆve added new notifications to keep you informed about important updates in your invoicing process.

Ģ Skipped Invoice Notification: Now, in a recurring booking invoice if the system does not find any bookings and skips an invoice, it will send a notification to the admin so that they are aware of the situation.
Ģ Ended Recurring Invoice Schedule Notification: The system will notify the admin in case a recurring booking invoice schedule is ended, either because no bookings were found or due to the cancellation of the recurring booking schedule.

You have to enable the notification template from Settings > Notification > Emails > Admin > Invoice.



Edit Custom Paid Invoice
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/edit-custom-paid-invoice

Edit Custom Paid Invoice
Need to make changes to a paid custom invoice? This article walks you through how to easily edit it, step by step.

Last updated on 06 Aug, 2025

Overview
Ģ With this new feature, you can edit details on a paid custom invoice directly from your dashboard without needing to issue a new invoice. Whether itÆs correcting line items, enhancing the customizations, updating the invoice summary, and many moreŚthis feature gives you the flexibility to keep your financial records accurate and professional, even after payment has been completed.
Ģ This enhancement is designed to streamline post-payment adjustments, reduce friction in your invoicing process, and give you more control over your business documentation.

Edit Paid Custom Invoice
To edit the paid custom invoice, please visit Bookings > Booking Charges > Invoices

Ģ Click on the " View " button to edit the paid custom invoice.
Ģ Click on the pencil icon if you want to edit your paid invoice. Once you click on the button, your invoice will be editable. As soon as you make the changes, you can save them changes by clicking on the Save & continue button .
Ģ If any changes were made, a pop-up will be initiated on Save & continue , which gives you flexibility to select whether you want to send a notification to the customer or not.
Ģ Similarly, if you want your changes to be synced with a third party, then check the checkbox " Sync to third party " given on the popup.
Ģ Finally, by clicking the "Update Invoice" button, you will be able to update your paid invoice.
