How to build a new leads funnel
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-build-a-new-leads-funnel

How to build a new leads funnel
What is a lead funnel?
A lead funnel is the series of steps it takes to convert a lead into a paying customer. A lead is any person with awareness of your business with the potential to purchase goods or services from you. The steps you take to nurture your leads will be reflected in the steps of your funnel.

Examples:
Ģ Automotive detailing company: Capture leads from a Spring promotion form, add them to a Spring Promotion funnel, and detail steps to contact the customer (provide quote, tell about savings, follow up).
Ģ Cleaning company: Import one-time customers into a funnel, create campaigns to gauge interest in recurring services, and target them with a special deal.

Funnels are a helpful way to keep these processes organized and efficient.

Add a New Leads Funnel
Navigation Path
Go to Leads Module > "Funnels" tab (along the top) > "Add" button (right side) > "Add new funnel".

Step-by-Step Instructions & Settings
1038 Name the funnel: Create a new name for the funnel.
1039 Assess columns: The three default columns that come with every funnel are Name, Details, and Won/Lost.
1040 Add a new column (Optional):
? Click on the "Add New Column" button in the top right corner.
? A pop-up window will open to enter column details:
Ģ Column name: Enter a title for the new column.
Ģ Tooltip text: Add a description of the column's purpose to be displayed as a tooltip.
Ģ Enable email automation?: Select "Yes" and choose an audience to add the lead to trigger an automated email campaign.
Ģ Select Custom field type: Select whether you want to add a single or group of custom fields to your column.
Ģ Select custom fields to add to this column: Select one or more custom fields to track progress or data related to this step of the funnel.
? Click the blue "Save" button to add the column to the funnel.

Funnel Settings
Below the columns, there are several Settings that pertain to the funnel:

1041 "How should the system update lead statuses that haven't been updated in a specific number of days?"
? Select the number of days (or immediate) from the first field.
? Select one of the following options for each query:
Ģ Remove from funnel
Ģ Do nothing
Ģ Mark them won
Ģ Mark them lost
Ģ Mark them other
1042 Specify booking actions based on frequency
? If "Yes": You will have two tabs for your settings: one for one-time and one for recurring.
? If "No": You will have a single set of settings for booking actions regardless of frequency.
1043 "What action should be taken if the lead books a service?"
? Select whether the lead should be marked as "Won", "Remove" it from the funnel, or if the funnel should "Do nothing".
? If you select "Won", there are additional settings:
Ģ "Do you want to override the revenue amount with the booking amount?"
? Select "Yes" to override the manually entered revenue amount with the booking total.
? Select "No" to use the manually entered revenue amount instead.
Ģ If "Yes" to the previous question: "Do you want to consider booking reschedule/cancellation amount in revenue?"
? Select "Yes" to consider the booking rescheduling or cancellation amount in the revenue.
? Select "No" if you do not want to consider these in the revenue.
Ģ "What action should be taken if someone cancels a booking?"
? Select "Lost" if you would like the lead marked as "lost" if they cancel a booking.
? Select "Move to funnel" if you would like the lead to be moved to a specific funnel if they cancel a booking.
1044 "Should leads inside this funnel be added to a campaign list?"
? Allows you to add leads from this funnel to one or more audience lists for your campaigns.
? Select "Yes" to pick which audiences will have the leads added.
? Select "No" if you do not want to add this funnel's leads to any audience lists.
1045 "Do you want to override the source of work and add your own for this funnel?"
? Pertains to the source from which the leads in this funnel originate.
? Select "Yes" to override the leads' source of work with a specific source whenever they are added to this funnel. All leads added to the funnel will be updated to whatever source of work you have selected from the field below.
? Select "No" if you would not like to override the source of work for leads added to this funnel. Leads will come in with whatever source of work was selected when the lead was added to the system.

Final Step: Once finished making selections, click the blue "Update" button at the bottom right side of the page to update your funnel. A green system notification will confirm your funnel has been updated successfully.

Duplicate an Existing Funnel
Navigation Path
Go to Leads Module > "Funnels" section > Select the funnel you would like to duplicate from the Funnels menu along the top left side > Click the "Add" button > Select "Duplicate Funnel".

Step-by-Step Instructions
1046 A pop-up window will appear, asking if you are certain you would like to duplicate this funnel.
1047 Enter the name of the new funnel into the field below.
1048 Click the blue "Duplicate" button.
1049 A green system notification will confirm that the new funnel has been duplicated successfully.
1050 The new funnel can be accessed from the Funnels drop-down menu.



How to add a lead
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-add-a-lead

How to add a lead
Learn how to add a new lead to your funnel(s).

Last updated on 26 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to add a new lead to the Leads Module

If you are looking to import or sync multiple leads into the system at once, click here.

Add a New Lead
There are a few different places you can add a new lead inside the Leads Module. First, go to the Leads tab along the top of the page and then click the Add New Lead button on the right side of the screen.

It is also possible to add a new lead from the Funnels tab by clicking on the Add button and selecting Add New Lead.

This will prompt the system to open an Add New Lead pop-up where you can enter the first name, last name, email address, and phone number of the lead.

In addition, you can also add tags to the lead using the Add Tag field.

If you would like to add the lead to one or more funnels, check the box next to " Would you like to add the lead to the funnels? " and then select which funnels to add the lead to.

At the bottom of the pop-up, there is a Notes section where you can add any pertinent notes about the lead to the file.

To finish adding the lead, click the blue Add button at the bottom right corner of the pop-up.

A green system notification will confirm that the lead has been saved successfully, and you will now see the lead tagged with a " New " tag under the Leads section.



How to filter and export leads
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-filter-and-export-leads

How to filter and export leads
Learn how to filter leads in the leads dashboard and how to export leads from BookingKoala.

Last updated on 25 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to filter leads in the leads dashboard
Ģ How to export leads

Filtering Leads
Leads can be filtered using several different options on the leads dashboard. To begin, click on the Leads section at the top of the page.

There are four basic filters across the top of this page: lead type, form, source, and date.

Ģ Lead type allows you to view all leads or just new leads.
Ģ Forms allow you to view leads associated with a specific form.
Ģ Original source allows you to view the leads by the source they came from.
Ģ Date allows you to view leads according to the date in which they were submitted.
Ģ Document status refers to the status of documents that have been sent to the leads. You can select from the following options: All, Sent, Progress, Declined, Completed, and Canceled.

In addition to these five basic filters, it is also possible to create your own filters using the Advanced Filters option. To begin, click the Advanced Filters button and select the blue Add Filters button.

Next, select one of the following categories to filter by a lead property: work progress, lead info, custom fields, and action forms.

For example, to see all leads that have been marked as "won", one would select the work progress category, click on Won lost status, check the box next to "is any of", then select won, and click apply filter.

Exporting Leads
To export your leads, use whatever filters you'd like to apply along the top of the leads page, then click the white Export button.

A pop-up will appear, asking if you would like the file format to be in .CSV or .XLSX. Once you've selected the filter type, click the blue Export button and the system will automatically email the file once it has been completed.



Lead reports
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/lead-reports

Lead reports
Learn how to read the reports in your leads dashboard.

Last updated on 25 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Which reports are available in the lead reports section
Ģ How to filter reports

Lead Reports
Inside the Leads Module, you can view your reports from the dashboard by going to the Reports tab.

There are three tabs at the top of the Reports page:
1051 The General tab contains reports related to your leads in BookingKoala.
1052 The Facebook Ad tab contains reports on any Facebook ads you have connected to Bookingkoala via the Facebook leads integration
1053 The Google Ad tab contains reports on any Google ads you have connected to BookingKoala via the Google leads integration

General Reports

The general reports can be filtered by:
Ģ Lead owner
Ģ Date
Ģ Funnel(s)
Ģ Forms
Ģ Sources
Ģ UTM Parameters

Data you can check in each report includes:
Ģ Leads - The total number of leads added during a specified period of time
Ģ Won - The total number of leads that were successfully converted into customers.
Ģ Revenue (Won) - The total amount of revenue resulting from successfully converted customers.
Ģ Lost - The total number of leads that were not successfully converted into customers.
Ģ Revenue (Lost) - The total amount of potential revenue lost with these leads.
Ģ Others - The number of leads that were neither lost nor won.
Ģ Revenue (Potential) - The total amount of potential revenue related to the "Others" category.
Ģ Work progress leads - The number of leads added to the selected funnel(s) with their statuses like "won", "lost", "other", "abandoned", etc.
Ģ Top lead sources - The number of leads originating from various sources with "Won" cases only. This includes the difference between this and the last session as well as the conversion rate.
Ģ Device category - The number and type of devices from which leads are generated
Ģ Browser type - The number and type of browser used by the leads.
Ģ Operating system - The number and type of operating system leads are using
Ģ Screen resolution - The screen resolution associated with the leads.
Ģ UTM Parameters



How to build a leads form
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-build-a-leads-form

How to build a leads form
Learn how to build lead forms to capture leads in the Leads Module.

Last updated on 25 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ How to build a new leads form
Ģ How to manage a lead form's settings
Ģ How to embed a lead form

Add a New Lead Form
A lead form is a form that collects information from potential customers for your business.

There are six different types of options for building a new form.

Ģ Embedded form - Build an integrated form for your website, seamlessly embedding it into your web pages.
Ģ Standalone page - Generate a shareable form on a dedicated webpage, accessible through a provided link.
Ģ Pop-up box - Integrate a centrally positioned form that pops up on your webpage for user convenience.
Ģ Dropdown banner - Implement a screen-top form that descends elegantly, offering a user-friendly interaction.
Ģ Slide in left box - Add a form sliding in from the left side, enhancing user engagement seamlessly.
Ģ Slide in right box - Introduce a visually appealing form sliding in gracefully from the right side of the screen.

To build a new leads form, go to the Forms section at the top of the page, click on the Forms tab on the right side of the page, and then click the blue Create New button.

Next, select which type of form you would like to build.

Once you've selected the form type, click the blue Next button at the bottom of the pop-up to provide a name for your form, then click Save .  You will be redirected to the form builder.

Form Properties
Along the right side column, you will see options for the page style, form style , and custom style .

Ģ The page style section contains options for the page's color schemes, background color, and/or background image.
Ģ The form style section contains options to change the color, size, and font used in the form's buttons, container, header, input, labels, paragraphs, sub-labels, and tool tips.
Ģ The custom style section contains a box where custom CSS can be entered to modify the appearance of the form.  After the CSS is pasted into the box, click the blue Save button to apply the changes.

Form Elements
Predefined Elements

All forms require 4 pieces of information: First Name , Last Name , Email , and Phone Number . This ensures that your leads are properly linked to their profile in the Leads Dashboard. These elements are part of the 8 predefined elements that can be added to your forms.  The others include:

Ģ Address - Capture an address.
Ģ Gender - capture the lead's gender identity
Ģ Profile Picture - capture a profile picture of the lead
Ģ Company Name - capture the lead's company name
Ģ Terms & Conditions - capture the lead's agreement to your terms and conditions of the form

In addition to the predefined elements, there are also basic and advanced elements that can be added to the form builder.

Basic Elements

There are a variety of different Basic Elements that can be added to your form from the left-side menu.

Ģ Header - Inserts a header and optional subheader
Ģ Label - Inserts a "label" style line of text
Ģ Paragraph - Inserts a "paragraph" style box of text
Ģ Divider - Inserts a divider line
Ģ Text Input - Inserts text and a small box for text input
Ģ Number - Inserts text and a box for number input
Ģ Multi-line Input - Inserts text and a large, multi-line box for text input
Ģ Dropdown - Inserts text and a drop-down box for input.
Ģ Radio Button - Inserts text and radio buttons to select one option
Ģ Multiple Choice - Inserts text and multiple-choice boxes to select one or more option

Advanced Elements

Below these, there are also several Advanced Elements that you can use if applicable.

Ģ Email - Insert an additional email address field
Ģ Phone - Insert an additional phone number field
Ģ Full Name - Insert an additional name field
Ģ Address - Insert an additional address field
Ģ File Upload - Insert a file upload box
Ģ Video - Insert a video
Ģ Date Picker - Insert a date box
Ģ Time - Insert an hours/minutes box
Ģ Rating - Insert a rating out of 5 stars box
Ģ Image - Insert an image
Ģ Signature - Insert a signature collected box
Ģ Captcha - Insert a CAPTCHA box
Ģ Range - Insert a range bar graphic
Ģ Plus/Minus - Insert a plus/minus box

Adding New Elements

To add a new element to the form, drag and drop the element name from the left side panel into the main form body.

Editing Elements

These elements can be edited by hovering over the section with your cursor and clicking the edit icon .

This will launch the edit options in the right-side menu.

Rearranging Elements

It is also possible to rearrange the order of elements in the form by clicking and holding the rearrange icon and dragging the element into a new position.

Archiving, Activating, and Deleting Elements

To archive or hide an element on the form, click the file box icon and select Yes to the pop-up message " Are you sure you want to archive this element? " This means that only the admin will be able to see the element when viewing the unpublished version of the form.

To activate an element, click on the up arrow icon in the top right corner of the action icons and select Yes to the pop-up message, " Are you sure you want to restore this element as active? "

If you'd like to delete an element, you must first archive it and then you will be able to access the trash can icon used to delete elements.  A pop-up will ask " Are you sure you want to delete this element? " Select the blue Delete button to proceed.

Customizing Elements

Whenever you click on one of your form's elements, the content editor will open on the right side of the screen. Depending on the type of element, there are multiple options to help customize how your quiz will function.

Whenever there is the option to enter text, the element menu will display a text editor box. Any writing inside the box can be formatted using word editing tools. Please be sure to highlight the text before applying a different format. The Label box will most likely contain your question.

You can also add an optional Sub Label or Sub Header to many elements. This will be displayed below the answers.

For any elements with a text input field, you can use a Placeholder to provide an example or hint of what information you are looking for.

The Address element can be formatted as a single line or Multiple Fields . You can also customize which address fields you would like to use.

The Date Picker can be used to collect today's date, a lead's birth date, or any other date information.  You can set the Default to:

Ģ None - leave the date field empty
Ģ Current - populates the field with the date the contract is submitted
Ģ Custom - set a specific date to display in the field

You can also enable Age Verification and set a Minimum Age to complete the form.  The date options can be further restricted to just Past Dates or Future Dates , and the date Format can be customized as well.

Use the Default setting to select a default answer to select.

Insert a Tooltip next to the question to provide additional context or clarify the type of answer you are looking for.

For any question that had more than one option to select, you will see an Options box. Type in each option that you'd like to display for this question, separated by commas (,).

If you would like to make a question mandatory, find the Required section and toggle it to Enabled . You can also enter an Error message to display when users do not respond to mandatory questions.

You also have the option to make a question Read Only so the user cannot select an answer, or Hidden entirely so only the admin can see it.

For elements that ask for a written response, click on Enable Charlimit (character limit) to limit how many characters the lead can use in their answer.

You can insert a File Upload box to collect any documents from your lead. This element can accept the following file formats: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLXS, CSV, TXT, RTF, HTML, ZIP, MP3, WMA, MPG, FLV, AVI, JPG, JPEG, PING, and GIF.  You can customize which files types can be uploaded by editing the File Types box. Please make sure that each value is separated by a comma (,) and no spaces.

The File Size Limit can also be restricted by setting a Minimum and/or Maximum size in KB. You can also enable " Multiple" for multiple files to be uploaded in this section and set a limit for the Number of Files .

Form Settings
In addition to the form builder, there is also a form settings tab. To view the options, click Settings next to the Builder tab.

The Form Settings section has four tabs.

Form Settings

The Form Settings section contains several different areas to better customize how your forms behave.

At the top of the page, you can edit your Form name, Form description, and Form page URL .

You can also specify which funnel(s) will collect information from this form.

The Form status can be switched to " Archive " if you no longer want to use this form, or " Un-archive " to make it live again.

The Google reCAPTCHA key option allows you to insert CAPTCHA boxes into your forms. You will need to generate a Google reCAPTCHA API key to set it up. Click here to learn how to generate the API key.

Google autofill can be enabled or disabled depending on if you would like the form to automatically suggest addresses based on previous searches and location bounds.

A Form tracking code can be entered into the box to help with your analytics.

You can also set a Default thank you message after the lead completes the form.  There are options to create a Custom "thank you" message, use an External link , or select an existing " Thank you" page from another form.

If you create a new Custom "thank you" message, you can also insert a Page Tracking Code from Facebook Pixel or Google Analytics, for example, to see who views the page.

At the bottom of the page, click the blue Save Draft button to update your draft with any changes.

Conditions

This section controls any conditional logic inside your form.

Create A New Field Condition

Click the Show/Hide field button to add a new condition based on the input your lead enters.

Conditions are based on "if" statements to perform a certain action depending on the input or selection made.

IF: Begin by using the drop-down menu to select the field.

STATE: Select the "state" of the field. Depending on the type of input, the options are:

Ģ Is Equal To - The action will be performed if the input is equal to the lead's answer
Ģ Is Not Equal To - The action will be performed if the input is NOT equal to the lead's answer
Ģ Contains - The action will be performed if the input contains the correct value anywhere the lead's answer
Ģ Does Not Contain - The action will be performed if the input does NOT contain the correct value anywhere the lead's answer
Ģ Starts With - The action will be performed if the beginning of the value matches the lead's answer
Ģ Ends With - The action will be performed if the end of the value matches the lead's answer
Ģ Is Empty - The action will be performed if the field is left empty
Ģ Is Filled - The action will be performed if the field is filled

VALUE: Enter the value that the input will be compared to.

DO: Select the action that will be performed if the condition is met. The options are:

Ģ Show - The field will be shown
Ģ Hide - The field will be hidden

FIELD: Select the field that will be affected by the action.

Click the blue Save button to save the condition.

Create A New Email Condition

Click the Send email button to add a new condition based on the input your lead enters.

Conditions are based on "if" statements to perform a certain action depending on the input or selection made.

IF: Begin by using the drop-down menu to select the field.

STATE: Select the "state" of the field. Depending on the type of input, the options are:

Ģ Is Equal To - The action will be performed if the input is equal to the lead's answer
Ģ Is Not Equal To - The action will be performed if the input is NOT equal to the lead's answer
Ģ Contains - The action will be performed if the input contains the correct value anywhere the lead's answer
Ģ Does Not Contain - The action will be performed if the input does NOT contain the correct value anywhere the lead's answer
Ģ Starts With - The action will be performed if the beginning of the value matches the lead's answer
Ģ Ends With - The action will be performed if the end of the value matches the lead's answer
Ģ Is Empty - The action will be performed if the field is left empty
Ģ Is Filled - The action will be performed if the field is filled

VALUE: Enter the value that the input will be compared to.

SEND EMAIL TO: Enter the email address that the email will be sent to.

SUBJECT: Enter the subject of the email.

MESSAGE: Enter the message of the email.

Click the blue Save button to save the condition.

Emails

This section controls the emails that are sent when a lead form is submitted.

Admin Notification Email

This email is sent to the admin when a lead form is submitted.

You can edit the Subject and Message of the email.

You can also use Shortcodes to insert dynamic information into the email.

Click the blue Save button to save the email.

Lead Notification Email

This email is sent to the lead when a lead form is submitted.

You can edit the Subject and Message of the email.

You can also use Shortcodes to insert dynamic information into the email.

Click the blue Save button to save the email.

Integrations

This section controls the integrations that are connected to your lead form.

Mailchimp

You can connect your lead form to Mailchimp to automatically add leads to your Mailchimp audience.

Click the Connect button to connect your Mailchimp account.

ActiveCampaign

You can connect your lead form to ActiveCampaign to automatically add leads to your ActiveCampaign lists.

Click the Connect button to connect your ActiveCampaign account.

Zapier

You can connect your lead form to Zapier to automatically send leads to other apps.

Click the Connect button to connect your Zapier account.

Preview
To preview your form, click the Preview button at the top right corner of the page.

This will open a new tab with a preview of your form.

Saving and Publishing Your Form
To save your form, click the blue Save button at the top right corner of the page.

To publish your form, click the blue Publish button at the top right corner of the page.

Once your form is published, you can embed it on your website or share the link with your leads.



Lead tags
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/lead-tags

Lead tags
Learn how to add and manage lead tags in the Leads Module.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to manage your lead tags
Ģ How to add tags to your leads

Lead Tags
Lead tags are tags used to help better categorize and sort your leads. You can use tags to do things like:
Ģ Sort your leads by the industry they are interested in. If you are a cleaning company, you may have industries like commercial, residential, office, home, etc. that can be turned into tags.
Ģ Display your lead origin/source more readily. Leads come from all sorts of places: forms, Facebook, Twitter, Reddit, Google, etc. Sources are a great use of the tags feature.
Ģ Tag leads according to potential. You can use tags like "Very Interested", "Somewhat Interested", and "Not Interested" to gauge the customer's likelihood of using your company's services.

Your tags can be managed from the Tags section at the top of the Leads dashboard.

Tags are listed according to their display order and will also show you how many leads are associated with each tag.

To add a new tag, click the Add New button near the top right corner of the page.

Enter the name of the customer and the order of priority you would like for the tag, then click the blue Add button.

To edit an existing tag, click the Options button under the Actions column and select "Edit".

This will allow you to edit the tag's name and display order.

You can delete a tag by selecting Options and then Delete.

Only press delete if you are certain you do not want to use that tag anymore.

Tagging to Your Leads
To tag your leads, click on a lead's name anywhere in the leads module to visit that lead's profile. Here you can leave notes, check on work progress, and manage the tags attached to your lead.

Scroll down the side menu on the left until you view the Add Tag field. Type or select the tags from the drop-down menu below.



Lead settings
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/lead-settings

Lead settings
Learn where to manage your general lead settings and sources.

Last updated on 25 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to locate the settings for the leads module
Ģ Where to manage your lead sources

Lead Settings
The Leads Module settings section contains several different options to help you manage your leads. You can view these settings by clicking on Settings in the top right corner of the module and selecting the Settings option.

The first set of settings can be found in the General tab at the top of the page.

The first question pertains to when the "new" tag is displayed on a lead.

For the question, "How would you like to display the new tag?", you can select one of two options:
Ģ "Based on view" means the "new" tag will be displayed until that lead is opened.
Ģ "Based on days" allows you to select a period of 1 through 7 days in which the "new" tag will be displayed on that lead.

The next setting, "Would you like to enable owners for leads?" allows you to decide if you'd like to assign a staff member as the "owner" of a particular lead.
Ģ "Yes" will allow the admin/staff to assign an owner to a lead whenever it has been added to the system.
Ģ "No" means there will not be an option to assign an owner to any of the leads.

The following setting, "Would you like to enable owners for work progress?" allows you to decide if you would like to assign a staff member as the "owner" for work progress on a lead when they've been added to one or more funnels.
Ģ "Yes" will allow the admin/staff to assign a work progress owner once a lead has been added to one or more funnels. This helps track which staff member is working the lead.
Ģ "No" means the lead will not have an option for a work progress owner.

The final setting in the Settings section is to "Select a theme mode for leads."
Ģ Select "Light" to use the light theme for the leads dashboard.
Ģ Select "Dark" to use the dark theme for the leads dashboard.

Lead Sources
The second tab under the Settings section is the Sources tab.

If you select "Yes" for "Activate sources", you will have the option to use both default and custom sources to assign to your leads.

Default Sources The default sources that come with the Leads Module include:
Ģ Direct
Ģ Bing
Ģ Yahoo
Ģ Tiktok
Ģ Thumbtack
Ģ Thread
Ģ Twitter
Ģ Yelp
Ģ Instagram
Ģ Facebook
Ģ Google
Ģ Import
Ģ Manual
Ģ Other

Custom Sources In addition to the default sources, you have a direct source for your store's URLs.

You can also add your own custom sources by clicking on the Custom tab and using the Add New Source button.
Ģ Enter the Source name into the first field.
Ģ Enter the Source URL(s) into the second field.
Ģ Enable the source by clicking on the Disabled button to switch it to Enabled.
Ģ Click the "Save" button to add the custom source.

You can edit or delete custom sources by using the Edit and Delete options located beneath the Actions column under the Custom section.



Lead notifications
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/lead-notifications

Lead notifications
Learn how to manage notifications for email, SMS, and system alerts in the Lead Module.

Last updated on 27 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:

Ģ What types of lead notifications are available in the Lead Module
Ģ How to set up, enable/disable, and edit email notifications
Ģ How to set up, enable/disable, and edit SMS notifications
Ģ How to enable/disable and edit system alerts

Types of Lead Notifications
BookingKoala has built an extensive Notifications section to help automate lead communications from your business. Three types of notifications can be enabled for the Leads Module:

Ģ Email notifications
Ģ SMS (text) notifications
Ģ System alerts

You can manage your lead notifications by going to:

Leads > Settings > Notifications

Email Notifications

Email notifications are notifications that get sent out from BookingKoala to a user's email address. Whenever a certain action takes place inside the Leads Module, a corresponding email will be sent to the email address associated with the action.

Email notifications can be sent to leads as well as the admin/staff.

Add or Change the Send Out Email Address

By default, emails are sent out by whatever send out email address you set up per the instructions in this article. However, you can use this section at the top of the Emails tab to set up a specific email address to use for this purpose.

To set up or change the email address you use to send lead notifications, follow these steps:

1054 Enter a valid email address into the Default send out email field. If an email is already in the field, click the grey Change button and enter a new email into the box.

? Using a domain-based email address like "support@mybusiness.com" or "hello@cleaningservice.com" instead of a personal email address like "firstname.lastname@gmail.com" or "myfullname@yahoo.com" can help authenticate your email address so emails do not go into the spam folder. To learn more about how to prevent emails from being spammed or spoofed, click here.
1055 Click the blue Send verification email button. Check that email address's inbox for a verification email from Support@bookingkoala.com. Inside the email, there will be a link that you must click. Once you've clicked the link, you will be redirected to an Amazon Web Service (AWS) page that says " Congratulations! ". Once you see this, your email is now verified and you can begin sending out email notifications.
1056 Optionally, use a separate Reply-to email address by entering it into the field below.
1057 Enter in the Sender Name you want recipients to see.

? For example, your display name may be " Demo 2 LLC " if your company is called this.
1058 Click the blue Save Changes button to update your sender email address details.

A green system notification will appear in the top right corner of the page confirming you have updated/added your sender email address successfully.

Master Templates

Below the sender email address area, the Master Templates section allows you to create custom email templates to match your business's branding. You can update the existing default templates or add your own using the Add master template button.

Master templates can save you a lot of time when it comes to customizing the appearance of your emails. Once enabled, these can be applied to any other email template in the lead section to display custom headers, footers, and other sections.

To edit an existing master template, click on Options below the action column and select Edit.

To add your own custom master templates, click the blue Add master template button.

To apply a master template to your existing email notifications, select Options and click on Appy to email .

Click here to learn more about how to set up and use master templates for your email notifications.

Edit, Enable, or Disable Email Templates

Below the master templates section are the email templates. These templates are organized into three categories:

1059 Lead - Email notifications that are sent to the lead from your company.
1060 Admin - Email notifications that are sent to your admin/staff regarding leads.
1061 Canned response - Custom email templates your admin/staff can manually send to individual leads.

Each notification has an Enabled/Disabled button that can be switched on or off depending on your preference, the name and description of each notification will provide details as to when the notification is triggered, and the Edit button can be used to edit the content and appearance of the notification.

Almost all of the templates in BookingKoala make use of shortcodes to communicate details by customizing the information presented in the notification. You can insert shortcodes to further customize your notifications by clicking on the View shortcodes button at the top right corner of the page when you open a notification to edit it.

In addition to using the Save button to update the template, there are also buttons to Send a test email and Restore [template] to default if you'd like to start editing from the beginning again.

SMS Notifications

SMS notifications are included in the Growing and Premium Subscriptions .

SMS notifications are notifications that get sent out from BookingKoala to a user's phone number via text message through the use of a third-party integration called Twilio . Whenever a certain action takes place inside BookingKoala, a corresponding text will be sent to the phone number associated with the account.

To manage your SMS lead notifications, click on the SMS tab at the top of the Notifications page.

Add or Change the Send Out Phone Number

By default, emails are sent out by whatever send out phone number you set up per the instructions in this article. However, you can use this section at the top of the SMS tab to set up a specific Twilio account to use for this purpose.

To set up or change the Twilio account you use to send lead notifications, follow these steps:

1062 Enter your Twilio account ID into the first box. Your account ID will always begin with "AC" and then a long string of numbers and letters. You can find your account ID in your Twilio dashboard by going to Account > Manage Account > General Settings .
1063 API key - Copy and paste the Auth Token into the box that says " API Key ". In your Twilio dashboard, scroll down to the Account Info section.
1064 Phone number - Copy and paste your phone number into the third box.

? Make sure your phone number is entered exactly as it is pictured under " My Twilio phone number ". Enter " + " plus your country code followed by the rest of the phone number.

Ģ For example, " +15672299756 " is correct .
Ģ "15672299756" is incorrect.
Ģ "5672299756" is incorrect.
Ģ "+1(567)2299756" is incorrect.
Ģ "+1-567-229-9756"is incorrect.  

     You can also find your phone number by going to **Phone Numbers > Manage > Active Numbers** in the left-side menu in your Twilio dashboard.
1065 Once you have completed each field, click the blue " Save " button to connect the Twilio account to your Lead Module.

Edit, Enable, or Disable SMS Templates

Below the send out email section are the SMS templates. These templates are organized into three categories:

1066 Lead - SMS notifications that are sent to the lead from your company.
1067 Admin - SMS notifications that are sent to your admin/staff regarding leads.
1068 Canned response - Custom SMS templates your admin/staff can manually send to individual leads.

Like emails, each SMS notification has an Enabled/Disabled button that can be switched on or off depending on your preference, the name and description of each notification will provide details as to when the notification is triggered, and the Edit button can be used to edit the content of the notification.

Many of the SMS templates in BookingKoala make use of shortcodes to communicate details by customizing the information presented in the notification. You can insert shortcodes to further customize your notifications by clicking on the Insert shortcodes button at the top right corner of the page when you open a notification to edit it.

In addition to using the Save button to update the template, there are also buttons to Send a test SMS and Restore [template] to default if you'd like to start editing from the beginning again.

System Alerts

System alerts are included in the Growing and Premium Subscriptions .

System alerts are notifications that are displayed at the top-right corner of your dashboard whenever a new action has been taken by an admin, staff member, or lead.

Only admin and staff are able to receive and view system alerts. System alerts pertain to day-to-day operations, so they are only displayed from the backend of BookingKoala when logged into an admin or staff account.

Specifically, you can enable these alerts when a new lead is added to the system and when a new note has been added for an admin or staff member.

Edit, Enable, or Disable System Alerts

Like email and SMS notifications, each system alert has an Enabled/Disabled button that can be switched on or off depending on your preference, the name and description of each alert will provide details as to when the alert is triggered, and the Edit button can be used to edit the content of the alert.

In addition to the Save button, there also is a Restore to default button if you need to restore the alert to its original state.



Lead logs
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/lead-logs

Lead logs
Learn where to view system, email, and SMS logs for the Leads Module.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to locate the logs section for the Leads Module
Ģ Where to view the system logs
Ģ Where to view the email logs
Ģ Where to view the SMS logs

Lead Logs
There are three different types of logs in the Leads Module: system logs, email logs, and SMS logs. You can view a list of each type by going to the Settings section of the Leads dashboard and selecting Logs.

Ģ System logs display all of the edits and changes made in the system by you and your staff. Logs can be filtered by activities relating to leads, tags, sources, funnels, custom fields, forms, thank you pages, notifications, import/sync, and settings.
Ģ Email logs show all of the emails sent by the system, including those that have been rejected, bounced, complained, delivered, opened, and clicked. Email logs can also be filtered by email status. To learn how to set up email notifications for the Leads Module, click here.
Ģ SMS logs display all of the SMS sent by the system and can be filtered by date. To learn how to set up SMS notifications for the Leads Module, click here.



Leads module overview

Leads module overview
Learn about the BookingKoala Leads Module and how to use its features.

Last updated on 28 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you learn more about:
Ģ What features are included in the Leads Module
Ģ Where to locate the Leads Module
Ģ Which BookingKoala subscriptions include the Leads Module

How do I use the Leads Module?
The Leads Module is designed to help you gather leads, track progress, and cultivate leads into customers, employees, etc. Leads can be imported from another software or gathered via a customizable lead form, which allows visitors to submit a request for a quote or inquire more about your services, prospect providers to fill out an application or collect feedback from your customers. Once a lead has been gathered, you can track that lead's progress in a funnel, noting if the lead has booked a service, how much revenue is related to the lead, and other custom fields.
