Where to Locate the Leads Module
The Leads Module is located just below the Bookings button on the left side panel.

This button launches your leads dashboard.

Who has access to the Leads Module?
If you have the Starter or Growing Subscriptions, you can build out one form and collect/view leads from that one form.

To access the rest of the great features in the leads module, as well as the ability to make multiple lead forms, is only included in the Premium subscription.



How to build an action form
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-build-an-action-form

How to build an action form
Learn how to build action forms to automatically capture feedback and data in lead funnels in the Leads Module.

Last updated on 27 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to build a new action form
Ģ How to manage an action form's settings
Ģ How to connect an action form to a leads funnel

Add a New Action Form
An action form is a form that can be embedded into a funnel to automatically display depending on input that is entered into the funnel's steps. Action forms can be used by the staff and admin to collect internal notes and data on a lead that is a part of one or more funnels.

To build a new action form, go to the Forms section at the top of the page, click on the Action Forms tab on the right side of the page, and then click the blue Create New button.

A pop-up will ask you to create a name for the form. Once you enter the form name, click the blue Save button and you will be redirected to the form builder.

Form Properties
Along the right side column, you will see options for the page style, form style, and custom style.

Ģ The page style section contains options for the page's color schemes, background color, and/or background image.
Ģ The form style section contains options to change the color, size, and font used in the form's buttons, container, header, input, labels, paragraphs, sub-labels, and tool tips.
Ģ The custom style section contains a box where custom CSS can be entered to modify the appearance of the form. After the CSS is pasted into the box, click the blue Save button to apply the changes.

Form Elements
Unlike lead forms, action forms do not have predefined fields because they are already directly linked to the leads via the funnel. However, they contain the same 10 basic elements and 13 advanced elements to build your form from.

Basic Elements There are a variety of different Basic Elements that can be added to your form from the left-side menu.
Ģ Header - Inserts a header and optional subheader
Ģ Label - Inserts a "label" style line of text
Ģ Paragraph - Inserts a "paragraph" style box of text
Ģ Divider - Inserts a divider line
Ģ Text Input - Inserts text and a small box for text input
Ģ Number - Inserts text and a box for number input
Ģ Multi-line Input - Inserts text and a large, multi-line box for text input
Ģ Dropdown - Inserts text and a drop-down box for input.
Ģ Radio Button - Inserts text and radio buttons to select one option
Ģ Multiple Choice - Inserts text and multiple-choice boxes to select one or more option

Advanced Elements Below these, there are also several Advanced Elements that you can use if applicable.
Ģ Email - Insert an additional email address field
Ģ Phone - Insert an additional phone number field
Ģ Full Name - Insert an additional name field
Ģ Address - Insert an additional address field
Ģ File Upload - Insert a file upload box
Ģ Video - Insert a video
Ģ Date Picker - Insert a date box
Ģ Time - Insert an hours/minutes box
Ģ Rating - Insert a rating out of 5 stars box
Ģ Image - Insert an image
Ģ Signature - Insert a signature collected box
Ģ Captcha - Insert a CAPTCHA box
Ģ Range - Insert a range bar graphic
Ģ Plus/Minus - Insert a plus/minus box

Adding New Elements To add a new element to the form, drag and drop the element name from the left side panel into the main form body.

Editing Elements These elements can be edited by hovering over the section with your cursor and clicking the edit icon. This will launch the edit options in the right-side menu.

Rearranging Elements It is also possible to rearrange the order of elements in the form by clicking and holding the rearrange icon and dragging the element into a new position.

Archiving, Activating, and Deleting Elements To archive or hide an element on the form, click the file box icon and select Yes to the pop-up message "Are you sure you want to archive this element?" This means that only the admin will be able to see the element when viewing the unpublished version of the form.

To activate an element, click on the up arrow icon in the top right corner of the action icons and select Yes to the pop-up message, "Are you sure you want to restore this element as active?"

If you'd like to delete an element, you must first archive it and then you will be able to access the trash can icon used to delete elements. A pop-up will ask "Are you sure you want to delete this element?" Select the blue Delete button to proceed.

Customizing Elements Whenever you click on one of your form's elements, the content editor will open on the right side of the screen. Depending on the type of element, there are multiple options to help customize how your quiz will function.

Whenever there is the option to enter text, the element menu will display a text editor box. Any writing inside the box can be formatted using word editing tools. Please be sure to highlight the text before applying a different format. The Label box will most likely contain your question or a statement.

You can also add an optional Sub Label or Sub Header to many elements. This will be displayed below the answers.

For any elements with a text input field, you can use a Placeholder to provide an example or hint of what information you are looking for.

The Address element can be formatted as a single line or Multiple Fields. You can also customize which address fields you would like to use.

The Date Picker can be used to collect today's date, a lead's birth date, or any other date information. You can set the Default to:
Ģ None - leave the date field empty
Ģ Current - populates the field with the date the contract is submitted
Ģ Custom - set a specific date to display in the field

You can also enable Age Verification and set a Minimum Age to complete the form. The date options can be further restricted to just Past Dates or Future Dates, and the date Format can be customized as well.

Use the Default setting to select a default answer to select.

Insert a Tooltip next to the question to provide additional context or clarify the type of answer you are looking for.

For any question that had more than one option to select, you will see an Options box. Type in each option that you'd like to display for this question, separated by commas (,).

If you would like to make a question mandatory, find the Required section and toggle it to Enabled. You can also enter an Error message to display when users do not respond to mandatory questions.

You also have the option to make a question Read Only so the user cannot select an answer, or Hidden entirely so only the admin can see it.

For elements that ask for a written response, click on Enable Charlimit (character limit) to limit how many characters the lead can use in their answer.

You can insert a File Upload box to collect any documents from your lead. This element can accept the following file formats: PDF, DOC, DOCX, XLS, XLXS, CSV, TXT, RTF, HTML, ZIP, MP3, WMA, MPG, FLV, AVI, JPG, JPEG, PING, and GIF. You can customize which files types can be uploaded by editing the File Types box. Please make sure that each value is separated by a comma (,) and no spaces.

The File Size Limit can also be restricted by setting a Minimum and/or Maximum size in KB. You can also enable "Multiple" for multiple files to be uploaded in this section and set a limit for the Number of Files.

Form Settings
In addition to the form builder, there is also a form settings tab. To view the options, click Settings next to the Builder tab.

The Form Settings section has two tabs.

Form Settings At the top of the page, you can edit your Form name, Form status, and Google reCAPTCHA key.

The Google reCAPTCHA key option allows you to insert CAPTCHA boxes into your forms. You will need to generate a Google reCAPTCHA API key to set it up. Click here to learn how to generate the API key.

Below here is a list of all the funnels from which you can trigger this action form.

And, the corresponding custom field options with columns from where you want to trigger the action form.

So custom fields like "won" or "lost" will trigger the column "Won/Lost" to display an interactive form, or like "Done" and "Waiting" will trigger an action form while "Does not apply" will not.

Conditions This section controls any conditional logic inside your form.

Create A New Field Condition Click the Show/Hide field button to add a new condition based on the input your lead enters.

Conditions are based on "if" statements to perform a certain action depending on the input or selection made.

Ģ IF: Begin by using the drop-down menu to select the field.
Ģ STATE: Select the "state" of the field. Depending on the type of input, the options are:
? Is Equal To - The action will be performed if the input is equal to the lead's answer
? Is Not Equal To - The action will be performed if the input is NOT equal to the lead's answer
? Contains - The action will be performed if the input contains the correct value anywhere the lead's answer
? Does Not Contain - The action will be performed if the input does NOT contain the correct value anywhere the lead's answer
? Starts With - The action will be performed if the beginning of the value matches the lead's answer
? Doesn't Start With - The action will be performed if the beginning of the value does not match the lead's answer
? Ends With - The action will be performed if the ending of the value matches the lead's answer
? Doesn't End With - The action will be performed if the ending of the value does not match the lead's answer
? Is Empty - The action will be performed if the input box is left empty.
? Is Filled - The action will be performed if the input box is filled.
Ģ TARGET: If you are using a state that compares the input to another answer, select whether you want to use a value or another field to compare the input.
Ģ VALUE: Select a value/answer you created for this field for the comparison.
Ģ ANOTHER FIELD: Select a value/answer from a different field's value for the comparison.
Ģ DO: Select the type of action you want to occur when the condition is met.
? Hide - Hide another field if the condition is met
? Show - Show another field if the condition is met.
? Show Multiple - Show multiple fields if the condition is met.
? Hide Multiple - Hide multiple fields if the condition is met.
Ģ FIELD: Select the field(s) to show or hide.

After selecting each option, click the blue Save button to add your condition to this form.

Add Multiple IF Rules and Actions You can also add multiple rules or actions within a condition by clicking the blue "+" icon.

If there is more than one IF statement, you can select whether you want the actions to be performed if Any or All rules are met.

Where to View Existing ConditionsEvery condition you create will be shown in this section as well.
You can use the action buttons to Edit, Disable, or Delete existing conditions.

Saving and Publishing Your Form
Once you are satisfied with your form, you'll want to make sure it is saved into the system.

Save DraftClick Save Draft on the top-right corner of the builder to save a draft of your form.
You can find your saved drafts at any time by going to the Forms page.
Click the drop-down menu where it says Active and select Draft.

Save and PublishIf you are ready for your form to be used by leads, click the blue Save & Publish button to push it live.
You will see a green pop-up notification in the top right corner of the page that says Form saved & published successfully.



How to import leads
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-import-leads

How to import leads
Article Title
How to import leads

Overview
Learn how to import external leads from your BookingKoala account.
The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to import leads
Ģ How to sync leads from your BookingKoala account

Navigation Paths
Ģ To import leads from a file: Go to Settings > Imports
Ģ To check import status in system logs: Go to Leads > Settings > Logs > System Logs

Features and Step-by-Step Instructions
Import Leads
1069 Click on the Settings tab near the top right corner of the page and select Imports.
1070 Click on Start an Import.
1071 Click the blue "Upload File" button to select the file you would like to import.
1072 Specify which funnel(s) you would like to add the imported leads to (if any).
1073 Click the blue Next button to proceed.
1074 Match your data columns to the form fields so they can be imported and mapped properly.
1075 Once all columns have been mapped, click the blue Import button at the bottom right side of the page.
1076 Your import will begin processing immediately. You can view it in the Import History section just below the Import and Sync options.
1077 Click the orange Reload button to check on the progress of your import. The status will change from Processing to Complete.

Important Notes & Limitations for Importing Leads:
Ģ Only .CSV and .XLSX files are accepted.
Ģ Import limits:
? File size: 50MB
? Contacts: 50000 leads per day
Ģ You can download a sample file by clicking on the Sample file for lead link below the Import and Sync options to use as a reference for formatting.
Ģ Once complete, you can view total records, new records, leads added to funnels, and an error count.
Ģ To download a file of failed records, click the orange Learn More link below the Complete tag and select Download.
Ģ Reasons a record may fail to import:
? The email and phone numbers are empty.
? The email or phone number already exists.
? There are use of special characters in the first or last name.
? The email or phone number is not valid.
Ģ You can use action options to View lead(s) or Undo the import if you made an error.

How to Sync Leads
1078 Click on the Settings tab near the top right corner of the page and select Imports.
1079 Click the blue Sync from BookingKoala button beneath where it says Sync.
1080 From below the New Import tab, select one of the following options:
? All booked customers - Sync customers who presently have a booking with your company.
? Specific customers - Sync specific customers by name.
? Not booked customers - Sync customers who currently do not have any bookings with your company.

Sync Specific Customers
1081 Select the Specific customers option.
1082 Use the Select customers field to search and select customers by name, email address, or phone number.
1083 Choose which funnel(s) you would like to add the customers.
1084 Click the blue Import button to sync the data.
1085 A green system alert will confirm the import has begun. Check system logs later for completion.

Sync Not Booked or All Booked Customers: One Time
1086 Select All booked customers or Not booked customers.
1087 Choose the one time import option.
1088 Use the customer-created date field to select a date range for the import data.
1089 Choose which funnel(s) you would like to add the customers.
1090 Click the blue Import button to sync the data.

Sync Not Booked or All Booked Customers: Scheduled Import
1091 Select All booked customers or Not booked customers.
1092 Select the Scheduled import option.
1093 Add a name to the import (e.g., "Spring One-Time Customers").
1094 Select an import start date and import start time.
1095 Set Repeats options:
? Weekly on Monday
? Every 2 weeks on Saturday
? Monthly on the 15th of the month
? Monthly on the 28th of the month
? Use the Custom Schedule button to set your own repeating time frame.
1096 Decide which week to consider for the import (Previous or Next).
1097 Decide how many days to consider (1-7 days).
1098 Decide if you allow duplicate work progresses in the selected funnel(s) (Yes or No).
1099 Select filters for the import:
? Status
? Rating
? Frequencies
? Industries
? Locations
? Service Category
1100 Choose which funnel(s) you would like to add the customers.
1101 Click the blue Import button to sync the data.

Import History
Ģ Recurring scheduled imports can be seen under the Scheduled import tab.
Ģ Import history is listed below the Import and Sync options on the main Imports page.
Ģ Each import provides the Option to View lead(s) that were imported or Undo import to redo an import for any reason.



How to manage other forms and popups
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-manage-other-forms-and-popups

How to manage other forms and popups
Learn where to manage other system forms and popups in the Leads Module.

Last updated on 28 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ Where to manage the rest of your forms and pop-up settings

What are "other forms" and popups?
Simply put, "other forms" means any other forms that can be found in the system, including the main system forms like your booking form, pop-ups, hiring forms, campaign forms, and short forms.

Thanks to the new Leads Module, all of these system forms and pop-ups can now be connected to your lead funnels.

Other System Forms & Popups
Beyond the lead form and the action form, there are other forms and pop-ups found throughout the BookingKoala system. To manage how these forms and pop-ups connect to your campaign audiences, go to the Forms section and click on the Other System Forms and Pop-Ups tab.

There are 5 categories within this tab:

Ģ Main System Forms
? Booking Form
? Gift Cards page
? Earn Credits page
? Sign Up page
Ģ Pop-Ups
? Click here to learn how to add a pop-up to the BookingKoala website and theme builder.
Ģ Hiring Forms
? Click here to learn how to build a hiring form.
Ģ Campaign Forms
? Click here to learn how to build a campaign form.
Ģ Short Forms
? Coming soon!

Use the options under the Actions column to Enable/Disable tracking for each form/pop-up and the Settings option to control which lists leads are sorted into.

Under the Settings section, you can sort users into specific lists per this article.



Lead profiles
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/lead-profiles

Lead profiles
Learn how to locate and use the various tools on the leads details page.

Last updated on 26 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to access a lead's profile
Ģ How to combine leads
Ģ How to add a lead to funnels
Ģ How to send messages to a lead
Ģ How to send forms to a lead
Ģ How to edit or delete a lead
Ģ How to use the Work Progress section
Ģ Where to view lead-related Activities
Ģ How to view and add notes and comments to the Notes section
Ģ Where to upload, download, and view lead-related files
Ģ How to prepare, send, and sign Smart Documents

Lead Profiles
In the Leads Module, each lead has a profile page with numerous tools to help you stay in contact, keep records, send and sign documents, and more. To access a lead's profile, go to the Leads section or locate a lead in the Funnels section and click on a lead's name.

On the left side of the page, you will see the lead's basic information including their name and any tags associated with their account.

Below are several buttons to help with lead management.

1102 Combine - The combine feature allows you to combine up to three leads at a time into a single profile.
1103 Funnels - Add the lead to one or more funnels. Funnels can be managed under the Work Progress section.
1104 Messages - Send personal messages to your leads to nurture them.
1104.1 Send Email - Send an email to your lead.
1104.2 Send SMS - Send a text to your lead.
1105 Forms - Send forms directly to your lead.
1105.1 Email - Send forms via email.
1105.2 SMS - Send forms via text.
1106 Invoice - Create an invoice for your lead.
1107 Notes - Add notes to the lead's profile.
1108 Actions - Additional action options include editing and deleting the lead.
1108.1 Edit - Edit the lead's basic information.
1108.2 Delete - Delete the lead entirely.
1109 Create Customer - Convert the lead into a customer.
1110 Original Source - Set the original source for the lead.
1111 Lead Owner - Set a lead owner.
1112 Add Tag - Add new and existing tags to a lead to keep them organized.

Then, below, are the lead's email(s) and phone number(s).

Leads Dashboard
On the main dashboard of the lead, there are five main sections to help with lead management.

1113 Work Progress - Manage the lead's funnel(s) from this section.
1114 Activities - See logs for the lead, emails, and SMS. Some logs have options to download, resend, or view the communication sent.
1115 Notes - The Notes section can be used by the admin and staff to add notes to the lead's profile. Users can add new notes, add comments, and tag users in the Notes section.
1116 Files - The Files section allows you to upload, download, and view files related to the lead.
1117 Smart Documents - The Smart Documents feature allows you to upload documents to share, fill out, and sign. Signatures come complete with an audit system to track when each signee completed the document.



How to combine leads
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-combine-leads

How to combine leads
Learn how to combine up to three leads into one profile.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to combine leads

Combining Leads
Let's say you are going through your leads module and notice three new leads have come all with similar information! Perhaps you have multiple pop-ups and the same client filled all of them out. Whatever the case may be, it is important to have a way to stay organized in scenarios like this. This is where the Combine Leads feature comes in. The Combine Leads feature allows you to combine those profiles into one by picking which details are primary versus secondary.

To use this feature, begin by clicking on the names of one of the leads.

This will bring you to that lead's Details page. Here there are several different buttons below the lead's profile picture and name.

Click on the "Combine" button and a Combine Profile pop-up will appear. You will see an overview of the lead's information, including a phone number, email address, and more.

To select the next lead, use the Select New Lead search bar near the top right corner of the pop-up to search for the profile by name. Click to select the profile and you will see the second lead's information has been added to the pop-up below. You can now add a third lead if needed and then move on to the next step.

There is a bullet point next to each piece of information. Select the primary options for each piece of data and then click the Next button.

On the next page, you will finalize the combination by typing the word "CONFIRM" into the field at the bottom and then clicking the "Combine" button.

You will see a green system alert at the top right corner of the page confirming the profiles have been combined successfully.

You can now view the profile of the newly combined lead and see all of the information combined into a single profile under the Primary and Secondary sections.



How to send messages and forms to leads
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-send-messages-and-forms-to-leads

How to send messages and forms to leads
Learn how to use the Leads Module to send messages, including forms, to your leads.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to send emails and SMS to leads
Ģ How to send forms to leads

How to Send Messages To Leads
One of the great features included in the Leads Module is the ability to message leads via email or SMS. There are plenty of reasons why you may want to connect with your lead, like letting them know about special promotions and offers, answering questions about your business, or extending a personal thank you for their interest. Whatever the case may be, the process for sending messages is as simple as going to your lead's profile.

To begin, click on the lead's name to visit their details page / profile.

This will bring you to that lead's Details page. Here there are several different buttons below the lead's profile picture and name. Click on the "Messages" button.

From here, decide if you would like to send an email or SMS. Keep in mind that most leads are more likely to respond to a shorter message than a formal email.

Send Email

To send an email, click Messages and select "Send email". An email template pop-up will appear with the lead's primary email address. You can CC any other email address, add a Subject, and construct the Email body using the pop-up.

If you have created any canned responses under the Leads Module Notifications section, you can click use the Templates button to insert those into the email body.

You can also save and add a signature using the Signature button.

To further customize the email, shortcodes can be inserted via the Insert short codes button.

If you'd like to send a test email to see what your message will look like, click the white Send test email button at the bottom right corner of the popup. Enter an email address into the main field and then click Send.

When you are ready to send your email, click the blue Send button at the bottom right corner of the pop-up. You will see a green system alert confirming the email was sent successfully.

Send SMS

To send an email, click Messages and select "Send SMS". A Send SMS template pop-up will appear with the lead's primary phone number.

Besides the main Message field, there are several buttons to help you customize your SMS. The link button allows you to insert links. The Insert short codes button allows you to insert SMS-friendly shortcodes to personalize your email content.

The Send test SMS button allows you to send a test SMS of this message to any number you'd like.

The Choose Template button allows you to insert any SMS templates you created in the Lead Module Notifications section.

Once you are ready to send your text, click the blue Send button at the bottom right corner of the popup.

A green system alert will appear letting you know that the SMS has been sent successfully.

How to Send Forms to Leads
Just like messages, forms can also be sent via email or SMS. This can come in handy if you are using forms to collect more information about your leads, set up an estimate, etc. Let's say you are a cleaning business and you are running a pop-up ad for people interested in Spring Cleaning promotions. You could create an estimate form to gather more details and get the client's preference of date and time for scheduling. This makes sending forms all the more important for your business.

To send a form to a lead, first go to the lead's profile by clicking on their name.

Once on the lead's profile page, click on the Forms button beneath the lead's profile picture and name.

Next, select whether you'd like to send the form via email or SMS.

After this, select which form(s) will be sent to the lead. Then, click the blue Next button.

After this, you'll see a preview of the email or SMS you are about to send with the form(s). You can edit the template, set a subject, and review the email or text before you are ready to send it.

Once the message has been prepared, the form(s) can be sent by clicking the blue "Send" button. A green system alert will appear in the top right corner confirming that the forms have been sent successfully.



How to edit or delete a lead
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-edit-or-delete-a-lead

How to edit or delete a lead
Learn how to edit a lead's basic information and how to delete a lead altogether.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to edit a lead
Ģ How to delete a lead

Edit a Lead
If you imported some of your leads into the system, there is a chance you'll discover a mistake or two as you go over your leads sections. Or, perhaps a lead contacted you and wanted to update the information they have on file. Whatever the case may be, you can easily edit your leads' details by accessing the lead's profile page.

First, go to the Leads section in the Leads Module and locate the profile you'd like to edit. Click on the lead's name.

This will bring you to that lead's Details page. Here there are several different buttons below the lead's profile picture and name. Click on the "Actions" button.

Select "Edit" to launch the Edit Lead pop-up. Here you can edit the lead's name, email address, phone number, and tags.

When finished, click the blue "Update" button to save any changes. A green system alert will appear in the top right corner of the page confirming that the lead has been updated successfully.

Delete a Lead
In some instances, you may need to delete a lead. Perhaps a customer filled out the same form twice with duplicate information, or maybe a lead even requested to be removed from your system. Whatever the case may be, it is possible to permanently delete a lead by going to the lead's Details page.

First, go to the Leads section in the Leads Module and locate the profile you'd like to edit. Click on the lead's name.

This will bring you to that lead's Details page. Here there are several different buttons below the lead's profile picture and name. Click on the "Actions" button.

Select "Delete". A popup will appear, asking if you are sure you want to delete this lead. Only click "Delete" if you are certain you do not want to retrieve this information ever again.

Once you click delete, a green system alert will appear in the top right corner confirming that the lead has been deleted successfully.



How to use Smart Documents
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-use-smart-documents-2ae1cbd2

How to use Smart Documents
Learn how to use the Smart Documents features in the Leads Module to prepare, send, and sign documents.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ What are Smart Documents
Ģ How to prepare a smart document

What are Smart Documents?
Smart Documents are documents that you can upload, edit, send, and sign all through the Lead's module. Smart Documents allow you to capture signatures from up to 10 parties, all with an audit trail to help you see when and who signed what. You can use Smart Documents to:
Ģ Send service agreements to potential customers
Ģ Deliver the results of an onsight estimate
Ģ Gather feedback regarding the lead's experience with your business

In addition to signature gathering, Smart Documents allow you to insert fields into the document for a fully customizable document for each party.

How to Use Smart Documents
To use Smart Documents, go to any lead's profile/"Details" page to whom you'd like to send a document and click on the Smart Documents tab.

To begin working with a document, click the blue Upload Docs button, or drag and drop a file into the surrounding section. Once the file is finished uploading, you'll see a preview of the document.

From here there are several actions you can take. If you click the blue Share button, you have three options:

Ģ Share as Link - Share a link to the document via email. You can further customize the email prior to sending out.
Ģ Request Signatures - Add signature fields to any document and send the document out via email to request signatures.
1117.1 After you click Request signatures, a Request Signatures pop-up will appear. You can decide if you'd like to add an extra level of authenticity to your document by enabling the authentication code option, which sends an email with a security code to the signer that they must enter in order to sign the document. Authentification can be enabled or disabled per each individual signer on the document by using the green toggle buttons.
1117.2 If you'd like to add additional signers, scroll down past the existing signers and click on the Add Signer button. From here, select "As a Staff" or "As a Lead" to decide which type of user you are adding. Then, search for the signer and select their name to add them to the list. Once you have finished adding your signers, click the blue Next button.
1117.3 Next, you'll be placing your fields for each signer. Select the signer's name from the Select signer menu at the top of the left side of the page and then drag and drop one of the three possible signer fields into the document.
Ģ Signature - Collects a binding signature from the client.
Ģ Text Field - A blank text field that can be used to fill out names, dates, and other personal details that the signer is required to share.
Ģ Checkbox- Adds a checkable box to any sections requiring such.
 Once finished adding fields for each signer, you are ready to click the blueSend button. A green system alert will appear in the top right corner confirming the document has been sent successfully.
Ģ Download PDF - Download a PDF of the document.

Once you close the document preview window, you'll be able to see all documents you've uploaded, including those that have been sent out for signatures.

If you click on the "[#]/[#] completed" link, you can access the Document Signing pop-up.

This has great features including the ability to send reminders using the Send Reminder button, see an audit trail for signing, and download PDFs of the original PDF, the signed PDF (once available), and the audit trail.



How to sync leads
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-sync-leads

How to sync leads
Learn how to sync internal leads from your BookingKoala account.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

In this article, you will learn how to:
Ģ Sync leads from your BookingKoala account into the leads module

How to Sync Leads
To pull leads from your BookingKoala account, click on the Settings tab near the top right corner of the page and select Imports.

Next, click the blue Sync from BookingKoala button beneath where it says Sync.

From below the New Import tab, select one of the following options:
Ģ All booked customers - Sync customers who presently have a booking with your company.
Ģ Specific customers - Sync specific customers by name.
Ģ Not booked customers - Sync customers who currently do not have any bookings with your company.

Sync Specific Customers

If you are looking to sync specific customers, select the Specific customers option and then use the Select customers field to search and select customers by name, email address, or phone number.

Next, choose which funnel(s) you would like to add the customers.

Finally, click the blue Import button to sync the data.

You will see a green system alert letting you know that the import has begun successfully and to check the system logs in a little while to see when it will be complete.

You can go to the system logs section to check your import status at any time by going to: Leads > Settings > Logs > System Logs

Sync Not Booked or All Booked Customers: One-Time

If you are looking to sync all booked customers or not booked customers, you have two options: run a one-time import or set up a scheduled import that runs automatically at a set recurring rate. In this case, the one-time option is explored.

When running a one-time import, use the customer-created date field to select a date range for the import data.

Next, choose which funnel(s) you would like to add the customers.

Finally, click the blue Import button to sync the data.

As mentioned above, one can check the lead module system logs for the status of any new import. Leads > Settings > Logs > System Logs

Sync Not Booked or All Booked Customers: Scheduled Import

If you are looking to sync all booked customers or not booked customers, you have two options: run a one-time import or set up a scheduled import that runs automatically at a set recurring rate. In this case, the scheduled import option is explored.

To create a recurring scheduled import, select the Scheduled import option and add a name to the import like "Spring One-Time Customers", for example, then select an import start date and import start time. For Repeats, your options are:
Ģ Weekly on Monday
Ģ Every 2 weeks on Saturday
Ģ Monthly on the 15th of the month
Ģ Monthly on the 28th of the month
Ģ Use the Custom Schedule button to set your own repeating time frame.

Next, decide which week you would like to consider for the import (Previous or Next), how many days you want to consider (1-7 days), and if you allow duplicate work progresses in the selected funnel(s) (Yes or No).

Next, select your filters for the import. The options are:
Ģ Status
Ģ Rating
Ģ Frequencies
Ģ Industries
Ģ Locations
Ģ Service Category

Next, choose which funnel(s) you would like to add the customers.

Finally, click the blue Import button to sync the data.

Import History

Once submitted, you can see your recurring scheduled imports under the Scheduled import tab.

Your import history is listed below the Import and Sync options on the main Imports page.

Each import provides the Option to View lead(s) that were imported or Undo import if perhaps you would like to redo a import for any reason.



Set up your leads notifications
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/set-up-your-leads-notifications

Set up your leads notifications
Learn how to set up your email and integrate Twilio to send out SMS notifications from the leads module.

Last updated on 31 Dec, 2024

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to set up your leads module email notifications
Ģ How to set up your leads module SMS notifications

Leads Module Email Notifications
To set up your Leads Module email notifications, please go to: Leads > Settings > Notifications > Email tab

Change Your Send Out Email Address If you'd like to change the default send-out email address for the Leads Module to something different, you can do so by clicking the "Change" button to the right of the current default send-out address.

A pop-up will appear, giving you the option to use a previously confirmed send-out email address from the "Already saved" list or select the "Add new" option to set up a new email. Once you've selected or entered the email address into the pop-up, click the "Update" button.

A green system alert will appear at the top-right corner of the page, confirming you have updated your sender email address. It will also indicate if your email address is already confirmed on AWS (Amazon Web Service) or if a verification link was emailed to your inbox.

Go to your inbox and find the email from Support@bookingkoala.com. Inside the email, there will be a link that you must click.

Once you've clicked the link, you will be redirected to an AWS page that says "Congratulations!". Once you see this, your email is now verified and you can begin sending out email notifications.

Back in your BookingKoala dashboard, you can also set up a Reply to email address and set your Sender name. Just be sure to click the "Save Changes" button at the bottom of the page once you've made your edits.

Leads Module Master Templates Below the email setup section, you will find two default master templates. These can be edited to apply your branding and other formatting to the emails that come out of the Leads Module.

Click on "Options" to Edit, Delete, or Duplicate a master template, and "Apply to email" to apply it to the email templates listed below.

For more information on how master templates work, click here (https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/change-the-header-image-in-your-email-notification-templates).

Leads Module Email Templates There are three categories under the Email Templates section of the Leads Module: emails to the lead, emails to the admin, and canned responses.

Emails to the lead and the admin can easily be enabled or disabled depending on your preference by clicking the toggle button.

To edit a template, click the pencil icon/"Edit" button under the Action column. Shortcodes can be inserted into email and SMS templates to further customize them.
Ģ Click here (https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/short-codes) to learn more about shortcodes and how to use them.

As always, be sure to click the "Save" button at the bottom of the page if you make any changes.

Leads Module SMS Notifications
To set up your Leads Module SMS notifications, please go to: Leads > Settings > Notifications > SMS tab

There are three fields that must be completed to connect your Twilio account.
1118 Account ID
1119 API key
1120 Phone number

Open your Twilio dashboard and scroll down to the Account Info section.
Ģ If you do not have a Twilio account yet or need help generating your API keys, please click here (https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/set-up-twilio).

1121 Account ID - Use the "copy" icon at the far right end of the box to copy and paste your Twilio account ID (or "SID") into the first box in BookingKoala. Your account ID will always begin with "AC" and then a long string of numbers and letters.
You can also find your account ID by going to Admin > Account Management > API keys and tokens in Twilio.
1122 API key - Copy and paste the Auth Token into the box that says "API Key".
1123 Phone no. - Copy and paste your phone number into the third box.
Ģ Make sure your phone number is entered exactly as it is pictured under "My Twilio phone number". Enter "+" plus your country code followed by the rest of the phone number.
? For example, "+15672299756" is correct.
? "15672299756" is incorrect.
? "5672299756" is incorrect.
? "+1(567)2299756" is incorrect.
? "+1-567-229-9756" is incorrect.
You can also find your phone number by going to Phone Numbers > Manage > Active Numbers in the left side menu in your Twilio dashboard.

Once you have entered each piece of information into the boxes in BookingKoala, click the "Save changes" button at the bottom of the page.

A green system notification will appear in the top right corner of the page, confirming your Twilio account has been connected successfully. The system is now ready to send out any SMS templates you have enabled.

Leads Module SMS Templates Just like email templates, SMS templates can be toggled to enabled or disabled depending on your preference.

SMS templates can be edited using the pencil icon/"Edit" button under the Action column. There are also SMS shortcodes that can be used to further customize your lead and admin templates.



What are hot leads?
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/what-are-hot-leads

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ What are hot leads
Ģ How to track them in BookingKoala

What are hot leads?
Hot leads are customers who never completed the booking form and left before finishing. (Premium feature). Sometimes, potential visitors leave your website without finishing the booking, but if they leave some information that includes a phone number and/or email, we can capture that information. Later you can reach out to them and see why they didn't complete the booking.

How are hot leads collected?
A hot lead is someone who fills out your booking form but "abandons their cart" before they complete the booking. These abandoned cart customers are some of the hottest leads you can have because they are interested in your services and they have already partially filled out the booking form, giving you a better idea of what they are looking for.

A few minutes after leaving the booking page, hot leads are automatically sorted into a pre-built Abandoned Cart funnel inside your Leads Module. Here you can follow up with the lead and try to entice them to book a service.

These leads are also tracked when adding a booking as admin/staff through the 'Add Booking' button. Whenever taking bookings over the phone, leads will automatically end up inside the leads module so you can work on them later.

Whether the booking was completed and saved by admin/staff or the person left without finishing the booking, the information will be saved inside the leads module. You can later go inside the leads module and continue where you left off.

Tips for Converting Hot Leads Here are some of our best tips on converting hot leads into customers:

Tip #1: Call AS FAST AS POSSIBLE!
Ģ Studies indicate that contacting a lead within 5 minutes can result in a 21 times higher conversion rate compared to waiting 30 minutes.
Ģ 50% of leads tend to choose the first company that reaches out to them.

Tip #2: Follow up with your leads.
Ģ Around 80% of sales are made after five or more follow-up attempts.
Ģ 60% of customers may say "no" up to four times before accepting a sale.

Tip #3: Be helpful and do not oversell your company.

How It Works
HereÆs how it works:
Ģ When a user's subscription plan expires, access to hot leads tracked from system forms is restricted.
Ģ Instead of providing full access to these leads, we notify the user via email.
Ģ If you'd like to stop such notifications, you can snooze them by clicking the 'Snooze notification' towards the bottom of that email.
Ģ Upon visiting the Leads module Leads section, users will see blurred lead items. This indicates that leads have been tracked, but their details are not accessible. These blurred leads are referred to as "hot leads."
Ģ To unlock access to all the leads, including the hot leads, users need to upgrade their subscription plan to the Premium subscription.

You can easily upgrade your plan by clicking on the blue Upgrade Now buttons located throughout the lead details sections.



How to add leads from listing pages
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/how-to-add-leads-from-listing-pages

How to add leads from listing pages
Learn how to manually add leads from your listing pages in BookingKoala.

Last updated on 26 Mar, 2025

In this article, you will learn how to:
Ģ Manually add leads from your listing pages

Adding Leads from the Listing Pages
The new Leads Module allows for the addition of leads from the listing or "list view" pages in BookingKoala.

There are several places you can add a lead to your funnels. This includes:
Ģ Today's Bookings
Ģ Upcoming Bookings
Ģ Unassigned Bookings
Ģ Job History
Ģ Cancellations

In addition to that, you can add a lead to a funnel from the Edit Booking page.

To begin, click to edit a specific booking, select "Bookings" from the left-side menu and then select one of the listing pages.

If your default view is calendar view, you can switch to list view by clicking the List View button at the top of the page.

Once you are in list view, look under the Actions column on the right side of the page and locate the Add to Leads Funnel icon. Once you click the icon, a "Choose Funnel(s)" pop-up will appear.

If the lead has already been added to one or more funnels, the names of those funnels will be displayed at the top of the pop-up.

Click on the Select Funnels dropdown to add the lead to one or more funnels by checking the box next to the funnel name.

Once you've selected the funnel(s), there are two more questions for you to answer:

Ģ Do you want to sync revenue?
? You can copy over booking revenue from here or add your own later. When syncing the revenue over to the leads module, the system will use the revenue that is attached to this booking and add it to the work progress inside the leads module.
Ģ Do you want to sync extra revenue?
? Syncing extra revenue will add any cancellation or rescheduling fees, additional tips, or extra charges that belong to this booking. That extra revenue will show up under the work progress section for the lead.

Once you've made your selections, click the "Submit" button. The lead will be added to the selected funnels.



Best ways to use leads
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/best-ways-to-use-leads

Best ways to use leads
Learn how the automatic funnels work in the leads module and how to set up other useful funnels.

Last updated on 28 Mar, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How automatic funnels work in the leads module
Ģ How to set up other helpful funnels

Getting Started
Here are the best ways to use the leads module when collecting leads. These are examples of what you can do with them to make more money and run a better service.

Keep in mind that these are just some of the examples we know of. If you have any other examples youÆd like to share with us, we can add them to our article.

To begin, open the Leads Module by clicking on "Leads" from the left side menu.

To view your current funnels and add new ones, click the "Funnels" button at the top of the page.

To learn how to add a new funnel, click here.

To pull leads from your BookingKoala account into a funnel, click on the Settings tab near the top right corner of the page and select Imports.

Next, click the blue Sync from BookingKoala button beneath where it says Sync.

To learn how to sync leads to your funnels or edit the existing funnel criteria, click here.

Preset Funnels
Six funnels have already been set up within your leads module. They are:
Ģ Abandoned Cart (HOT Leads)
Ģ Canceled Bookings
Ģ New Reviews and Feedback
Ģ One-Time to Recurring
Ģ Negative Reviews
Ģ Lead Form

Abandoned Cart (HOT Leads) Funnel The Abandoned Cart (HOT Leads) funnel is set up to collect all leads that come through the booking form on the customer end and admin end. Once they are in the funnel, see if they book, mark them, or have them automatically marked as "Won". Then work with the ones that have not finished a booking. So, if you are doing a booking as an admin and it's not finished, it will automatically end up here. You can call the lead a couple of times to see if they want to complete the booking.

New Reviews and Feedback Funnel Next, we have the New Reviews and Feedback funnel. Automatically, the system is going to be checking the bookings that occurred yesterday and it will add them in according to the import leads criteria. You can follow up with leads in that funnel and try to get reviews from them. To learn a little more about how that works, we can go to the recurring sync for this funnel.

This funnel is based on a scheduled import that comes and checks the previous day every day at 4 AM and pulls bookings according to the booking status. In this case, bookings must be in the job history and any customers who have completed up to 2 bookings total in their job history.

So if this is their first or second booking ever, theyÆll be added to the funnel. however, if this is their third booking, they will not be imported as we can assume they already left a review or are not interested, and we want to cut out time. There are several different ways of setting this up, but this is the way we felt would be most successful.

If someone does end up in here, they are likely a new customer with one or two bookings, itÆs unlikely theyÆve left a review. So you can start calling them if you check the customer and do not see any reviews left thus far by clicking the View Customer button under the leadÆs profile.

One-Time to Recurring Funnel For the One-Time to Recurring funnel, the system checks for any one-time appointments from yesterday and imports them into this funnel. This is achieved by a recurring sync of all one-time bookings that occurred the day before.

If you run a recurring type of business like home cleaning, this is beneficial because you can call the one-time clients and try to convince them to become a recurring client.

Canceled Bookings Funnel For the Canceled Bookings funnel, can see the criteria for this under the imports section. The filter is for any canceled bookings from yesterday will end up in this funnel.

This will be for either one-time or recurring cancelations.

Negative Reviews Funnel All negative internal reviews (ratings that are 3 stars or lower) will go inside this funnel.

You can contact customers who had a bad experience and try to fix the situation.

Lead Form Funnel We created a default Lead Form funnel for you. All leads from the default form will appear here so you can work on them conveniently.

To view and edit your default lead form, click on Forms from the top menu, then select Options under the Actions column and select Edit.

Ģ For more information on how to build a leads form, please click here.

Other Funnel Ideas
Customers Who HavenÆt Booked Since Funnel You can into the import sections or set up a quarterly import and set up a filter so customers who havenÆt booked since a certain date range will be pulled into the funnel.

Declined Payments Funnel You can check this every day as well. Any declined payments go into this funnel. You can call the customers to try and collect payment.

Quotes Funnel If you have any quotes that have been sent out, you can make a quote funnel and then follow up with those.



How to add a leads form to your website

How to add a leads form to your website
Learn how to embed a leads form onto your website.

Last updated on 07 Feb, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you understand:
Ģ How to embed your lead form(s) on your website

Getting Started
To locate your lead form, click on Leads from the left side menu to open the Leads Module.

Next, click on "Forms" from the top menu.

This is where all your lead forms are stored and new forms can be created.
Ģ To learn how to build a new lead form, click here.

A default lead form has been generated for you and is connected to the "Lead Form" funnel under the funnels section.

From here you can further modify the form and/or funnel to your liking.
Ģ To learn how to build or edit a funnel, click here.

Embedding Your Form(s)
Once you've gotten your lead form(s) to your liking, it is time to put them to use. To add a lead form to your website, you will copy an embed code and paste it into a custom CSS section in your website builder.

To get the embed code, go to the Forms section, select Options from under the Actions column, and then select Embed Code.

A pop-up window will appear with the embed code in it. Click the Copy Code button at the bottom of the window to copy the entire code.

Adding a Lead Form to a Third-Party Website

Once your embed code has been copied, navigate to your third-party website builder and follow the instructions in the links below to embed the form. Here are instructions for some of the most common website builders our clients use:
Ģ WordPress
Ģ Wix
Ģ Squarespace
Ģ GoDaddy
Ģ Weebly
Ģ Shopify

If you do not see your website builder listed here, please email us at support@bookingkoala.com and let us know so we can update our article and provide assistance with embedding your forms. If you permit us, we are also happy to embed your forms on your behalf. We just need access to your website builder and must know where you want each form embedded.

Adding a Lead Form to BookingKoala

If you are using the BookingKoala website builder for your website, there are a few different ways you can add a lead form to your site. All options take place inside the BookingKoala website and theme builder.

To begin, open your admin dashboard and go to: Settings > Design Forms & Website > Website Builder & Themes

Click on "Customize Theme" to open the builder.

Next, navigate to the "Get Quote" page, created to display your primary lead form.

Scroll down the page and click in the space below until you locate a long rectangle shape. Click to reveal the Edit button in the top left corner, then click Edit to select which lead form you would like to use.

After making a selection, that form will be imported into the space.

You can also add your lead form to any other page by adding a new section and then selecting "Lead" from the left-side menu. All of the forms you've created will be displayed. Click on a form to add it to the section.

You can also utilize the "Custom" option from the left-side menu which allows you to paste the embed code for the lead form into a field for custom CSS.

Once you've finished making any changes, just be sure to click the Save & Publish button in the top right corner of the page.



Setting up Google ad reports in the leads module

Setting up Google ad reports in the leads module

Last updated on 26 Mar, 2025

Ģ Integrating Google ads with your leads module
Ģ Accessing and analyzing Google ad reports effortlessly
Ģ Improve your marketing strategies with detailed insights

Overview
Integrating Google ads with your leads module enables powerful tracking and reporting capabilities. You can monitor lead performance, optimize campaigns, and make data-driven decisions effortlessly by linking your Google ad account.

Prerequisites

Before proceeding with the integration, ensure you have:
1124 A Google account.
1125 Access to Google Ad Manager to manage your reports and data

How to Integrate Google Reports
Step 1: Access the Leads Module 2. From the sidebar menu, go to the Leads .

Step 2: Navigate to Integrations
1126 Once the Leads module opens, locate the Settings option in the header navigation.
1127 Hover over the Settings menu and search for Integrations .
1128 Click on Integrations , and the integration settings page will open.

Step 3: Connect Google Account
1129 Click on the Login with Google button.
1130 A small pop-up window will appear.
1131 Enter your Google email and password.
1132 After filling in the credentials, another pop-up window will appear, asking permission to continue.
1133 Click Continue to grant access.

Step 4: Confirm Integration
1134 Once the integration is successful, you will be redirected to the integration settings page.
1135 Now, please select the AdWords MCC account ID and the AdWords client account ID , then save.
1136 You can now access Google reports by navigating to Reports and then go to Google Ad.
1137 The Google section in the integration page will be updated to reflect the successful connection.

Managing the Google Integration
Deactivate Integration : You can disable Google integration at any time by clicking the Deactivate button on the Integration Settings page .

By following these steps, you can efficiently set up Google Ad Reports in your leads module and make the most of your marketing analytics.



Setting up Facebook Lead Tracking and Ad Reports in the Leads Module
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-facebook-ad-reports-in-the-leads-module

Setting up Facebook Lead Tracking and Ad Reports in the Leads Module
Learn how to integrate Facebook with your Leads Module to track leads and generate insightful reports. With this integration, youÆll never miss an opportunity and can enhance your marketing campaigns with comprehensive analytics.

Last updated on 17 Oct, 2025

This guide will help you:
Ģ Connect Facebook with your Leads Module
Ģ Track leads directly from Facebook Lead Ad forms
Ģ Access and analyze Facebook ad reports with ease
Ģ Improve your marketing strategies through detailed insights

Overview
Integrating Facebook Ads with your leads module unlocks advanced lead tracking and reporting features. By connecting your Facebook ad account, you can easily track lead performance, optimize your campaigns, and make informed, data-driven decisions. With this connection, Booking Koala enables you to:
Ģ Capture leads instantly in your Leads Module
Ģ View campaign performance and key ad metrics
Ģ Make informed marketing decisions without switching tools

Prerequisites Before proceeding with the integration, ensure you have:
1138 A Facebook account.
1139 Admin access to the Facebook Page(s) you want to track leads from
1140 Access to Facebook Ad Manager to manage your reports and data. The admin must have at least one active ad account connected to the business profile.

How to Integrate Facebook Lead tracking and Ad Reports
Step 1: Access the Leads Module 2. From the sidebar menu, go to the Leads module.

Step 2: Navigate to Integrations
1141 Once the Leads module opens, locate the Settings option in the header navigation.
1142 Hover over the Settings menu and search for Integrations.
1143 Click on Integrations, and the integration settings page will open.

Step 3: Connect Facebook Account
1144 Click on the Login with Facebook button.
1145 If you are not already logged into Facebook, a popup will appear asking you to log in.
1146 After successfully logging in, you will be redirected to the following popup window. This popup will need your confirmation like your name.
1147 The next popup window will prompt you to "Choose the pages you want BookingKoala Leads to access."
1148 Select the businesses you want Booking Koala to access for lead tracking and ad insights
1149 The next popup window will request your access permissions as outlined below.
1150 A final confirmation popup will confirm that [Your Account Name] has been connected to BookingKoala Leads.

Navigate to the Leads Integrationsection in the leads module. You will see that the field is now enabled to get started.
Select theAd Accountfor which you want to see Facebook reports in the reports section.
Lead tracking has been automatically enabled for the Facebook pages selected during login. You can enable/disable it any time you want.
You have now successfully integrated Facebook with BookingKoala Leads.

How things work
Ģ The Leads Module starts receiving leads from Facebook Lead Ads instantly, which appear in the Leads Listing page.
Ģ Leads will only be captured from the Facebook Pages you granted access to during login.
Ģ You must be an admin of the selected Facebook Pages to track leads.
Ģ To view reports, select the Ad Account in the integration settings, then go to the Reports page and switch to the Facebook tab.

Managing the Facebook Integration
Ģ Reports ¢ You can change the connected Facebook Ad Account in the integration settings anytime.
Ģ Leads Tracking ¢ You can enable or disable lead tracking from the integration settings anytime.
Ģ Page Access: To update which Pages are connected, deactivate the integration and log in again.

Troubleshooting (Fallback mechanisms)
Ģ If leads are not being tracked, confirm that your integration is active and lead tracking is enabled.
Ģ Please note that, only admins of the connected Facebook Pages can track leads.
Ģ If you see dummy data in reports, ensure youÆve selected an Ad Account in the integration settings.
Ģ If issues persist, try Deactivate and re-logging into Facebook.

Deactivating the Integration
You can disable this Facebook integration at any time by clicking the Deactivate button on the integration settings page. This revokes permissions and stops Lead capture and reporting.

By following these steps, you can efficiently set up Facebook lead tracking and Ad reports in your Leads module and make the most of your marketing analytics.



What are abandoned form leads?
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/what-are-abandoned-form-leads

What are abandoned form leads?
Learn more about abandoned form leads in the leads module.

Last updated on 30 Apr, 2025

In this article, you will learn more about:
Ģ How abandoned form leads work
Ģ Where to locate them in the Leads Module

Abandoned Form Leads
Abandoned form leads are customers who never completed the lead form and left before finishing. (Premium feature). Sometimes, potential visitors leave your website without finishing, but if they leave some information that includes a phone number and/or email, we can capture that information.

These premium leads have an "Abandoned Form" tag associated with them.

How are abandoned form leads collected?
An abandoned form lead is someone who fills out your lead form but "abandons the form" before they complete the form. These are some of the hottest leads you can have because they are interested in your services and they may have already partially filled out the lead form, giving you a better idea of what they are looking for. Even thought the prospect did not complete the form, we capture whatever information that they entered before leaving the form.

A few moments after leaving the lead form page, abandoned form leads are automatically sorted into your funnel(s) inside your Leads Module. Here, you can follow up with the lead and try to entice them to book a service, schedule an interview, or something else.

How do I view an abandoned form leads?
You can view new abandoned form leads from the Leads section in your Leads Module dashboard.

Click on the lead's name to view that lead's detail page. Here, you can click the eye icon under the Work Progress section to view the form that the lead abandoned.



Setting up Zapier in the leads module
URL: https://help.bookingkoala.com/help/setting-up-zapier-in-the-leads-module

Setting up Zapier in the leads module
Learn how to integrate Zapier with your Leads module to automate lead creation and enhance your workflow efficiency with seamless data transfer.

Last updated on 18 Feb, 2025

The purpose of this article is to help you learn more about:

Ģ Integrate Zapier with your Leads module
Ģ Automate lead creation using Zapier actions
Ģ Streamline your lead management process with minimal manual effort

Overview
Integrating Zapier with your Leads module enables automated lead creation from various apps and services. Setting up a Zap lets you connect your preferred tools to automatically send lead data to your system, reducing manual work and improving response times.

Prerequisites

Before proceeding with the integration, ensure you have:

Ģ A Zapier account
Ģ A BookingKoala account with admin access.

How to Integrate Zapier with Leads Module
Create a Zap for Lead Creation

1151 Click Make a Zap in Zapier.
1152 Choose a trigger app (e.g., Facebook Leads, Google Forms, etc.).
1153 Select the trigger event (e.g., ōNew Leadö).
1154 Click Continue and authenticate your trigger app.
1155 Search for BookingKoala as the action app.
1156 Choose Create Lead as the action event.
1157 Connect your BookingKoala account to Zapier.
1157.1 To connect your Zapier account to BookingKoala, go to: Settings > General > Apps & Integrations
1157.2 Scroll down to the Zapier section and click the" Click Here " button.
1157.3 Next, click on the red " Disabled " button to switch it to " Enabled ". You will see a green system notification in the top right corner letting you know that the Zapier app has been enabled successfully.
1157.4 Next, click the blue " Generate API Key " button. This will create a unique key for you to connect your BookingKoala account with Zapier.
1157.5 Once the API key has been generated, use the blue Copy button to copy the code.
1157.5.1 Enter the API key you copied from BookingKoala into the first box.
1157.5.2 Enter your account's subdomain into the second box.
1158 Map the required lead fields from the trigger app to BookingKoala.
1159 Click Test & Continue to ensure the integration works correctly.
1160 Click Publish Zap to activate the automation.

Managing Your Zapier Integration

Ģ View Active Zaps : Manage and monitor your active Zaps from your Zapier dashboard.
Ģ Deactivate Zaps : To pause or stop a Zap, toggle it off or delete it within Zapier.

Details Synced from Zapier
The following tables contain each detail that is synced from Zapier. The column on the right shows the detail's name and the column on the left shows an example.

Create Lead

By following these steps, you can seamlessly integrate Zapier with BookingKoala to automate lead creation, enhancing your operational efficiency and ensuring prompt engagement with new prospects.
